gmw_00000745.name: NGC 1300
gmw_00000745.overview: NGC 1300 is a grand-design barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1009.0376, arXiv:1311.1530] characterized by a strongly skewed bar [arXiv:1304.3529] and a highly asymmetric spiral structure [arXiv:1002.1259]. The galaxy hosts a nuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:0710.4036, arXiv:2010.11815] and exhibits tentative evidence of hosting a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0503693].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1300 as a strongly barred disk galaxy with no nuclear activity, a smooth continuum, and a low-SNR but clearly apparent nuclear ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 1300 The SBbc spiral NGC 1300 is the prototype of a strongly barred disk galaxy, and has been extensively used as a testbed for dynamical modelling lin96,lin97,agu00 . The dust structure in the central kpc as revealed by the V-H color map of does not resemble a ring, but rather is a good example of a grand design spiral. NGC 1300 shows no signs of nuclear activity, and find no evidence for a distinct stellar nucleus. Nevertheless, from an HST/STIS study of the kinematics of the nuclear emission line gas, conclude that NGC 1300 hosts a SMBH of 6.6 10^6 .

Continuum emission As in the case of NGC 1079, the continuum morphology of NGC 1300 is very smooth, which has also been noted by . There is no signature of the ring evident, indicating that in terms of stellar mass, the star formation ring is a small perturbation to the overall potential. Emission lines Of all galaxies in our sample, NGC 1300 is the one with the least integration time, and the SNR of the line maps, and in particular the velocity fields is therefore rather low. Nevertheless, the ring structure is clearly apparent, especially in \ and .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1300 as an ansae-bar model based on a K-band potential, finding chaotic spiral-supporting orbits and a very asymmetric spiral structure with a broken right arm.

quotes.quote: The potential we used in this case is from an estimation of the potential of NGC 1300, from a K band image of this galaxy (Kalapotharakos et al. 2008). For the figures we present below we have used as inclination and position angles for the bar 42 2 and 100 6 respectively. The K band image of the galaxy deprojected with these values is given in Fig. n1300bar . It is interesting that in these models we find qualitatively different effective potentials for nearby values of _ p .
Again here we consider the orbits that support the spirals found. They are chaotic and their typical morphologies can be seen in Fig. 13orbits .
Especially in NGC 1300, the spiral structure is very asymmetric. The right arm, as depicted in Fig. n1300bar , practically breaks in two parts.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of NGC 1300, identifying GMCs across its bar, arm, and bar-end regions and arguing GMC property differences do not explain SFE variations.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the ^ 12 CO( 1-0 ) observations toward the western bar, arm and bar-end regions of the strongly barred galaxy NGC 1300 with ALMA 12-m array at a high angular resolution of 40 pc. We detected GMCs associated with the dark lanes not only in the arm and bar-end regions but also in the bar region, where massive star formation is not seen. Using the CPROPS algorithm, we identified and characterized 233 GMCs across the observed regions. Based on the Kolmogorov-Smirnov test, we find that there is virtually no significant variations in GMC properties (e.g., radius, velocity dispersion, molecular gas mass, and virial parameter) among the bar, arm and bar-end region.

In this study, therefore, we focus on a nearby prototype strongly barred galaxy of NGC 1300 (Fig. fig:NGC1300_Ha_continuum ). Maeda:2018bg carried out ^ 12 CO( 1-0 ) observations toward the bar and arm region of NGC 1300 with a single dish telescope of Nobeyama 45-m telescope. They find that the _ H_2 in the bar region is comparable to that in the arm region ( _ H_2 10 M_ pc^ 2 ), indicating the star formation activity in the bar region is clearly suppressed. In this paper, we report ^ 12 CO( 1-0 ) observations toward the western bar, arm, and bar-end regions in NGC 1300 at a high angular resolution of 40 pc with Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes response models for NGC 1300, arguing its bar and spiral morphology are best reproduced by combined thick-disk and 2D components with different orbital support.

quotes.quote: We present the orbital analysis of four response models, that succeed in reproducing morphological features of NGC 1300. Two of them assume a planar (2D) geometry with _p =22 and 16 respectively. The two others assume a cylindrical (thick) disc and rotate with the same pattern speeds as the 2D models. These response models reproduce most successfully main morphological features of NGC 1300 among a large number of models, as became evident in a previous study.

Comparing our response models among themselves we find that the NGC 1300 morphology is best described by a thick disc model for the bar region and a 2D disc model for the spirals, with both components rotating with the same pattern speed _p =16 . In such a case, the whole structure is included inside the corotation of the system. The bar is supported mainly by regular orbits, while the spirals are supported by chaotic orbits.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1300 as the barred-galaxy target for its model: its about 10 kpc bar motivates the standard parameters, and simulations reproduce the observed twin spiral arms.

quotes.quote: Moreover in Section rs we shall try to replicate the the spiral structure of the SBb galaxy NGC 1300, which is one of the best known barred galaxies. According to BT08 (plate 10) the semi-major axis of the bar of NGC 1300 is about 10 kpc and this fact justifies our choice.

In Section galmod we explained that the fiducial value of the semi-major axis (a = 10) was chosen having in mind the barred galaxy NGC 1300. Following a similar procedure, as the one described earlier in Fig. ssg , we conducted numerous numerical simulations, in a wide range of values of energy, in order to see whether or not our model can describe the spiral structure of this barred galaxy. Finally, for E = -3225 we obtained a sufficient match between the theoretical and the observational data. In Fig. fit we have superposed the numerical simulation output at t = 420 Myr above the real image of the galaxy. One may observe that the twin spiral arms obtained from the numerical simulation almost coincide with the corresponding real ones.

sources.summary: The paper measures CO(2--1)/CO(1--0) on 100 pc scales in barred spiral NGC 1300, finding environmental R21 variation, enhanced ratios near H emission, and diffuse molecular gas effects.

quotes.quote: We thus measured the R_ 21 in the strongly barred galaxy NGC 1300, where star formation activity strongly depends on galactic structure, on 100 pc scale. CO images were obtained from ALMA and Nobeyama 45-m telescope. The resultant typical R_ 21 in NGC 1300 is 0.57 0.06 .

We find environmental variations in R_ 21 ; it is the highest in the bar-end region ( 0.72 0.08 ), followed by arm ( 0.60 0.07 ) and bar regions ( 0.50 0.06 ). GMCs with H emission show a systematically higher ratio ( 0.67 0.07 ) than those without H ( 0.47 0.05 ). In the bar region, where massive star formation is suppressed, H emission is not associated with most GMCs, resulting in the lowest R_ 21 . These results raise a possibility that properties of GMCs derived from CO(2--1) observations with the assumption of a constant R_ 21 are different from those derived from CO(1--0) observations. Furthermore, we find the R_ 21 measured on kpc scale tends to be lower than that of the GMCs probably due to the presence of an extended diffuse molecular gas in NGC 1300.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1300 as a barred spiral, testing potentials and pattern speeds against its observed morphology to reproduce bar, spiral, and disk features.

quotes.quote: We study the stellar response in a spectrum of potentials describing the barred spiral galaxy NGC 1300. These potentials have been presented in a previous paper and correspond to three different assumptions as regards the geometry of the galaxy. For each potential we consider a wide range of _p pattern speed values. Our goal is to discover the geometries and the _p supporting specific morphological features of NGC 1300.

In order to compare the images of NGC 1300 with the density maps of our models, we define a new index which is a generalization of the Hausdorff distance. This index helps us to find out quantitatively which cases reproduce specific features of NGC 1300 in an objective way.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1300 as a strongly barred galaxy with evidence for a 400 pc nuclear ring, intermediate-age ring stars, low young-star fractions, and lower disc metallicity.

quotes.quote: The four selected galaxies were chosen to have a wide range of circumnuclear properties (e.g. the presence and size of a nuclear ring and the level of star formation). \ NGC 1300 is an SB(rs)bc galaxy without nuclear activity 2004AJ....128.1124S . It is an archetypal example of a strongly barred galaxy and has been used in many studies related to the dynamical modelling of galaxies e.g. 1996A&A...313..733L, 1997A&A...317...36L, 2000A&A...361..841A . While the presence of a nuclear ring is not evident given the smooth appearance of the stellar light in the inner regions e.g. 1989ApJS...71..433P, 2000MNRAS.317..234P , 2008AJ....135..479B found evidence of a nuclear ring of 400 pc radius 2010MNRAS.402.2462C traced by hot spots of Br and [ Fe ii ] emission lines. These lines are related to the photo-ionised gas around OB stars and supernova remnants respectively, thus tracing the regions where star formation activity may be taking place.

On the other hand, the ring of NGC 1300 seems to be dominated by an intermediate-age stellar population, with a mean age of 4.6 Gyr. The ages in all these rings correspond reasonably well with the ellipses we used to define them (see Sect. sec:methods for details). The other two regions defined in these galaxies display different behaviours. NGC 1097 has a disc with a stellar population of intermediate age ( 6 Gyr) and a central region dominated by older stars (with mean age over 8 Gyr). In NGC 1300 the disc has an average age of 8.3 Gyr, while the region inside the ring has a slightly younger mean age of 5.9 Gyr with a smaller dispersion ( 0.7 Gyr for the central region and 1.6 Gyr for the disc).

sources.summary: The paper studies cloud-cloud collision speeds in barred spiral NGC 1300, finding faster collisions in the bar and bar end and linking bar star-formation suppression to diffuse gas and high-speed collisions.

quotes.quote: Focusing on the strongly barred galaxy NGC 1300, we investigate the CCC speed. We find the CCC speed in the bar and bar-end tend to be higher than that in the arm. The estimated CCC speed is 20 km s^ 1 , 16 km s^ 1 , and 11 km s^ 1 in the bar, bar-end, and arm, respectively. Although the star formation activity is different in the bar and bar-end, the CCC speed and the number density of high-speed CCC with > 20 km s^ 1 are high in both regions, implying the existence of other parameters that control the star formation.

The difference in molecular gas mass (average density) of the giant molecular clouds (GMCs) between the bar (lower mass and lower density) and bar-end (higher mass and higher density) may be cause for the different star formation activity. Combining with our previous study (Maeda et al.), the leading candidates of causes for the star formation suppression in the bar in NGC 1300 are the presence of a large amount of diffuse molecular gases and high-speed CCCs between low mass GMCs.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1300 as a strong-bar R1' comparison object whose strong spiral structure and long arms require a later simulation time for a morphology match.

quotes.quote: Comparison of particle simulations to galaxies exhibiting similar spiral-like structure Figure b2picsb.eps shows four galaxies, with strong R1' type pseudorings; NGCs 4548, 7552, 1300, and 4134. To best match their pseudoring morphology, we chose simulations with _ b,0 =0.8 and a bar that grows in three bar periods. Each of the galaxy images is compared with the density distribution at a time during or just following bar growth. NGC 4548 and NGC 7552 have the weakest strengths Q_T = 0.34 and 0.4 respectively. NGC 4314 and NGC 1300 have stronger bars with Q_T = 0.44 and 0.54 respectively.

Thus we chose a relatively early time (1.5 bar periods since the beginning of the simulation) for the simulation matching NGC 4548 which has weaker spiral structure and later times for the other galaxies ( t=2P_ b,0 , 2.5 P_ b,0 and 4 P_ b,0 for NGC 7552, NGC 1300 and NGC 4314, respectively). NGC 1300 has the strongest spiral structure but also has the strongest bar. A later time in the simulation is required to match the NGC 1300's longer spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1300 as a grand-design barred spiral, deriving a near-infrared gravitational potential to study its bar, spiral morphology, and stellar dynamics.

quotes.quote: In a series of papers we study the stellar dynamics of the grand design barred-spiral galaxy NGC 1300. In the first paper of this series we estimate the gravitational potential and we give it in a form suitable to be used in dynamical studies. The estimation is done directly from near-infrared observations.

The motivation of this study is to investigate the dynamics of NGC 1300, which is a well known barred-spiral grand design galaxy with a prominent bar. Its archetypical morphology promises a deeper understanding of the dynamics of a class of similar objects.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1300 as a strong barred-galaxy outlier in scaling relations, connecting its anomalous position to possible bar-altered kinematics or unusual black-hole and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: Except for NGC 4395, it is apparent from inspection of Figure fig:v-sigma that five galaxies (Circinus, NGC 613, NGC 1300, NGC 4151, and NGC 5055) are mild outliers, noticeably outside the 1 ( 1 _ rms ) scatter band. Of these five galaxies, two galaxies (NGC 1300 and NGC 5055) are >2 outliers. Davis:2018 note that NGC 1300 is an outlier in the M_ BH - M_ *,tot diagram for spiral galaxies, with either an overmassive black hole or a low total stellar mass, which is matched here by either a low v_ max or a high _0 .

For example, NGC 613 and NGC 1300 (both classical barred galaxies) are prominent outliers in Figure fig:v-sigma , suggesting that their bars might have significantly altered the kinematics of these galaxies. Graham:2011 demonstrate that the scatter in the M_ BH - _0 diagram can be mitigated by fitting an offset relation for barred galaxies. For pseudobulges built from bars, the ``bulge'' is very flattened, and the velocity dispersion is anisotropic, much larger in the horizontal direction than in the vertical direction. Bellovary:2014 specifically treat this anisotropic issue, and show that increased inclination not only produces an elevated scatter at the low-mass end of the M_ BH - _0 relation, but also introduces a bias, to increase globally both the average and the median value above the true _0 , which tends to show an offset M_ BH - _0 relation for disky galaxies. This bias applies not just to barred galaxies but all to spiral galaxies because all spiral galaxies will likely possess a bar at some point in their life. Sheth:2008 describe the development of a bar as an indicator of maturity, although bars may be recurrent and therefore not a sign of either maturity or immaturity. In consideration of the effects of inclination, we have investigated _0 vs. \ (i) and found no correlation. We do, however, note that some galaxies have uncharacteristically low _0 values. For instance, NGC 253 (the Sculptor galaxy) has _0=(96 18) km s^ 1 , but it is a bulgy, massive, and inclined ( i=75 3 ) galaxy. Perhaps some explanation for this galaxy can be obtained by considering that NGC 253 is a prototypical starburst galaxy Watson:1996,Kornei:2009,Leroy:2018,Mangum:2019,Martin:2019,Takano:2019 . As a result, _0 may depend on the age of the stars: when there is gas, young stars can form from the gas, strongly weight the optical spectrum, and ``cool'' the stellar component. This is contrary to a collision-less simulation, where the stellar component only heats up. This process in the disk can contribute to the measurement of _0 , leading to a small _0 drop at the center of a galaxy and could explain why _0 is low for NGC 253 and other star-forming galaxies. Indeed, other prominent starburst galaxies display this behavior. The v_ max / _0 ratios of NGC 253 and three other prominent star-forming galaxies, IC 342, NGC 2146, and NGC 6946 see and references therein Gorski:2018 , are all elevated with v_ max / _0=2.04 0.38 , 3.11 0.49 , 2.33 0.16 , and 5.66 0.98 (all from HyperLeda), respectively. Moreover, the bar in NGC 253 is not end-on but inclined (if not completely side-on), reducing its effect on _0 . The widespread presence of low- to medium-luminosity, active galactic nuclei (AGNs) in spiral galaxies e.g., Beifiori:2009,Jiang:2011b,Xiao:2011 indicates that the efficient growth of SMBHs through the accretion of gas is prevalent. It is evident that secular processes in disk galaxies play a key role in the development of SMBHs Cisternas:2011,Schawinski:2011,Schawinski:2012,Salvo:2012,Kocevski:2012,Simmons:2012,Treister:2012,Debattista:2013,Zubovas:2019 . Nuclear bars have been theorized to drive gas inflows into the center, feeding black holes e.g., Shlosman:1989,Shlosman:1990,Hopkins:2010 . see also Valluri:2016 Du:2017 considered the effect of an SMBH on a nuclear bar, showing that nuclear bars are destroyed at lower SMBH masses than the large-scale bars in their simulations. Specifically, they found that embedded bars are quickly dissolved when M_ BH 0.2 M_ *,tot , halting the inner-bar-driven gas-feeding mechanism. For comparison, we find that for the subsample of 35 pseudobulge spiral galaxies in Davis:2018 , the median ratio is is approximately an order of magnitude lower, M_ BH =(0.016 0.011 ) M_ *,tot . The central velocity dispersion is enhanced by the presence of a bar, ultimately leading to slightly higher values of _0 in barred galaxies than in unbarred galaxies. Hartmann:2014 conducted simulations, showing that bar growth heats the disk, forms the bulge, and concentrates the mass, The bar strength is not necessarily a clear indicator of mass concentration, as a galaxy could have already passed its maturity, concentrated its mass, and its once strong bar could now appear weakened or have disappeared. therefore the mass evolution increases the central velocity dispersion, The simulations of Hartmann:2014 show that the formation of a bar yields an elevated _0 even when viewing the disk face-on. This suggests a connection with out-of-plane buckling that leads to peanut shell-shaped structures Burbidge:1959,Vaucouleurs:1974,Illingworth:1983,Jarvis:1986,Combes:1990,Bureau:1999,Lutticke:2000,Lutticke:2000b,Patsis:2002,Buta:2007,Athanassoula:2016,Ciambur:2016 that are more readily visible in edge-on spiral galaxies, but are also recognizable in more face-on systems Laurikainen:2011,Laurikainen:2013,Laurikainen:2014,Laurikainen:2015,Laurikainen:2018,Erwin:2013,Athanassoula:2015,Buta:2015,Laurikainen:2016,Laurikainen:2017,Herrera-Endoqui:2017,Salo:2017,Salo:2017b,Saha:2018 . The bar or peanut shell shape may also increase the vertical dispersion of the disk, in addition to the bar, but this is a small effect considering the great streaming in the disk. Therefore, if Hartmann:2014 see an effect for i=0 , it is more likely due to the concentration of the mass in the center and the global heating than due to the peanut shell shape. Moreover, when gas is present, there is a diminished box or peanut shell-shape effect; the presence of a large gas fraction tends to weaken or destroy the peanut shell shape. which causes barred galaxies to move to higher velocity dispersions in the M_ BH - _0 diagram. However, their simulations did not include gas see Seo:2019 , which meant that gas was not present to possibly form new stars (cooling the stellar component and decreasing _0 ) or fuel SMBHs in their galaxies (increase M_ BH ). From complementary simulations, Brown:2013 found that the growth of an SMBH also affects the nuclear stellar kinematics of a galaxy, increasing _0 . Hartmann:2014 remark that the increase in _0 due to SMBH growth within a bar, seen by Brown:2013 , is only a small effect ( 7 ) compared to that resulting from the formation and evolution of a bar. The M_ DM - _0 Relation As mentioned in Section sec:intro , some theoretical models suggest that dark matter halos should be related to the mass of their SMBHs. Additionally, lambda cold dark matter ( CDM) disk--bulge--halo models indicate that the disks and bulges are formed from the cooling of baryons inside dark matter halos, implying that the disk and bulge of a galaxy should also be partially determined by the properties of its dark matter halo e.g., Haehnelt:1998,Bosch:2000,Zhang:2000 . As a consequence of this bulge--halo connection and the known M_ BH - _0 relation, there should be an M_ DM - _0 correlation. Combining our v_ max - _0 relation (Equation ( eqn:log_v-log_sigma )) with Equation ( eqn:conversion ) yields rCl M_ DM M_ & = & (1.58 0.29) _0 141 km s^ 1 && + (12.03 0.12). Additional uncertainty is introduced through the propagation of errors Error propagation calculations were performed with the python package uncertainties ( http://pythonhosted.org/uncertainties/ ). when Equations ( eqn:log_v-log_sigma ) and ( eqn:conversion ) are combined, however, Equation ( eqn:M_DM-sigma ) provides a useful method of quickly estimating M_ DM in spiral galaxies with bulges when only _0 measurements are available. Zahid:2016 examine the _0 - M_ *,tot relation for massive ( M_ *,tot 10^ 10.3 M_ ) quiescent galaxies at z<0.7 . They find that _0 M_ *,tot ^ 0.3 , which is the same as the scaling relation between the dark matter halo velocity dispersion and halo mass (i.e., _ DM M_ DM ^ 0.3 ) obtained by N body simulations Evrard:2008,Posti:2014 . We can compare our result by rearranging Equation ( eqn:M_DM-sigma ) to show that _0 M_ DM ^ 0.63 0.11 for late-type galaxies has twice the slope derived by Zahid:2016 for early-type galaxies. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first time that the relationship between the mass of the dark matter halo and the central stellar velocity dispersion has been derived or presented for spiral galaxies, although we caution against its extrapolation to late-type ( Sc) spiral galaxies with _0 100 km s^ 1 that potentially are overinfluenced by the disk kinematics. Alternatively, combining Equation ( eqn:log_v-log_sigma_alt ) with Equation ( eqn:conversion ) yields rCl M_ DM M_ & = & (0.74 0.26) _0 141 km s^ 1 && + (12.03 0.12). The M_ BH - v_ max Relation Here, we derive the M_ BH - v_ max scaling relation for the largest sample of spiral galaxies with directly measured black hole masses to date. Our (v_ max , M_ BH ) dataset, consisting of 42 galaxies, can be described by = 0.74 , p=2.24 10^ 8 , r_s=0.72 , and p_s=6.70 10^ 8 . The bces bisector regression yields the relation rCl M_ BH M_ & = & (10.62 1.37) v_ max 210 km s^ 1 && + (7.22 0.09), with _ rms = 0.58 dex and =0.45 dex , both in the M_ BH direction (see Figure fig:M_BH-log_v ). These levels of scatter place the M_ BH - v_ max relation on par with the scatter ( _ rms = 0.63 dex and =0.58 dex ) about the M_ BH - _0 relation for spiral galaxies Davis:2017 . The slope for the M_ BH - v_ max relation is significantly steeper than the slopes presented in previous works. However, the steep slope is in good agreement with the prediction from Equation ( eqn:pred ), consistent at the level of 0.46 , which both confirms that our M_ BH - v_ max relation is in agreement with the Tully--Fisher relation and that the M_ BH - M_ *,tot relation must have the steep slope of 3 reported by Davis:2018 . For reference, the M_ BH -(stellar bulge mass) relation for spiral galaxies has a slope of 2.44_ 0.31 ^ +0.35 Davis:2019 . We do not find barred galaxies to be offset in our M_ BH - v_ max relation (see Figure fig:M_BH-log_v ); the bulk of our galaxies have barred morphologies (35 out of 42). Courteau:2003 found that barred galaxies are not offset in the Tully--Fisher relation, reinforcing our finding that v_ max appears to predict black hole masses equally well for barred and nonbarred galaxies. In a highly influential work, Ostriker:1973 concluded from their simulations of galaxies that massive halos would act to stabilize disks and suppress bar instabilities; thus, bars would preferentially form in galaxies with low-concentration halos. However, we now know that this is wrong. Bosma:1996 remark on the stabilizing influence of a bulge, and Sellwood:2001 find that high central rates of shear can stabilize a disk regardless of the dark matter halo density. In fact, the halo does not stabilize the disk, and even in some cases destabilizes it by increasing the bar instability Athanassoula:2002 . That is, a single M_ *,tot - v_ max Tully--Fisher relation for spiral galaxies combined with a single M_ BH - M_ *,tot relation for spiral galaxies yields a single M_ BH - v_ max relation for spiral galaxies. Based on the behavior of four spiral galaxies, Ferrarese:2002 postulated that halos with M_ DM 5 10^ 11 M_ , i.e., (M_ DM /M_ ) 11.70 , are progressively less efficient or perhaps incapable of forming SMBHs. Alternatively, Wassenhove:2010 find that a few percent of their simulated galaxies with massive black holes encounter stripping during their evolution, which leaves them ``naked,'' having lost most of their surrounding dark matter halos. As can be seen in Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , three of our galaxies (Circinus, NGC 1566, and NGC 4395, two of which were outliers in Figure fig:v-sigma ) have (M_ DM /M_ ) < 11.70 (i.e., v_ max < 159 km s ^ 1 from Equation ( eqn:conversion )). Our results show that 5 10^ 11 M_ is not a hard-and-fast threshold; however, given that our uncertainties on M_ DM are greater than 0.1 dex (see Table table:Sample , Column (9)), our smallest dark matter halos are only marginally below this limit. For example, based on v_ max =98 7 km s^ 1 , we predict that NGC 1566 has a quite low (M_ DM /M_ )=11.67 0.15 . However, because NGC 1566 is a Type I Seyfert galaxy Alloin:1985,Oknyansky:2019 , it is hard to imagine it not possessing an SMBH. If we track the location of the six outliers from Figure fig:v-sigma to Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , we find that the locations of NGC 613, NGC 1300, and NGC 4151 suggest that their maximum rotational velocities are anomalous, rather than their central stellar velocity dispersions or black hole masses.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1300 as having recent central star formation, a sharp bar-edge age drop, and a stellar disk whose radial trend turns negative in the outer regions.

quotes.quote: Some galaxies show evidence of recent star formation in their inner-most region ( R 0.2 R_ 25 ), leading to a sudden decrease of stellar age (e.g., \ NGC 1300, NGC 1365 among others).

Some galaxies show a sharp drop in LumW age at the outer radius of the bar (NGC 1300, NGC 1365, NGC 1672, NGC 7496), consistent with a scenario where the oldest populations dominate in the inner and rounder part of the bar, while young populations are located on more elongated orbits, populating the bar ends, where they spend a larger fraction of their orbiting period Beuther2017, Wozniak2007, Neumann2020 .

Other galaxies, such as NGC 1672 or NGC 1300 show a positive trend across parts of their stellar disk, and then a negative trend in the outer radii. Such a negative trend suggests that in these galaxies, the outer regions have remained relatively quiescent for long periods of time, compared to the inner radii. This does not imply that they lack current star formation, but it means they have not formed a substantial amount of stellar mass in the last 5 Gyr.

sources.summary: NGC 1300 is described as having a pronounced optical bar, nuclear and bar-end radio maxima tied to star formation, weak polarization, and unreliable northern map extensions.

quotes.quote: NGC 1300 (Fig. 6) has one of the most pronounced optical bars in our sample. Total radio intensity is maximum in the nuclear region and at the ends of the bar where the spiral arms start (both are sites of strong star formation). No significant radio emission has been detected in the bar. The polarized emission is weak. The northern extensions visible in the maps at 22 and 6 cm are not real and result from poor data coverage in the uv plane.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1300 as barred, with ansae, tightly wound arms emerging near the bar end, and outer spiral structure linked to chaotic-orbit dynamics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1300: There are indications of a weak central bar with a_2 = 10 within 8 . The main bar reaches an amplitude a_2 = 55 around 64 , where it widens into ansae. A tightly wound set of arms emerges from the end of the bar close to 95 , while a faint spiral pattern can be traced outside 150 with ( a_2 , i ) = (0.2, 13.7 ). A dynamical study by patsis10 suggests that the inner arms are supported by stars on chaotic orbits that escape through the Lagrangian points at the end of the bar.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC1300, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC1300 (Fig. fig:NGC1300 ) is a grand-design spiral, with the bar and 2-arm structure being dominant. A conventional b+d model is created for the sake of completeness but is clearly inadequate. The most notable aspect of its mismatch with the data is that the ``disk" component of the best solution mostly follows the bar's light instead of the large-scale spiral+disk structure.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 1300's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 1300: The bulge model is taken from Fisher2008 , who correctly discard the bar contribution. The huge difference between our R_e ( 4'' ) and the value quoted by Sani2011 , 45.98'' , stems from the fact that they do not separately fit the strong bar of the galaxy and thus include it in the bulge component. The M/L is derived by converting the average of the two values in the F160W band given in Table 4 of Atkinson2005 , using the color of Hyperleda. We adopt the distance of KormendyHo2013 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1300 as a barred spiral galaxy, using observed stellar and gas distributions, HST imaging, CO maps, and a revised 20.7 Mpc distance to construct its simulation potential.

quotes.quote: The structure of the galactic disc Our galaxy is modelled on the barred spiral galaxy, NGC1300, with the stellar potential and gas distribution taken from observational results. Figure fig:CO21_faceon shows the face-on views of V-band image taken with Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and velocity-integrated ^ 12 CO( 2-1 ) intensity map corrected for the position angle and inclination of 85.5^ and 50.2^ England1989a , respectively.

We use 10^6 fixed-motion star particles to create a stellar potential model in keeping with the observed global characteristics of the stellar distribution in NGC1300. This model is based on the work of England1989b , revised with the Hubble parameter of H =73 \ km \ s^ 1 \ Mpc^ 1 and the distance to NGC1300 of d = 20.7 \ Mpc .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1300 as a skewed-barred galaxy with two phase-shift crossings, separating an inner bar-driven spiral from an outer spiral mode.

quotes.quote: Figure ngc1300 shows the interesting case of NGC 1300, which has a well-defined skewed bar and two strong P/N crossings corresponding to the smaller and larger circles on the image. The main P/N crossing associated with the bar lies inside the prominent bar ansae, while the main outer crossing is clearly associated with the outer spiral arms. We conclude: (1) Only the inner portion of the spiral arms associated with the bar is a bar-driven spiral; the outer portion appears to break off at the location of the strong N/P crossing between the two CRs and is likely to be a distinct mode; and (2) the prominent bar spills over its CR radius by more than 30 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1300 as an example of a strong skewed bar, suggesting its bar may extend to the outer Lindblad resonance rather than corotation.

quotes.quote: BZ11 describe the case of NGC 1300 as an example. In NGC 1300, there is a clear skewness to the apparently strong bar, meaning it is a very open spiral. In such a case, it is possible that the bar extends to its OLR and not to its CR. BZ09 show two other examples of superfast bars (NGC 4902 and NGC 5643).

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 1300 as a possible weak AGN, identifying it as one of two additional candidates from relatively high [N II] line ratios.

quotes.quote: Also, in an attempt to find any unknown AGN we use [ N 2 ] and [ S 2 ] line flux ratios (relative to H ) and find tentative evidence for weak AGN in NGC 1300 and NGC 4536.
Two possible additional weak AGN, indicated by the relatively high values of the [ N 2 ] ratios are NGC 1300 and NGC 4536.
colina1 ) we attempted to identify any unknown AGN by using [ N 2 ] and [ S 2 ] line flux ratios (relative to H ) and found tentative evidence for weak AGN in NGC 1300 and NGC 4536.

gmw_00000746.name: PKS 1045 188
gmw_00000746.overview: PKS 1045 188 is a core-dominated quasar [arXiv:2012.04923] and a hybrid blazar that straddles the Fanaroff-Riley boundary [arXiv:1505.05851]. The active galactic nucleus features a parsec-scale jet that bends on kiloparsec scales [arXiv:2012.04923], displaying five Fanaroff-Riley II approaching jet knots, extensive X-ray emission, and a Fanaroff-Riley I counter-jet plume [arXiv:1505.05851].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1045 188 as a hybrid blazar with FR II approaching jet knots, a counter-jet plume, extensive X-ray jet emission, and limited optical detections.

quotes.quote: We present multiwavelength imaging observations of PKS 1045 188, 8C 1849+670, and PKS 2216 038, three radio-loud active galactic nuclei from the MOJAVE-Chandra Sample that straddle the Fanaroff-Riley (FR) boundary between low- and high-power jets. These hybrid sources provide an excellent opportunity to study jet emission mechanisms and the influence of the external environment. We used archival VLA observations, and new Hubble and Chandra observations to identify and study the spectral properties of five knots in PKS 1045 188, two knots in 8C 1849+670, and three knots in PKS 2216 038.

PKS 1045 188 Given that MOJAVE VLBA imaging shows a one-sided parsec-scale jet at position angle 146 (measured from north through east), we consider the kiloparsec-scale jet at position angle 125 Lister13 to correspond to the approaching jet. The 4.8 GHz VLA image shows five FR II approaching jet knots and the FR I counter jet plume. Extensive X-ray emission is present along the approaching jet up to and including knot C, after which the radio jet bends 90 ^ and continues to knots D and E (Figure fig:1045rad ).

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1045 188 as a selected core-dominated quasar, using its pc/kpc jet bend and X-ray knots to constrain central-source inverse Compton emission.

quotes.quote: These criteria are met by the following objects: 3C 273, PKS 1127 145, PKS 1045 188, PKS 1510 089.
It can be seen that, if the pc-scale jet deviates from the pc-scale jet in the direction of the z axis, then 0^ < <180^ , as is the case of PKS 0637 752 and PKS 1045 188.

Therefore, we assume that the X-ray radiation of all knots of the jets of PKS 0637 752, PKS 1045 188, and PKS 1510 089 is formed due to IC/CS. In the farthest knots of these jets detected in the radio range, X-rays are not detected, and it is possible that IC/CMB occurs in them.

gmw_00000747.name: 8C 1849+670
gmw_00000747.overview: 8C 1849+670 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus that straddles the Fanaroff-Riley boundary [arXiv:1505.05851]. Its kiloparsec-scale structure features two Fanaroff-Riley class I approaching jet knots and a terminal Fanaroff-Riley class II counter-jet hotspot [arXiv:1505.05851].

sources.summary: The paper studies 8C 1849+670 as a radio-loud hybrid blazar with two FR I approaching jet knots, a terminal FR II counter-hotspot, and one X-ray detected knot.

quotes.quote: We present multiwavelength imaging observations of PKS 1045 188, 8C 1849+670, and PKS 2216 038, three radio-loud active galactic nuclei from the MOJAVE-Chandra Sample that straddle the Fanaroff-Riley (FR) boundary between low- and high-power jets. These hybrid sources provide an excellent opportunity to study jet emission mechanisms and the influence of the external environment. We used archival VLA observations, and new Hubble and Chandra observations to identify and study the spectral properties of five knots in PKS 1045 188, two knots in 8C 1849+670, and three knots in PKS 2216 038.

8C 1849+670 Given that MOJAVE VLBA imaging shows a one-sided parsec-scale jet at position angle 52 , we consider the kiloparsec-scale jet at position angle 0 Lister13 to correspond to the approaching jet. The 1.4 GHz VLA image shows two FR I approaching jet knots and the terminal FR II counter jet hotspot. X-ray emission is detectable only from the approaching jet knot A (Figure fig:1849rad ). No optical emission is detected from either jet (Figures fig:1849opt and fig:1849opt2 ).

gmw_00000748.name: PKS 2216 038
gmw_00000748.overview: PKS 2216 038 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1505.05851] classified as a hybrid blazar [arXiv:1505.05851]. Its structure features three Fanaroff-Riley Class I approaching jet knots with extensive X-ray emission, alongside a Fanaroff-Riley Class II counter-jet hotspot [arXiv:1505.05851].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2216 038 as a hybrid blazar with three FR I approaching jet knots, an FR II counter-hotspot, strong X-ray jet emission, and unclear nearby optical field-galaxy association.

quotes.quote: We present multiwavelength imaging observations of PKS 1045 188, 8C 1849+670, and PKS 2216 038, three radio-loud active galactic nuclei from the MOJAVE-Chandra Sample that straddle the Fanaroff-Riley (FR) boundary between low- and high-power jets. These hybrid sources provide an excellent opportunity to study jet emission mechanisms and the influence of the external environment. We used archival VLA observations, and new Hubble and Chandra observations to identify and study the spectral properties of five knots in PKS 1045 188, two knots in 8C 1849+670, and three knots in PKS 2216 038.

PKS 2216 038 Given that MOJAVE VLBA imaging shows a one-sided parsec-scale jet at position angle 170 , we consider the kiloparsec-scale jet at position angle 135 Lister13 to correspond to the approaching jet. The 1.4 GHz VLA image shows three FR I approaching jet knots and the FR II counter jet hotspot. Extensive X-ray emission is present throughout the approaching jet (Figure fig:2216rad ). The HST image shows a field galaxy near the optical core position, but no clear jet emission (Figures fig:2216opt and fig:2216opt2 ). It is not clear if this galaxy is physically close to the host galaxy of PKS 2216 038 because no spectroscopic information is available on it.

gmw_00000749.name: PDS 456
gmw_00000749.overview: PDS 456 is a highly luminous, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:0912.4946, arXiv:1910.00593] that hosts a compact rotating molecular disk [arXiv:1903.10528] and a complex radio-emitting nucleus [arXiv:2008.05194]. The system exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:0912.4946] and highly ionized ultra-fast outflows [arXiv:1801.04302, arXiv:2401.07676].

sources.summary: The paper presents PDS 456 as a luminous radio-quiet quasar with broad emission lines, rapid X-ray variability, spectral changes, reflection, and intermittent ultra-high-velocity outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: quasars: individual: PDS 456 -- X-rays: galaxies -- techniques: spectroscopic \ was discovered as a radio-quiet luminous quasar in the course of the Pico dos Dias survey (PDS) of bright IRAS sources torres97 . The optical spectrum of \ features broad emission lines and a redenning-corrected luminosity 30 higher than that of 3C 273 simpson99 . The high luminosity of \ provides a low limit of 10^9 M _ on the black hole mass, just from the Eddington limit. Rapid \ variability of \ was first noticed with the \ satellite in 1998 by reeves00 , who observed the 2 -- 10 keV flux double in 17 ks, which is rather uncommon for such a luminous quasar and indicative of an unusually compact source of a few gravitational radii.

The present spectrum is considerably different from several previous \ spectra recorded for \ since 1998. The ultra-high-velocity outflow seen as recently as February 2007 is not detected in absorption. Conversely, a significant reflection component is detected ( ^2 = 313 compared to a simple absorbed power law). The reflection model suggests the reflecting medium may be outflowing at a velocity v/ = -0.06 0.02 ( ^2 = 28 compared to v/ = 0 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies PDS 456 as a redshift 0.184 quasar with a prototype X-ray ultra-fast outflow and argues its UV BAL is likely a record-speed outflow.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage The quasar PDS 456 (at redshift 0.184) has a prototype ultra-fast outflow (UFO) measured in X-rays. This outflow is highly ionized with relativistic speeds, large total column densities N_ H ( cm ^ 2 ) >23 , and large kinetic energies that could be important for feedback to the host galaxy. A UV spectrum of PDS 456 obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope in 2000 contains one well-measured broad absorption line (BAL) at 1346 \ (observed) that might be \ at v 0.06c or 1240 at v 0.08c .

We argue that the UV BAL is probably 1549 at v 0.30c . This would be the fastest UV outflow ever reported, but its speed is similar to the X-ray outflow and its appearance overall is similar to relativistic UV BALs observed in other quasars. The \ BAL identification is also supported indirectly by the tentative detection of another broad \ line at v 0.19 . The high speeds suggest that the UV outflow originates with the X-ray UFO crudely 20 to 30 r_g from the central black hole. We speculate that the \ BAL might form in dense clumps embedded in the X-ray UFO, requiring density enhancements of only 0.4 dex compared clumpy structures already inferred for the soft X-ray absorber in PDS 456.

sources.summary: The paper studies PDS 456 as a luminous radio-quiet quasar with powerful X-ray outflows, compact radio components, and recent low-power jet-driven AGN activity.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The correlation position for PDS 456 is R.A.= 17^ h 28^ m 19 7920 , Dec.= 14 15 55 912 (J2000).

quotes.quote: PDS 456 is a radio-quiet quasar at z=0.184 Simpson1999 with a black hole mass of M_ bh 10^9 M _ Nardini2015 and is one of a few AGN known to host an extremely powerful outflow 10 ^ 46 erg s ^ 1 , Nardini2015, Tombesi2016 and a compact radio counterpart Yun2004 . It is one of the most luminous quasars in the Universe at z<0.3 Simpson1999, Torres1997 and has a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 Reeves2000 , close to its Eddington luminosity L_ Edd Nardini2015 . X-ray spectroscopic observations indicate a powerful mildly relativistic outflow covering a solid angle of 3 and active over time scales of years Matzeu2017, Nardini2015 . The X-ray outflow shows a tight correlation with the X-ray luminosity, indicating that the outflow is likely driven by the radiation pressure Matzeu2017 .

In order to understand the origin of compact radio emission from the nuclear region, we observed the optically and X-ray luminous but radio-quiet quasar PDS 456 with the e-EVN at 5 GHz. In the image with the full resolution, we found two faint and diffuse radio components with a separation of about 20 pc and an average brightness temperature of around two million Kelvin. In relation to the sub-mas-accuracy Gaia position, the two components are putatively located on opposite sides of an unobserved radio core. The radio structure indicates recent jet-driven AGN activity at a low radio power.

sources.summary: PDS 456 is a radio-quiet quasar with a long-established ultra-fast outflow; new XMM-Newton spectra resolve soft X-ray absorber lines matching its iron-K wind velocity.

quotes.quote: As discussed above, the radio-quiet quasar PDS 456 (at z=0.184 , Torres97 ) was one of the first prototype examples of an ultra fast outflow. Indeed, since its initial detection in 2001 with \ Reeves03 , the presence of the ultra fast outflow in PDS 456 has now been established through over a decade's worth of X-ray observations Reeves09,Behar10,Reeves14,Gofford14,Nardini15,Hagino15,Matzeu16,Matzeu17a,Matzeu17b,Parker18,Reeves18a,Reeves18b,BM19 . Furthermore Hamann18 recently claimed a fast UV counterpart to the X-ray wind on the basis of a broad C iv trough predicted from photoionization modeling. In this paper we reveal the structure of the wind through the detection of resolved soft X-ray absorption lines in the XMM-Newton RGS, which as we will show, exactly match the outflow velocity of the wind as measured in the iron K band.

A new observation of PDS 456 from September 2019 was presented which revealed three resolved lines in the soft X-ray spectrum taken with the XMM-Newton RGS. The lines are required to originate from a fast outflowing absorber, which when fitted with xstar was found to have an outflow velocity of v/=-0.258 0.003 , with the lines originating from the 1s-2p transitions of O viii Ly , Ne ix He and Ne x Ly . The outflow velocity of the soft X-ray absorber is entirely consistent with the main zone of iron K absorption, confirmed from the same dataset, which has an outflow velocity of v/=-0.261 0.007 .

In PDS 456, the clumpy, lower ionization soft X-ray absorbing clouds likely form from gas further out (on parsec scales) compared to the highest ionization phase of the disk wind, which is launched closer to the black hole.

sources.summary: PDS 456 is presented as a luminous local radio-quiet AGN with powerful ultra-fast outflows; GRAVITY infers the sample's largest BLR radius and a lower BH mass than scaling estimates.

quotes.quote: PDS 456 is known to be the most luminous radio-quiet AGN in the local Universe ( z 0.3) Torres1997, Simpson1999, Bischetti2019, Yun2004 . Aside from its brightness and proximity, it has been extensively studied due to its ultra-fast outflows (UFOs) of highly ionised Fe Reeves2003 . These outflows possess high kinetic power ( 20 of its bolometric luminosity; Luminari2018 ) and are also radiatively driven, which can be explained by the AGN's high Eddington ratio Matzeu2017 . Its BH mass, unfortunately, is not well-studied. No RM measurements are available for PDS 456 because such a luminous target should produce very long time lags ( several years), requiring decade-long RM campaigns.

Our BLR modelling with PDS 456 infers a BLR radius of log R_ BLR [ ld ] = 2.49 ^ +0.08 _ 0.38 ; the largest among our sample. The best-fit BH mass of PDS 456 is log M_ BH [ M_ ] = 8.23 ^ +0.01 _ 0.49 . Our BH mass is a factor of 10 smaller than that measured by Reeves2009 and Nardini2015 via scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper treats PDS 456 as a bright low-redshift quasar and fits its spectrum with power-law plus disc reflection, host-redshift reflection, narrow absorption lines, and modeled high-energy emission.

quotes.quote: PDS 456 Similarly to h0419 and 1365, we model the spectrum of 456 with a two component powerlaw plus disc reflection interpretation. As previously stated, we allow for neutral absorption in excess of the Galactic value, however in this instance find the excess absorption is located at z=0 , so we allow the local absorption to vary with a lower limit of the Galactic value. The photon index is the same for the intrinsic and reflected components, the redshift of the reflection fixed at at that of the host galaxy, ( z=0.184 ; Torres97 ) and the outer disc radius fixed at 400 . In addition, we also include two absorption lines to model the narrow lines at 7.67 and 8.13 \ identified by .

This reflection-based model provides a good fit to the observed spectrum, with = 414/379. As shown in Fig. fig_pds , the high energy data are well modelled, as is the strong emission feature at 0.8 \ (see Fig. fig_ratio ), identified here with iron L-shell emission, as in .

PDS 456 is one of the brightest low redshift quasars known, and although the observation analysed here finds the source with the lowest flux to date it is still emitting at an intrinsic rest frame luminosity of L_ 0.5-10.0 10^ 45 .

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA observations of PDS 456's host, finding a compact rotating molecular disk plus a galaxy-wide molecular outflow that may quench star formation.

quotes.quote: However, since _ of is the ratio between a time-averaged quantity ( E_ of ) and an instantaneous quantity ( E _ UFO ), a small value may be also explained in terms of an "outburst" phase of the UFO in the two sources with L_ Bol 10^ 47 \ considered here (i.e., PDS 456 and APM 08279 + 5255). Alternatively, a small coupling of the UFO with the host-galaxy ISM can be invoked to account for the short _ of observed in these QSOs in a scenario where the kpc-scale outflow is undergoing an energy conserving expansion. In this work, we report on the ALMA observation of the 1 mm continuum and 32 \ line emission in \ ( z_ CO =0.185 ).

These data enable us to probe with unprecedented spatial resolution ( 700 pc) the ISM in the host-galaxy of a hyper-luminous ( L_ Bol 10^ 47 ) QSO. We provide the first detection of a molecular outflow in a radio-quiet, non-lensed source at the brightest end of the AGN luminosity function. Our observation highlights the importance of the combined high spatial resolution and high sensitivity provided by ALMA in revealing broad wings much weaker than the core of the CO emission line, and disentangling the relative contribution of outflowing and quiescent molecular gas to the emission from the innermost regions around QSOs.

We detect at 350 significance the 32 \ emission from the host-galaxy of , finding that the bulk of the molecular gas reservoir is located in a rotating disk with compact size ( 1.3 kpc) seen under a small inclination ( i 25 deg), with an intrinsic circular velocity of 280 .

sources.summary: The paper measures PDS 456 with differential visibility methods, describes it as a compact radio-quiet luminous quasar, and adopts optical/UV-IR luminosities because of strong X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: An example is shown in fig:diffamp for PDS 456, where the visibility amplitude averaged over long baselines shows a significant increase at the spectral line, following the expected behavior.
PDS 456 also shows a compact size and is radio quiet.

PDS 456 : This source is very luminous in UV/optical. Its 5100 \ luminosity is 2 10^ 46 Simpson1999MNRAS,Reeves2009ApJ , corresponding to L_ bol, 5100 = 1.0 10^ 47 . This is very well consistent with the bolometric luminosity derived from UV--IR SED Simpson1999MNRAS . PDS 456 is extremely variable in X-ray Reeves2002MNRAS and the observed 2--10 keV luminosity is only 0.2 of the bolometric luminosity Reeves2009ApJ . Therefore, we do not quote the bolometric luminosity estimated from X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper uses PDS 456 as the z=0.2 prototype quasar, running ten tuned simulations and predicting heavy and light seed formation through its assembly history.

quotes.quote: We choose as proto-typical objects for the three redshifts the quasars J1148 at z_ QSO =6.4 Fan01 , SDSS J2345+1104 at z=2 hereafter J2345 Shen11, Schulze19 , and PDS 456 at z=0.2 PDS456 hereafter Nardini15, Bischetti19 . We model the evolution of J1148, J2345 and PDS456 performing, for each of them, ten independent simulations adopting the set of model parameters described in Table  tab:parameters . These are tuned to reproduced the observed SMBH mass and host galaxy physical properties see for more details Valiante11,Valiante14,Valiante16 .

A total of 39 (31 and 40) heavy and 4228 (5327 and 5319) light seeds are formed along the assembly history of J1148 (J2345 and PDS456, respectively). In all cases, light seeds form in larger numbers at very high redshift ( 12<z<30 ) and over a longer period of cosmic evolution than heavy seeds, which are rarer (with relative fraction 1 ) and form for a shorter period of time at slightly lower redshift ( z 12 - 17 , depending on the considered system). In-situ and/or external pollution determines the end of the seed (and Pop III stars) formation era: as soon as all the galaxies have been enriched above the critical metallicity threshold ( Z_ cr =10^ 3.8 Z_ ), the transition to the Pop II star formation regime is completed. This critical level is reached, on average, at z 16 , 13 and 12 for quasar J1148, J2345 and PDS456, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper models PDS 456 as a luminous low-redshift radio-quiet quasar whose ultra-fast outflow broadens variance-spectrum spikes and likely has multiple velocity layers.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present an improved model for excess variance spectra describing ultra-fast outflows and successfully apply it to the luminous ( L_ bol 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 ) low-redshift ( z=0.184 ) quasar PDS 456. The model is able to account well for the broadening of the spike-like features of these outflows in the excess variance spectrum of PDS 456, by considering two effects: a correlation between the outflow velocity and the logarithmic X-ray flux and intrinsic Doppler broadening with v_ int = 10^4 km s ^ 1 . The models were generated by calculating the fractional excess variance of count spectra from a Monte Carlo simulation. We find evidence that the outflow in PDS 456 is structured, i.e., that there exist two or more layers with outflow velocities 0.27 - 0.30 , 0.41 - 0.49 , and 0.15 - 0.20 for a possible third layer, which agrees well with the literature.

PDS 456 was discovered serendipitously during the Pico del Dias Survey (PDS) by Torres97 at z=0.184 and it is considered the most luminous ( L_ bol 10^ 47 erg s^ 1 ; Simpson99,Reeves00 ) radio-quiet quasar in the local Universe. Such high luminosity is more typical of sources at red shift z=2 - 3 , the peak of quasar activity, when AGN feedback played an important role in the evolution of galaxies DiMatteo05 . During a 40 ks XMM-Newton snapshot in 2001, Reeves03 detected a deep absorption trough above 7 keV likely corresponding to highly ionized iron with an outflow velocity of v_ out 0.10c . After nearly 20 years of additional studies, the high velocity outflow detected in the Fe K band is well established e.g., Reeves09,Nardini15,Matzeu17,Parker18_pds456,Boissay19 and ranging between v_ out 0.25 - 0.35c .

sources.summary: The paper applies a Monte Carlo disk-wind model to PDS 456, explaining its variable blueshifted absorption lines through viewing-angle changes rather than large mass-outflow changes.

quotes.quote: By applying our wind model to the multi-epoch X-ray data of an archetypal wind source PDS 456, we find the variability in the absorption line is explained by a change of the wind outflowing angle without any large variability in the mass outflow rate of the wind. This result indicates that the fast disk winds are stable and that local hydrodynamic instabilities produce a large time variability of the absorption line.

We apply our spectral model to observational data of an archetypal disk wind source PDS 456. This source is observed by Suzaku for 5 times between 2007 and 2013, and most of these data clearly show blueshifted absorption lines Reeves2009,Gofford2014 . Also, as already shown in Fig. fig:ufospec , the absorption line features strongly vary. It means that this source is one of the best target to study the origin of the spectral variation in the highly ionized absorption lines.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Suzaku observations of PDS 456, finding a long soft X-ray lag consistent with inner-disc reverberation and modeling its spectrum with relativistic reflection plus warm absorbers.

quotes.quote: By analysing the archival Suzaku observations totalling an exposure time of 770 ks, we discover a soft lag of 10 3.4  ks at 9.58 10^ 6 Hz in the luminous quasar PDS 456, which is the longest soft lag and lowest Fourier frequency reported to date. In this study, we use the maximum likelihood method to deal with non-continuous nature of the Suzaku light curves. The result follows the mass-scaling relation for soft lags, which further supports that soft lags originate from the innermost areas of AGN and hence are best interpreted by the reflection scenario. Spectral analysis has been performed in this work and we find no evidence of clumpy partial-covering absorbers. The spectrum can be explained by a self-consistent relativistic reflection model with warm absorbers, and spectral variations over epochs can be accounted for by the change of the continuum, and of column density and ionization states of the warm absorbers.

PDS 456 is a luminous ( L_ bol =10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , Simpson99 ) nearby (z = 0.184, Torres97 ), radio-quiet quasar, which is known for powerful disc winds/outflows Reeves03,Reeves09 and rapid X-ray variability Reeves00,Reeves02 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PDS 456 as a radio-quiet quasar with multi-velocity ultra-fast outflows, likely radiatively driven near an Eddington-accreting black hole and powerful enough to affect its host galaxy.

quotes.quote: Considering the fact that the supermassive black hole in PDS 456 is accreting at about the Eddington limit, its detected UFO should be radiatively driven Matzeu2017a,Nardini2015 , supported by a correlation between the velocity of the outflow and the ionizing luminosity, with UV line driving strongly contributing to the wind acceleration Hagino2015,Hamann2018,Reeves2018b . However, a MHD-wind model has also been successfully applied recently to XMM-Newton and NuSTAR data of PDS 456 by . Despite the fact that PDS 456 is a radio-quiet quasar, it has been observed recently by the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 5 GHz Yang2019 .

We found that all the winds detected in the three datasets are thermally stable and can coexist. The outflow of PDS 456 is probably composed of several components from multiple layers having different velocities and ionizations, launched from the accretion flow close to the supermassive black hole, and certainly radiatively driven. Both relativistic components of the outflow are powerful enough to play a role in the evolution of the host galaxy, with mass outflow rates of 2-20 and kinetic powers of 0.8-8 of the Eddington values.

sources.summary: The paper uses VLBI observations of PDS 456 to reveal a complex radio nucleus, interpreting its extended emission as likely wind-driven shocks tied to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: Among the nearby critical accretion quasars, PDS 456 has a very massive black hole (about one billion solar masses), shows a significant star-forming activity (about seventy solar masses per year) and hosts exceptionally energetic X-ray winds (power up to twenty per cent of the Eddington luminosity). To probe the radio activity in this extreme accretion and feedback system, we performed very-long-baseline interferometric (VLBI) observations of PDS 456 at 1.66 GHz with the European VLBI Network (EVN) and the enhanced Multi-Element Remotely Linked Interferometry Network (e-MERLIN). We find a rarely-seen complex radio-emitting nucleus consisting of a collimated jet and an extended non-thermal radio emission region.

The diffuse emission region has a size of about 360 pc and a radio luminosity about three times higher than the nearby extreme starburst galaxy Arp 220. The powerful nuclear radio activity could result from either a relic jet with a peculiar geometry (nearly along the line of sight) or more likely from diffuse shocks formed naturally by the existing high-speed winds impacting on high-density star-forming regions. If the component W is associated with the nuclear molecular outflows, and formed by the AGN winds sweeping up the high-density star-forming region, the energy conversion efficiency is L_ R L_ bol 10 ^ 7 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PDS 456 as a luminous local QSO whose host has companion clumps, molecular gas, cold dust constraints, AGN-dominated radio emission, and unusual ULIRG-to-QSO transition properties.

quotes.quote: We report new , radio continuum, and CO (1--0) imaging observations and 850 m photometric observations of PDS 456, the most luminous QSO in the local universe ( z<0.3 ). The 0.6'' resolution \ image obtained using the Keck telescope shows three compact m_K 16.5 ( M_K 21 ) sources at a projected distance of 10 kpc to the southwest, and the host galaxy of PDS 456 may be interacting or merging with one or more companions. The observations using the OVRO millimeter array has revealed a narrow CO (1--0) line (FWHM = 181 ) centered at z=0.1849 , and 9 10^9 M_ of molecular gas mass is inferred.

Radio continuum luminosity is nearly an order of magnitude larger than expected from its FIR luminosity, and the radio source, unresolved by the 2 \ beam of the VLA, is dominated by the AGN activity. Our 850 m photometric observations suggest that the cold dust content of the host galaxy is less than one half of the amount in Arp 220. The analysis of the spectral energy distribution reveals both a QSO-like and a ULIRG-like nature, and the observed IR, X-ray, and gas properties suggest that the AGN activity dominates its luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper finds unusual line behavior in PDS 456: weak blended emission, difficult optical subtraction, low Ly-fluorescence contribution, and evidence that collisional excitation dominates broad O I emission.

quotes.quote: The only exceptions were PDS 456 and H 1821 + 643, where we used the Pa profile instead. In PDS 456, the 1.1287 m profile is much narrower than that of 8446 , and in H 1821 + 643, 1.1287 m appears to be blue-shifted relative to 8446 by 1400 km s ^ 1 .

No attempt was made to correct the broad line profiles of IRAS 1750 + 508 and PDS 456 for the presence of a narrow line component. The emission lines of IRAS 1750 + 508 show a very strong blue asymmetry, and those of PDS 456 are very weak and strongly blended with (optical) .

In summary, Ly fluorescence is the dominant production mechanism of broad 8446 in just over half of our sources (14/23 objects). In roughly a third of our sources (6/23 objects) Ly fluorescence accounts for about half of the broad 8446 flux, and in only a few sources it is not the dominant process. In one of these few sources, namely, PDS 456, collisional excitation dominates instead.

sources.summary: PDS 456 has a unique enhanced primary component, a reflection-like higher component, rapid variability, a strong absorption edge, high-velocity outflow, and successful relativistic broadband modeling.

quotes.quote: PDS 456 The primary component we find for the z=0.184 quasar PDS 456 appears to be unique. Rather than being suppressed at the energy of the soft excess and iron line, this component appears to be enhanced. The second component appears to be a good match for the third or fourth order components described in mcg6objects (although more redshifted), which are attributed to relativistic reflection variability. PDS 456 is rapidly variable and shows a strong absorption edge at 8 keV Reeves00 , and there is strong evidence of high-velocity outflowing material OBrien05,Reeves14 . The broad band spectrum has been successfully modelled using a standard relativistic model by Walton10 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PDS 456's X-ray spectral variability, finding a variable Fe K ultra-fast outflow feature whose line strength responds to the continuum.

quotes.quote: We present a spectral-variability analysis of the low-redshift quasar PDS 456 using principal component analysis. In the \ data, we find a strong peak in the first principal component at the energy of the Fe absorption line from the highly blueshifted outflow. This indicates that the absorption feature is more variable than the continuum, and that it is responding to the continuum.

The prototypical AGN for studying UFOs is the low-redshift ( z=0.184 ) quasar PDS 456. This source shows strong and highly variable high-energy Fe absorption features e.g. Reeves09, Reeves14 from a UFO ( v 0.24c ).

sources.summary: The paper models the UFO in quasar PDS 456, constraining its wind covering fraction, opening angle, and velocity to assess AGN feedback from disk winds.

quotes.quote: We aim to establish new constraints on the covering fraction and on the kinematic properties of the UFO in the powerful ( L_ bol 10^ 47 erg/s ) quasar PDS 456, an established Rosetta stone for studying AGN feedback from disk winds. This will allow us to estimate the mass outflow rate and the energy transfer rate of the wind, which are key quantities to understand the potential impact on the host galaxy.

Using the novel wind emission (WINE) model, we constrained the velocity (mean value 0.23 ), opening angle (71 deg), and covering fraction (0.7) of the UFO in the quasar PDS 456. The results of our analysis are in agreement with the estimates on the bulk wind velocity and C_f in N15. For the first quantity they estimated 0.25 , which is inside our radial excursion ( 0.17 \ \ 0.28 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes PDS 456 in its wind comparison, combining black-hole and bolometric luminosity estimates with X-ray UFO properties and a multi-component CO galaxy-scale outflow.

quotes.quote: PDS 456 . We use the M_ BH determined by Nardini+15 using the 5100 luminosity and the \ FWHM. We take the averaged L_ BOL from two different estimates, the first from the 5100 luminosity with bolometric correction Richards+06 , the second using the 1350 luminosity from Hamann+18 . The properties of the X-ray UFO come from Nardini+15 , where they specifically determined a wind opening angle /4 of 0.5 . A galaxy-scale outflow is detected in CO by Bischetti+19 and consists of an extended component, in turn partitioned into four different blobs, and a compact component, which largely dominates the energetics of the system. For the purpose of measuring the outflow energetics with consider both components together.

sources.summary: The review describes PDS 456 as a luminous quasar with ultrafast-outflow absorption features, a large inferred wind solid angle, and wind models fitting its XMM-Newton data.

quotes.quote: Early observations revealed absorption-like features above 7 keV (rest-frame) in the distant ( z=3.91 ) lensed quasar APM 08279+5255 , the nearby ( z=0.184 ) luminous quasar PDS 456 , and the narrow-line Seyfert 1 quasar, PG 1211+143 . Attributed to K-shell absorption lines from Fe xxv (He-like, 6.70 keV) and Fe xxvi (H-like, 6.97 keV) implies relativistic velocities between 0.1-0.3 .

Indeed, in PDS 456 an average solid angle of 3.2 is estimated . In general, the application of P-Cygni profiles to simultaneously fit the absorption and emission from the wind imply it might be close to spherically symmetric . As shown in Figure fig:pdspg , the MHD- and radiative-driven winds describe XMM-Newton data of PDS 456 and PG 1211+143 relatively well.

sources.summary: The paper argues PDS 456 likely hosts a fossil outflow because its weak, detached, lopsided molecular outflow coexists with a recently restarted nuclear ultra-fast outflow.

quotes.quote: PDS 456 , conversely, has a notoriously weak outflow, with energy rates lower than even the momentum-driven model would predict Bischetti2019AA . The CO map of the outflow Fig. 3a in Bischetti2019AA shows that the ``extended'' outflow is clearly detached from the nucleus. It is also highly lopsided: the two outflow bubbles are located to the east and south of the nucleus. Both properties suggest that it is a fossil; this conclusion helps explain the weakness of the outflow component as well. The system hosts a powerful ultra-fast outflow (AGN wind) in the nucleus Nardini2015Sci, Matzeu2022arXiv , but it is likely that the AGN has just recently restarted after a period of quiescence Zubovas2020MNRAS .

sources.summary: The paper describes PDS 456 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: PDS 456 is a radio-quiet AGN at a redshift of z=0.184 with a black hole mass of 1.58 10^9 M _ Nardini2015 . This source is known for its ultra-fast wind with an outflow velocity of 0.25-0.3c Boissay2019,Nardini2015 . In the X-ray regime, the first flare was detected Reeves2000 and later, rapid variability has also been observed in long observations with different X-ray observatories. In this work, we used XMM-Newton and Swift /XRT observations of PDS 456. The observation details are given in Table table:obs_log .

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton spectroscopy of PDS 456, finding deep X-ray absorption and highly ionized iron features that require an extreme, massive quasar outflow.

quotes.quote: We report on XMM-Newton spectroscopic observations of the luminous, radio-quiet quasar PDS 456. The hard X-ray spectrum of PDS 456 shows a deep absorption trough (constituting 50 of the continuum) at energies above 7 keV in the quasar rest frame, which can be attributed to a series of blue-shifted K-shell absorption edges due to highly ionized iron. The higher resolution soft X-ray RGS spectrum exhibits a broad absorption line feature near 1 keV, which can be modeled by a blend of L-shell transitions from highly ionized iron (Fe xvii - xxiv ). An extreme outflow velocity of 50000 km s ^ 1 is required to model the K and L shell iron absorption present in the XMM-Newton data. Overall, a large column density ( N_ H =5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) of highly ionized gas (log =2.5 ) is required in PDS 456.

sources.summary: PDS 456 shows strong spectral variability, lacks sufficient HXD/PIN counts for reflection fitting, and exhibits year-scale Fe K absorber variability with changing ionisation states.

quotes.quote: The remaining nine sources with more than one observation (3C 111, Fairall 9, Mrk 766, NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 3783, NGC 4051 and PDS 456) showed strong spectral variability in both the shape of the spectrum and/or drastic changes in flux state which made co-adding impractical.
PDS 456 and APM 08279+5255 are not fitted with a reflection component because they lack sufficient counts in the HXD/PIN..

Similar is also true in Mrk 766, PDS 456 and NGC 1365, which all exhibit absorption line variability albeit over a longer (i.e., t year) baseline. In both Mrk 766 and PDS 456 the absorber appears to change in ionisation states between the epochs, with the outflow in Mrk 766 also showing a decrease in outflow velocity.

sources.summary: The paper uses PDS 456 as a nearby analogue, noting its Eddington-accreting black hole and complex VLBI radio nucleus with extended emission and a faint jet.

quotes.quote: The best nearby ( z < 0.3 ) analogy to might be the optically luminous quasar PDS 456 at z=0.184 . PDS 456 has a 10^9 M_ black hole accreting at the Eddington rate, both of which are similar to those of our target. The VLBI observations of PDS 456 revealed a quite complex radio nucleus consisting of some very extended emission and a faint jet, including a candidate radio core at 1.66 GHz Yang2020 . At the higher frequency > 5 GHz, only the candidate radio core would be detectable because of its partially optically thick radio spectrum Yang2019, Yang2020 .

sources.summary: The review describes PDS 456 as a variable quasar with P-Cygni wind profiles, soft-X-ray high-velocity outflows, luminosity-correlated FeK absorption speeds, and constrained molecular-outflow driving.

quotes.quote: For example, the PCygni profiles detected in the and observations of the quasar PDS 456 yielded a solid angle (filled by the winds) =3.2 0.6 sr (See Figure Fig:pcygni right panel). The high z quasars are an important laboratory for studying the AGN phenomenon, as these are the periods (redshift z 1-3 ) when the quasar luminosity function peaked. It is commonly believed that the SMBH `negative' feedback may have produced quasar downsizing, and hence studying feedback through outflows in the quasars at these redshifts are important to understand the luminosity function evolution. Early studies by 2002ApJ...579..169C detected high velocity Fe XXVI absorption features in the lensed BAL quasar APM 08279+5255 at a redshift of z=3.91 . 2018A&A...610L..13D and 2015ApJ...799...82C detected similar such high velocity outflows in high redshift quasars. However, high redshift studies to date remain only a few and the next generation instruments such as and can shed more light on these interesting sources. Apart from the so called traditional UFOs detected in the 6-9 as Fe absorption features, there have been detections of high velocity outflows using high resolution grating spectra in the soft X-rays ( 0.3-2 ), such as in PG 1211+143 2003MNRAS.346.1025P,2016AN....337..518P,2018ApJ...854...28R , IRAS 17020+4544 2015ApJ...813L..39L , Mrk 590 2015ApJ...798....4G , Ark 564 2013ApJ...772...66G and PDS 456 2020ApJ...895...37R . The UFOs detected in the soft X-ray spectra have velocities in the range 10,000 0.2c , but with a much lower column density ( ( / ) 22 ) and the ionization parameter ( ( / ) 3 ) compared to that of the traditional UFOs detected in the FeK band. The response of the UFOs to varying AGN continuum flux serves as an important indicator of how and when these outflows are generated. Studies on the variable quasar PDS 456 with , and observations found that the centroid energy of the blue shifted FeK absorption profile increases with AGN luminosity, and the wind outflow velocity correlates with the hard X-ray luminosity as v/ L_ 7-30 ^ 0.22 0.04 , which indicates that the wind is predominantly radiatively driven due to the high Eddington ratio of the source 2017MNRAS.472L..15M . Such occurences have also been found in other sources accreting at high Eddington ratio indicating that the UFOs may be driven by intense radiation pressure in sources accreting at or close to the Eddington rate, such as in APM 08279+5255 2003ApJ...595...85C , PG 1211+143 2003MNRAS.345..705P , IRAS F11119+3257 2015Natur.519..436T , 1H 0707-495 2016MNRAS.461.3954H , and IRAS 13224-3809 2017MNRAS.469.1553P . Interesting anti-correlations with the 3-10 luminosity and the UFO absorption equivalent width have been detected for the source IRAS 13224-3809 2017Natur.543...83P indicating a connection between the accretion process and the outflow generation. Although rare, there has been a few tentative detections of ultra-fast ``inflows" indicative of feeding of the black hole accretion disk. These features are detected as absorption at 5.5 and under the assumption that they arise from H- or He- like Fe, implied infall velocities are estimated to be 0.15-0.2c see for e.g., detected in Mrk 509 using BeppoSAX 2005A&A...442..461D . More recent findings of transient absorption signatures of highly ionized Fe lines in the 5-6 pointed towards a short lived ultra-fast inflow with an inflow velocity of v 0.3c in nearby quasars PG 1211+143, 2005ApJ...633L..81R,2018MNRAS.481.1832P . It is important to note that UFOs have also been found in stellar mass blackhole candidates such as IGR J17091-3624 2012ApJ...746L..20K . Photoionization modeling of these winds point towards an origin within 43,300 Schwarzschild radii of the black hole and the BH may be expelling more gas than it accretes. These are interesting case studies because the stellar black hole candidates are possibly the scaled down versions of AGN 2006Natur.444..730M . Although UFOs are of paramount importance in terms of kinematics and SMBH feedback, it has only been possible to detect these outflows in a few dozen AGN, mostly due to lack of SNR and/or spectral resolution in these energy bands. Future telescopes XRISM and Athena will be able to characterize these outflows in much greater detail (See Section Sec:instruments ). The connection between the outflows Figure Fig:outflows shows the typical parameter ranges of the different ionized outflows detected in AGN. We note that there is considerable overlap in the distance scales as well as the outflow parameters ( , and v ) between the NALs and the WA. Similar such overlap is found in the UFO and the BAL outlfows. However, a direct link between these outflows has not yet been established, due to several limiting factors, such as, different sensitivity of the instruments in the energy bands where these outflows are detected, lack of accurate distance estimates of these outflows etc. Keeping in mind these caveats, we can possibly say that the UFO and the BALs originate from the innermost regions of the accretion disk, and the BALs may be the lowest ionization tails of UFOs. Future work on a systematic search for BALs in systems where lower ionization UFOs (soft X-ray) are detected can provide more insights. A few BAL quasars have also shown UFO for example, Mrk 231, APM 08279+5255 2015A&A...583A..99F,2002ApJ...579..169C . However, it is not clear if they form the same outflow, or the UFO drives the BAL. Attempts to connect the UV NAL outflows and the warm absorbers as a part of a single phase wind 1994ApJ...434..493M have met with mixed response for different sources. Recent multiwavelength high resolution campaigns on sources such as NGC 3783, NGC 5548, NGC 4593 have found that the soft-X-ray outflows are kinematically similar to the UV outflows, but most of the WA material is in a too high ionization state to produce measurable lines in the UV spectra 2015A&A...577A..37A,2000ApJ...535L..17K,2001ApJ...554..216K,2003ApJ...583..178G,2001ApJ...551..671K,2003ApJ...597..832K . The correlations of the plasma parameters of the UFOs and the WA point towards a possibility that they are a part of the same stratified outflow, the UFOs originating much nearer to the SMBH compared to the WA 2013MNRAS.430.1102T . Supporting this idea, it has been conjectured that the warm absorbers may be generated via a Compton-cooled shocked wind, when a UFO launched very near to the SMBH, loses most of its mechanical energy after shocking against the interstellar medium 2013MNRAS.433.1369P . For example in the narrow line Seyfert 1, NGC 4051, with a black hole mass of ( / ) 6 , the shock radius turns out to be 10^ 17 , much in line with sample studies on WA and UFO 2013MNRAS.430.1102T,2016MNRAS.457.3896L . In some sources UFOs are detected simultaneously in the soft X-rays and also in the FeK band. Such detections have led to a view that these absorbers may have a multi-phase origin, as if a clumpy-ISM has been entrained by an inner UFO, resulting in a simultaneous flow of both the WA and UFO 2019A&A...627A.121S .
