quotes.quote: Another source exhibiting a number of different EVPA rotations, is 3C 279. The largest rotation in its jet amounts 500 ^ counterclockwise rotation, 2016AA...590A..10K and occurs during the quiescent state of the source, shown in Fig. fig_pa_3c279 . 2016AA...590A..10K conclude that the stochastic process is responsible for this rotation. We argue, that the model of a circular motion of a jet can reproduce observed properties of this EVPA rotational event (Fig. fig_rot ), resulted in relatively constant optical flux, zero degree of polarization and continuos change of EVPA. The small, short-term fluctions overlaid on this 500 ^ rotation may arise from the turbulent component of magnetic field, superimposed with a large scale ordered magnetic field.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 279 as a best-known blazar observed by IBIS/ISGRI in a 2015 high state with SED fits allowing leptonic or lepto-hadronic models.

quotes.quote: 2015), the flat-spectrum radio quasars 3C 279, 3C 454.3, PKS 1502+106, and PKS 1510--089 were INTEGRAL targets in various occasions.

IBIS/ISGRI observed 3C 279 ( z = 0.539 ), the best known blazar, for 50 ks during its June 2015 high state, as part of the INTEGRAL survey of the Coma region (Bottacini et al. 2016). The broad-band spectral energy distribution is consistent both with a purely leptonic and a lepto-hadronic model, although the latter requires an extreme total jet power close to the Eddington luminosity of a black hole of mass similar to the one thought to lie at the center of 3C 279 ( 2.5 10^ 8 M _ ). This high-state of 3C 279 adds to the rather complex multi-wavelength time behavior known from previous campaigns centered on INTEGRAL observations (Collmar et al.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 279 as an FSRQ whose 2015 outburst enabled low-threshold VHE observations and showed challenging minute-scale gamma-ray variability.

quotes.quote: Indeed, further proof of the successful lowering of the threshold energy of analysis utilising mono data comes from the observations 3C 279, which underwent a giant outburst back in July 2015. mono analysis of these observations of the flare achieved a record low energy threshold energy of 66 GeV for AGN results with this instrument. Fig. 3C279_spectra shows the HESS and Fermi components of its SED of during the second night of the flaring outburst.

Specifically, for 3C 279, minute time-scale structure in the > 100 MeV HE lightcurves was discovered . Likewise, at VHE, for PKS 1510-089 tens of minute time-scale structure. Together, both the lack of internal absorption features in the flaring FSRQ spectra, and the short variability time-scales observed during the flare, make for rather challenging constraints.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 279 as a compact, bright flat-spectrum quasar calibrator whose jet is unresolved by KVN and whose phases calibrate 3C 273.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 is a highly polarized quasar at a redshift of z=0.536 3c279z with flat spectrum and a flux density of up to 10 Jy at 86 GHz. Despite showing a clear jet structure on the south--west direction e.g. MOJAVE , it is not resolved with KVN baselines, making its structure relatively compact for this observation and an ideal source as a calibrator Figure. fig:mapspair1 ; see also iMOGABA-I_paper . Figure fig:fpt2pair1 (left) is an excerpt of Figure fig:fpt2all (right) where we only focus on the scans corresponding to the source pair 3C 279 and 3C 273. The gap between the scans of 3C 273 and 3C 279 is about 30 minutes (i.e. a duty cycle of 1 hour). When we calibrate 3C 279 and apply its phase solutions to 3C 273, all residual phases are well calibrated (Figure fig:fpt2pair1 ,right), the phases of 3C 273 are mostly within 30 degrees around zero for all baselines.

sources.summary: The paper uses the z=0.536 FSRQ 3C 279 as a test case, fitting its 2014 gamma-ray flare SED with synchrotron, SSC, and external Compton components.

quotes.quote: To further study and validate the proposed broadband spectral fitting algorithm using XSPEC, we choose the well studied FSRQ, 3C 279 ( z=0.536 ), as a test case. We select the flaring epoch of 3C 279, during March-April 2014, when the source was observed to be very bright in gamma rays. This huge gamma ray flare was witnessed by Fermi gamma ray telescope and was simultaneously monitored at X-ray energies by Swift-XRT and in UV/optical by Swift-UVOT , SMARTS and Steward observatories, thereby providing an unprecedented multi wavelength data 2015ApJ...803...15P . In Figure fig:3c279obs , we show the observed SED corresponding to the highest gamma ray flux state (2-8 April 2014) encountered during this flaring episode. Earlier studies on the broadband SED of 3C 279 suggests, substantial contribution of synchrotron, SSC and EC processes and this further assures that this source can be a right choice for testing the proposed spectral fitting algorithm 2012MNRAS.419.1660S,2001ApJ...553..683H .

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 279 as the modeled AGN source, fitting its SED and estimating IceCube neutrino rates under a time-dependent lepto-hadronic model.

quotes.quote: The starting point for the parameter scan is an approximate fit to the SED of 3C 279 . The SED is presented in Fig fig:base_sed and the fit parameters are shown in Table tab:parameters . The fit was done ``by eye'' and only aims to qualitatively model the distinct features of the SED, namely the flux scaling, spectral indices and energy cutoffs.

For the fit parameters of 3C 279 ( B= 5 G , N_ inj,p = 1E46 s^ 1 , N_ inj,el = 5E44 s^ 1 ) a rate of approximately e-8 s^ 1 is computed, equaling one neutrino per three years as might be expected from an extremely bright source like 3C 279 .

It is important to note that, in contrast to the K factor, values stay several orders of magnitude below unity which, in the case of 3C 279 , can be attributed to the proton synchrotron emission dominating the Fermi range.

sources.summary: The review discusses 3C 279 as an FSRQ with a 2018 orphan VHE flare delayed from a GeV flare and lacking renewed optical or X-ray activity, challenging single-zone models.

quotes.quote: A remarkable and unusual case of an orphan flare was detected by H.E.S.S. from the FSRQ 3C 279 on January 27 -- 28, 2018 JPL2018 . H.E.S.S. observations had been triggered by a Fermi LAT detected GeV ray flare of 3C 279 around January 16, 2018. While H.E.S.S. did not detect the source around the time of the Fermi LAT flare, a significant detecton ( 11 ) resulted with a delay of 11 days with respect to the GeV flare (see Fig. 3C279orphan ). Also the optical (e.g., from the Steward Observatory Blazar Monitoring Program http://james.as.arizona.edu/ psmith/Fermi/ ) and X-ray (e.g., Swift XRT https://www.swift.psu.edu/monitoring/ ) light curves showed no renewed activity at the time of the H.E.S.S. VHE flare detection. Such orphan flares appear to strongly argue against simple single-zone emission models, and possible alternatives will be discussed in Section theo_variability .

sources.summary: 3C 279 is described as an FSRQ/blazar with superluminal VLBI motions, broad electromagnetic variability, high polarization, historical flares, and very-high-energy detection.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 is the first AGN for which superluminal motions were measured with VLBI techniques whi71,coh71 , and also the first to be detected as a HE emitter by the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory (CGRO) har92 . It is one of the seven flat spectrum radio quasars (FSRQ) detected at very high energy (VHE) with the Cherenkov ray observatories: High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.), Major Atmospheric Gamma Imaging Cherenkov Telescopes (MAGIC), and Very Energetic Radiation Imaging Telescope Array(VERITAS) alb08,mar10,mir14,abe15,cer17,mir17 . 3C 279 presents variable emission throughout the entire electromagnetic spectrum on several timescales, showing some periods of low activity and others in which intense flares are observed mar94,weh98,har01,lar08,cha08,col10,abd10,ale11,hay12,ran17,pat18 . It is also a highly polarized blazar, being one of the first radio sources for which optical polarization was measured kin67 .The polarization is variable on timescales that range from hours and03 to several months e.g., abd10,hay12,hay15,kie16,jer16 , and the polarization degree ( PD ) varies from values smaller than 1 to values as high as 45 . A historical optical light curve of 3C 279, starting in 1927, was compiled by web90 , who noticed a similarity in the flaring activity at two epochs separated by 50 years; fan99 , using 27 years of observations in the near infrared, found evidence of a 7-year periodicity in the light curve.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 279 as a well-studied FSRQ with EGRET and MAGIC detections, broad-band flares, multi-band QPO reports, and a possible binary SMBH engine.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 3C 279 ( =12^ h 56^ m 11 17, =- 05 47 21 53, J2000 epoch), located at z=0.536 Burbidge65 and with a BH mass of 2.7 10^ 8 M_ Woo02 , is one of the well monitored and studied FSRQs at multiwavelength Collmar10,Hayashida15,Alvarez18 . It is one of the first blazars discovered in rays by EGRET Hartman92 , and subsequently in VHE by the Major Atmospheric Gamma-ray Imaging Cherenkov (MAGIC) telescope MAGIC08 . Several outbursts were monitored in broad-band spectrum, i.e. ray flares in 2013 Asano15 , 2014 Paliya15 , and 2015 Ackermann16 . Sandrinelli16 found QPOs with peaks at 24 and 39 days in 6-years-long Fermi LAT data, as well as several peaks in near-IR and optical LCs. Interestingly, Qian19 reported a possible binary SMBH system as an engine of 3C 279, examining 31 superluminal components in the jets.

sources.summary: The paper reports optical circular-polarization upper limits for 3C 279 during a high linear-polarization state, using the non-detection to constrain magnetic-field strength and jet composition.

quotes.quote: We report on optical circular polarization observations of two famous blazars, namely 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089, at high linearly polarized states. This is the first time PKS 1510 089 is observed in optical circular polarization. While only upper limits can be extracted from our observing campaign, the non-detection of optical circular polarization allows us to provide meaningful constraints on their magnetic field strength and jet composition.

In this letter, we present optical CP observations of two well-studied, and highly polarized blazars namely 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089. Assuming an intrinsic CP signal exists, we use the detected upper limits in combination with their linear polarization degree to constrain their magnetic field strength and jet composition. 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089 are famous for their bright outbursts, high variable emission, extreme polarization behavior as well as potentially multimessenger emission Marscher2010,Abdo2010-III,Fermi2016,Kreter2020,Blinov2021 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 279 as a bright, variable FSRQ, analyzing its gamma-ray flares and finding no VERITAS VHE activity despite strong Fermi-LAT flares.

quotes.quote: 3C 279, at z=0.536 Lynds1965 , is one of the most well-studied blazars. It is among the brightest and most variable extragalactic objects in the gamma-ray sky, giving rise to one of the first large amplitude gamma-ray flares measured by EGRET in 1996 Wehrle_1998 . In recent times, it underwent multiple bright gamma-ray flares in 2014, 2015, and 2018. Notably, during a flare beginning on June 16, 2015, it was detected by High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.), and Fermi LAT observed minute-scale variability Romoli2017, Ackermann2016 .

No VHE gamma-ray activity was observed during multiple flares of 3C 279, which is the brightest of the three sources as observed with Fermi LAT, but VERITAS detected flares of both PKS 1222+216 and Ton 599.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 279 shows a possible 4.2-year gamma-ray QPO locally, but it is not significant over red-noise simulations; optical, polarized, and radio searches give the same result.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 The preliminary analysis shows a possible QPO of 4.2 years in the HE ray emission of 3C 279 over a local significance of 2 with both employed methods. While this period is reflected in the LS periodogram and the WWZ as a power peak, the quasi-periodicity analysis is not able to significantly detect it over the modeled red noise with the artificial LCs. In order to check this period in the rest of the bands included in this work, we have carried out the quasi-periodicity search in the optical total and polarized light and the radio emission of 3C 279, with the same results as the ray LC, as presented in Figure 3c279_results . This source has been claimed in the past to show a quasi-periodic behaviour in the optical band, with periods of 256 days and 931 days and significances <3 by . However, the results reported by these authors do not consider the correction of the local significance introduced by the trial factors.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 279 as a modeled quasar/blazar case, adopting its synchrotron-core photon field and comparing predicted neutrino-related gamma-ray flux with Fermi LAT observations.

quotes.quote: For our calculations we use parameters of a well known quasar 3C 279, for which a detailed broadband spectral modelling is available. This source was associated with a high-energy neutrino event detected by IceCube on 2015-09-26 . For the target photon spectrum, we use the 3C 279 synchrotron SC spectrum model from Ref. (Fig. 7 of that work, low state), presented here in Fig. fig:target .

The estimated GeV-band flux of the gamma rays related to the neutrino production in our model is of order 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , to be compared with the Fermi LAT observation of 4 10^ 8 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 flux from 3C 279. This is in line with the lack of direct correlation between neutrino and Fermi-LAT luminosities of blazars, observed e.g. \ in .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 279 as a powerful blazar whose proton-synchrotron parameter space is small and requires an extreme magnetic field when high Doppler factors are considered.

quotes.quote: Here we study two powerful blazars, which are S5 0716+714 and 3C 279. Compared to the blazars studied in Case 1 and Case 2 , they have higher peak luminosities and lower peak energies. It can be found in Figs. S5 and 279-02G that the super-Eddington jet power is generally needed if applying the proton synchrotron radiation to explain their high-energy humps. Only when setting R=1 10^ 14 cm , 3C 279 can have a small parameter space. However if considering _ D 30 , it is necessary to introduce an extreme magnetic field exceeding 10^3 G, which is obviously contrary to the observation.

sources.summary: The paper compares 3C 279 photon-index estimates under different EBL assumptions, finding a hard median value closer to the high-EBL case and noting high-energy spectral flattening.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 (MAGIC/2008) : present two ``extreme'' values for the intrinsic photon index, of = 2.9 0.9 (stat) 0.5 (syst) (low EBL, ) and = 0.5 1.2 (stat) 0.5 (syst) (high EBL, ). Our result, of = 0.94^ +0.96 _ 0.66 , is in between both, although closer to the high EBL case. Overall, the uncertainties are large, do not discarding values compatible to 1.5 . Notably, D11 presents a much higher photon index for 3C 279, of = 3.78^ +0.10 _ 0.08 0.88 . In any case, a ``flattening'' of the spectrum in the higher energy bins is identified, as we can see in the bottom-right panel of Figure fig:spec_fit_PL_6 of Appendix appendix:spec_fit .

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 279 as a bright FSRQ/blazar whose observed variability, redshift, and 1996 flare spectrum set physical parameters for neutrino calculations.

quotes.quote: The measured variability time scale t_ var and synchrotron ( L_s ) and Compton ( L_C ) luminosities determined from the spectral energy distribution of the bright, well-studied blazar 3C 279 implies physical parameters of its jet emission region. Its redshift z = 0.538 , which implies a luminosity distance d_ L 1.05 10^ 28 cm for an _m = 0.3 , _ = 0.7 cosmology with a Hubble constant of 65 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 .

For calculations of n^ _s( ) from 3C 279, we approximate the flux density F( ) ^ observed during the flare of 1996 in the form of a continuous broken power-law function, with indices _ 1 0.5 , _ 2 1.45 , and _ 3 0.6 at frequencies _0 < _1= _ pk , with _ pk 10^ 13 _ 13 Hz , _1 _2 =10^ 16 Hz , and _2 , respectively. The F_ synchrotron radiation flux f_s( ) reaches a maximum value f_s( _ pk ) 1.7 10^ 10 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 at = _ pk or = _ pk . The flaring and three-week average flux of the Compton ray component peaks at 500 MeV and is 20 and 5 10 times larger than f_s( _ pk ) at the corresponding times .

sources.summary: The paper reports 147 GHz transatlantic VLBI detections of 3C 279, treats it as one of two analyzed quasars with zero-consistent closure phases, and derives a 27 microarcsecond lower source-size limit.

quotes.quote: We note that we have detected 3 sources (NRAO150, 3C279, 1633+382) on the transatlantic baselines from Arizona (HHT, KP) to Pico Veleta at a fringe spacing of 4.2G (corresponding to 24 as resolution). This is, as far as we know, the highest angular resolution, ever achieved in astronomical radio interferometry.

So far we analyzed only the data for the two sources, which are detected with SNR > 7 on all of the 3 baselines (3C 279, 1633+382) and for which closure phases could be measured (see Fig. 2). For both sources the closure phases are consistent with zero, indicating either a point-like, or a symmetrical source structure (within the limitations given by the uv-coverage).

With the usual assumption of the brightness temperature being limited by the inverse Compton effect ( T_B 10^ 12 K), an interesting lower limit to the source size of 17 as for 1633+382 and 27 as for 3C 279 is obtained (from _ mas 1.22 S_ Jy / _ GHz ^2 ^ 0.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 279 as a quasar whose circular and linear polarization motivated Faraday-conversion jet models and constraints on plasma composition.

quotes.quote: Quasar 3C279 war98 (1998) reported the discovery of circular and linear polarization in 3C 279 and attributed CP to internal Faraday conversion. Typical fractional linear and circular polarizations in 3C 279 are of order 10 and >1 , respectively. They concluded that if the jet is composed of normal plasma, then the low-energy cut-off of the energy distribution of relativistic electrons must be as high as _ i 100 in order to avoid Faraday depolarization and overproduction of the jet kinetic power. They were unable to fit their polarization models to the observational data for _ i >20 and thus claimed that the jet must be pair-dominated. However, the above observational constraints on CP and LP and the jet energetics can be satisfied for a variety of microscopic plasma parameters.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 279 as a prototype FSRQ blazar, modeling hadronic neutrino and gamma-ray production, persistent gamma-ray flux, and ultra-high-energy neutral-beam power in its jet.

quotes.quote: Using the parameters of the non-thermal flares detected from the FSRQ blazar 3C279 and from the BL Lac object Mkn 501 as prototypes, the fluxes of high-energy neutrinos and gamma-rays (of hadronic origin) are calculated assuming that the power to accelerate relativistic protons is equal to the power injected into relativistic electrons needed to explain the non-thermal flares detected by EGRET and BeppoSax . Fig. 1a ( left panel ) shows the spectra of neutrons escaping the blob (heavy solid curve) and then the BLR (dashed curve) for 3C279, calculated for the variability time t_ var = 1 d and the jet's Doppler-factor = 10 , which defines the blob size R t_ var /(1+z) .

We note, however, that the neutrinos can be expected not only during the flares, but also in periods between the active flares, insofar as the EGRET observations show a persistent average level of gamma-ray fluxes from 3C 279 at the level of 10 of the flare flux, implying persistent electron/proton acceleration. Since the external photon target is always available, over the course of one year several neutrinos should be detected from FSRQs such as 3C 279 with km-scale neutrino detectors.
Thus, for the jets in 3C 279 this would correspond to a mean power for UHE neutral beam production 10^ 45 erg/s .

sources.summary: The paper detects strong positive 15 GHz circular polarization only at the 3C 279 core, consistent with prior 15 GHz epochs but contrasting with a later lower-frequency sign change.

quotes.quote: 1253 055 (3C 279): Figure f:3c279cp shows the first 5 milli-arcseconds of the jet of 3C 279 at 15 GHz in November of 2002. The top panel is Stokes I, the middle panel is linear polarization, and the bottom panel is circular polarization. Positions where circular polarization measurements or limits were taken are marked and numbered on the Stokes I map. We detect strong circular polarization of m_c=+0.30 0.08 only on the jet core. The sign and magnitude of the circular polarization is consistent with five epochs of CP detections by HW99 at 15 GHz during 1996.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 279 as a blazar/AGN jet source, fitting its multiwavelength variability with shock-in-jet models and comparing inferred jet properties across black-hole mass scales.

quotes.quote: We discuss differences and similarities between jets powered by super-massive black holes in quasars and by stellar-mass black holes in microquasars. The comparison is based on multi-wavelength radio-to-infrared observations of the two active galactic nuclei 3C 273 and 3C 279, as well as the two galactic binaries GRS 1915+105 and Cyg X-3. The physical properties of the jet are derived by fitting the parameters of a shock-in-jet model simultaneously to all available observations.

Until now, we studied data sets of four objects: the quasar 3C 273 (T urler \ ; ), the blazar 3C 279 (Lindfors \ ) and the microquasars GRS 1915+105 (T urler \ ) and Cyg X-3 (Lindfors \ in prep.). In this short contribution, we try to compare and discuss the jet properties derived for these four sources as one of the first attempts to sketch out a more global picture of black hole jets across the range of mass.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1253-055 as 3C279, an OVV and superluminal radio prototype with infrared outbursts and complex color-magnitude behavior.

quotes.quote: 1253-055, 3C279 , z=0.536 3C279 is a well known member of OVVs, a large optical variation of B 6.70 mag (Eachus Liller 1975) and a highly optical polarization of P_ opt =44 ( see Fan et al. 1996) and a possible variability period of 7-year (Fan 1999) are reported. It is the prototype of superluminal radio sources and shows a violently optical brightness increase of 2.0 mag during an interval of 24 hours (Webb et al. 1990). The infrared light curves show 3 outbursts. The (J-K) is strongly associated with (J-H) and (H-K). There is a positive correlation between color index and magnitude ( see Fig. 9).

sources.summary: The paper discusses two multiwavelength campaigns on 3C 279, finding that large optical changes were accompanied by little or negligible hard-X-ray variability, leaving theoretical implications unresolved.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 (z=0.536) This is another well known blazar and was the target of two multiwavelength campaigns: one in 2003 and the other in 2006 (PI Collmar in both cases) . While the first campaign was planned independently from the status of the source, the second one was triggered by high optical flux observed by ground telescope at the beginning of January 2006 (see also ). By comparing the SED obtained from the two campaigns, the most striking feature is that, despite a strong change in optical fluxes (about one order of magnitude), little or negligible changes occurred in hard X-rays . The theoretical implications are still to be understood.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C279 as a photon-rich active nucleus whose primary TeV photons are unlikely to escape, making its observations consistent with secondary photon production.

quotes.quote: For example, the intrinsic photon densities around M87 and 3C279 can be inferred from observations, and these densities appear inconsistent with the unattenuated emission of VHE photons 2008AIPC.1085..644C,2008ApJ...685L..23A .

The photon-rich environments of M87 and 3C279 are not likely to allow the primary TeV photons to come out  2008AIPC.1085..644C . However, the cosmic rays are not attenuated significantly by the same backgrounds. The observations of M87 and 3C279 are consistent with the secondary photon interpretation.

sources.summary: The catalogue describes 3C 279 as an optically active blazar that was barely detected in 2003 but later brightened by nearly a factor of ten in OMC V-band data.

quotes.quote: The AGN 3C 279 was barely detectable around June 2003, with a 2 OMC detection at V = 17.0 . The catalogued value for it in the low state is V = 17.8 mag ( ). After May 2005, this blazar was clearly detected with a brightness in the range V = 14.8-15.8 mag, indicating that it brightened by up to a factor close to 10 (Fig.  fig:3C279-OMC ). This is a very active source, with optical photometry reported in the range B = 18.3 to B = 11.3 ( ).

sources.summary: MAGIC detected the FSRQ 3C 279 at very high energies, describing it as a bright, distant blazar whose redshift makes it useful for EBL studies.

quotes.quote: The FSRQ 3C 279 is one of the brightest objects at all wavelengths and was the first blazar to be discovered at rays . It is also the first FSRQ found at VHE rays and, given its redshift of z=0.536 , the most distant VHE ray emitter detected so far. Due to its large redshift 3C 279 is an important object to study the EBL photon field. The Magic observations which lead to the detection at 5.8 were performed in spring 2006.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C279 as a z=0.536 blazar detected by MAGIC around 0.5 TeV; its observed spectrum and similar low/high-energy signal support unexpectedly transparent VHE gamma-ray propagation.

quotes.quote: Early indications by H.E.S.S. and the subsequent detection of blazar 3C279 by MAGIC show that the Universe is more transparent to very-high-energy gamma rays than previously thought.
A quantitative estimate of this effect indeed explains the observed spectrum of 3C279.

Stronger evidence comes from the observation of blazar 3C279 ( z = 0.536 ) at E 0.5 TeV by the MAGIC collaboration . In particular, the signal from 3C279 collected by MAGIC in the region E<220 GeV has more or less the same statistical significance as the one in the range 220 GeV < E < 600 GeV ( 6.1 in the former case, 5.1 in the latter) See ref. for a different view. .

sources.summary: The paper measures two 1-2 GHz RM components toward 3C 279 and argues their similarity to 8 GHz VLBA values may trace a similar region despite intrinsic variability.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 Our observations of 3C 279 (J125611--054720) find two RM components at 15 and 66 rad m ^ 2 with polarized brightnesses of 270 and 160 mJy ( 2.7 and 1.6 of total intensity). The 1--2 GHz RM is similar to the that seen at 8 GHz with the VLBA in June 2000 z03 , which is much less than that seen at earlier dates z01 . The observations of z03 show two sources at milliarcsecond-resolution with RM of 91 and 13 rad m ^ 2 and polarized brightnesses of about 1.4 Jy ( 9 ). Intrinsic variability makes it hard to make a strong conclusion, but this similarity suggests that a similar part of 3C 279 is seen from 1 to 8 GHz.

sources.summary: 3C 279 is described as the farthest VHE source then detected; MAGIC observations constrained EBL density and showed the same compact-emission-region problem seen in another quasar.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 (z=0.536) is the farthest VHE source detected so far. It was discovered by MAGIC at VHE in 2006 and allowed to place very strong constraints on the density of EBL, which have only now been confirmed by the detection of a second quasar.

Recent MAGIC observations of 3C 279 show the same apparent contradiction. Some explanations have already been proposed in order to solve it, invoking the presence of very compact emission regions embedded within the large scale jet or the possibility of a very strong jet recollimation (see for more details).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C279 as the flat-spectrum radio quasar in the seven-source optically thick sample, contributing comparably to the excess with a 3.8-sigma leave-one-out relevance.

quotes.quote: The seven sources with measurements extending to >2 contribute about equally to the overall excess. Removing each source spectrum and re-calculating the significance given in the following list provides an estimate on the relevance of each source: Mkn 421 ( S( Mkn 421 )=3.8 ), Mkn 501 ( S( Mkn 501 )=3.7 ), H 1426+428 ( S( H 1426+428 )=3.5 ), 1ES1101-232 ( S( 1ES1101-232 )=2.8 ), 1ES0347-121 ( S( 1ES0347-121 )=4.6 ), 1ES0414+009 ( S( 1ES0414+009 )=3.6 ), 3C279 ( S( 3C279 )=3.8 ). The result demonstrates that the different objects (6 high frequency peaked Blazars and 1 flat spectrum radio quasar) at widely different redshifts ( z=0.031 , 0.034 , 0.129 , 0.186 , 0.188 , 0.287 , and 0.536 ) contribute about equally to the overall significance. A systematic effect based upon the type of source or its distance is not evident.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 279 as one of two TeV FSRQs, using literature SEDs, BLR energy densities, and a synchrotron/SSC/external-Compton model to fit its emission.

quotes.quote: Twelve blazars are included in our sample, two FSRQs (3C 279 and PKS 1510-089) and ten BL Lacs (W Com, Mkn 421, Mkn 501, PKS 2155-304, 1ES 1101-232, BL Lacertae, 1ES 2344+514, 1ES 1959+650, PKS 2005-489, S5 0716+714). The observation data of the broadband SEDs for 3C 279 and PKS 1510-089 are from literature . The radiation from a BLR is assumed to be a blackbody spectrum and the corresponding energy densities seen in the comoving frame are U^ ' _ BLR =5.37 10^ 3 ^ 2 erg cm ^ 3 and U^ ' _ BLR =4.98 10^ 3 ^ 2 erg cm ^ 3 for 3C 279 and PKS 1510-089, respectively, where we take = . Thus, the syn+SSC+EC/BLR model is used to fit the observed SEDs of the two FSRQs and the model can explain the observed SEDs well as shown in Fig. 1.

sources.summary: The review highlights 3C 279 as a flaring VHE-detected FSRQ/blazar, detected by MAGIC in 2006 and then the highest-redshift VHE AGN source.

quotes.quote: So far only few (e.g., 3C 279 whose thermal components may be overwhelmed by a strong non-thermal flux in a bright state pian99 ; or the BL Lac prototype, BL Lacertae, which shows occasionally broad lines e.g., capetti10 ) of such objects have been detected at VHEs when they reached an elevated flaring state albert08,brm09,albert07b . E.g., while monitoring 3C 279 in 2006 in a dedicated multifrequency campaign see Fig.  3C279 b07 this source transitioned to an overall high state observed in the optical as well as at X-rays. During this state, in February 2006, a VHE signal from 3C 279 was detected by the MAGIC telescope albert08 .
The so far highest-redshift source detected at VHEs is 3C 279 at z=0.537 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 279 as one of the brightest and most variable TeV blazars, splitting its Fermi data into flux states and noting dramatic GeV flares.

quotes.quote: Data from the two brightest blazars (Markarian 421 and 3C 279) were split into three subsets, with low, medium and high-flux states.

The largest flux increase is seen in the case of the two FSRQs 3C 279 and PKS 1222+21. As depicted by their respective light curves in Figure fig:lc , these two objects have undergone dramatic GeV flares. Such a large scale flux increase does not hold for the remainder of the blazars.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 279 gamma-ray PSD slopes consistent with NM13 errors but discrepant from soft-X-ray results, where no high-frequency PSD break was detected.

quotes.quote: We found that in PKS 0454-234, 3C 279, 3C 66A, and PKS 2155-304 the two methods are consistent with each other in terms of the derived PSD slopes within the provided error bars. Soft X-ray blazar PSD (here below 20 keV) were computed for three BL Lac sources (Mkn 421 and PKS 2155-304 also included in our Fermi/LAT sample, and Mkn 501) by Kataoka et al. (2001), and for FSRQ sources by McHardy (2008; 3C 273), Chatterjee et al. (2008; 3C 279). Characteristic time scales corresponding to the high-frequency PSD break were detected in all of these sources with the exception of 3C 279. The PSD slope derived here for 3C 279, 1.1 , is in disagreement with that reported in the soft X-rays by Chatterjee et al. (2008, 2.3 ).

sources.summary: The paper notes that 3C 279 had the brightest gamma-ray flare in its band on 2014 April 03, but found no significant optical or millimetre cross-correlations.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 : The ray flare that occurred on 2014 April 03 was the brightest event observed at this band Hayashida2015 . Despite the coincidence of the ray events in 3C 279 to the optical variations, no significant correlation was obtained. Neither did we obtain a significant correlation between other wavebands. The millimetre light curves needed to be detrended prior to the correlations due to the decreasing trend from the decay of the major flare that occurred toward the middle of 2012.

sources.summary: The paper generates a flaring SED for 3C 279 and finds it more luminous than the NLSy1 galaxies, with similar SED shape and Compton dominance characteristic of FSRQs.

quotes.quote: We, therefore, generate SED of the FSRQ 3C 279 covering its recent GeV outburst in 2015 June 2015ApJ...808L..48P and plot it along with other NLSy1 galaxies in Figure fig:SED_comp . As can be seen, 3C 279 is more luminous than any NLSy1 galaxy, especially at ray energies. The overall shapes of the SEDs of 3C 279 and NLSy1 galaxies are similar, thus suggesting similar mechanisms to be working for the observed flaring behaviors, with more extreme parameters for 3C 279. Finally, the Compton dominance (the ratio of the inverse-Compton to synchrotron peak luminosities) of all the GeV flaring NLSy1 galaxies is found to be greater than unity, a feature generally exhibited by FSRQs like 3C 279.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 279 as the first FSRQ-class VHE emitter and reports VERITAS follow-up during a multiwavelength flare with an upper limit similar to MAGIC.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 (FSRQ, z=0.536 ) This quasar is the first of its class detected as a VHE emitter 3C279Magic . \ observations during 2011 were triggered by flaring activity observed at lower wavelengths (optical, X-rays and HE rays). The same flare triggered observations with the \ telescopes 3C279Magicbis , which resulted as well in no VHE detection (flux upper limit equal to 1.7 Crab above 260 GeV). The \ flux upper limit ( 2.1 Crab above 260 GeV) is similar to the one measured with the \ telescopes.

sources.summary: The review describes 3C 279 as a quasar with optical polarization rotations tied to gamma-ray flares and discusses helical-field and magnetic-dissipation interpretations of its EVPA swing.

quotes.quote: A further connection between the optical polarization and gamma-ray flares was suggested in two quasars 3C 279 ( ) and PKS 1510-089 ( ). In both of these the optical polarization angle was seen to rotate by more than 180 degrees over a course of 20 to 50 days during which a sharp gamma-ray flare was observed.

This model was used to successfully fit the EVPA rotation in the quasar 3C 279 ( ), although one should bear in mind the caveat that with the additional data from , the rotation was not as long as originally presented by .

stated that the EVPA swing in the 2009 flare of 3C 279 was reproduced by a model that ``favors magnetic energy dissipation process during the flare'' because the magnetic field strength was seen to decrease when the total intensity increased.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 279 as a well-studied FSRQ/LSP whose 2009 and 2011 XMM-Newton spectra show no significant curvature and power-law photon indices of 1.77--1.80.

quotes.quote: 3C 279: This blazar is very well studied FSRQ and is classified as an LSP (Ackermann et al. 2011). In previous studies by Donato et al. (2005), its spectra were fitted by power law and broken power law and break energy is found at low energy ( 0.5--0.7) keV. Wierzcholska Wagner (2016) studied the source and found it to be well described by the log parabola model with positive curvature. We analyzed the spectra of 3C 279 in two occasions in 2009 and 2011 using XMM Newton. In both the pointings, we did not find any significant curvature and F-test gave reasonable fit using power law model. The photon indices are varying between 1.77--1.80 which are in accordance with previous studies.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 279 as a monitored UMRAO program source with circular-polarization reversals and frequency-dependent signs, contrasting its behavior with 3C 84.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 (right) has shown polarity reversals at 8 and 4.8 GHz and frequency-dependent differences in sign at a common epoch. With the exception of 3C 84, there is no clear correlation between the amplitude of the variability in fractional circular polarization and that in total flux or fractional linear polarization. The polarity exhibits a variety of behavior patterns. OV-236 is the only UMRAO program source which exhibits a constant polarity over decades of measurements during a time window with several major outbursts.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 279 as a luminous FSRQ/blazar with broad emission lines, strong gamma-ray variability, jet structure, and a June 2015 AGILE-triggered multifrequency flare campaign.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 is associated with a luminous FSRQ at z = 0.536  1965ApJ...142.1667L with prominent broad emission lines detected in all accessible spectral bands, and revealing highly variable emission. It consistently shows strong ray emission, already clearly detected by EGRET 1992ApJ...385L...1H,Kniffen1993 , AGILE 2009A&A...494..509G , Fermi-LAT Hayashida2012,2015ApJ...807...79H , and also detected above 100 GeV by MAGIC Albert2008 . The central black hole mass estimates are in the range of (3 - 8) 10^8 M_   2001MNRAS.327.1111G,2002ApJ...579..530W,2009A&A...505..601N . The 3C279 jet features strings of compact plasmoids as indicated by radio observations Hovatta2009 , which may be a by-product of the magnetic reconnection process Petropoulou2016 , even though it must be taken into account that the superluminal knots observed in Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) images are probably much larger structures than reconnection plasmoids formed on kinetic plasma scales, hence this connection is uncertain 2008ApJ...689...79C .

sources.summary: The review discusses the 2015 flare of 3C279, noting its historical GeV maximum, two-minute Fermi variability, lack of lower-band changes, and tension with conventional leptonic models.

quotes.quote: Among these, the flare of 3C279 in 2015 was recorded as a historical maximum flux in the GeV energy range for the source. With dedicated observation, Fermi LAT measured an intrinsic variability timescale of 2 minutes. Unlike the large variability observed in the gamma-ray energy range, no significant flux changes were measured in the lower energy bands where the synchrotron energy peak was measured in a broadband SED. These observations challenge the conventional leptonic model.

sources.summary: The paper finds minute-scale gamma-ray variability for 3C 279, treats it as a known very-high-energy FSRQ, and reports radio-core distance constraints.

quotes.quote: Consecutively zooming in on the brightest flares, which we identify in an objective way through Bayesian blocks and a hill-climbing algorithm, we find variability on subhour time scales and as short as minutes for two sources in our sample (3C 279, CTA 102) and weak evidence for variability at time scales less than the Fermi satellite's orbit of 95 minutes for PKS 1510-089 and 3C 454.3. This suggests extremely compact emission regions in the jet. We do not find any signs for ray absorption in the broad-line region (BLR), which indicates that rays are produced at distances greater than hundreds of gravitational radii from the central black hole. This is further supported by a cross-correlation analysis between ray and radio/millimeter light curves, which is consistent with ray production at the same location as the millimeter core for 3C 273, CTA 102, and 3C 454.3. The inferred locations of the ray production zones are still consistent with the observed decay times of the brightest flares if the decay is caused by external Compton scattering with BLR photons. However, the minute-scale variability is challenging to explain in such scenarios.

As noted in the Introduction, two of the sources (3C 279 and CTA 102) have already been shown to be variable on extremely short timescales TheFermi-LAT:2016dss,2018ApJ...854L..26S . Here we will introduce a novel objective method to identify a large set of flares in order to conduct a comprehensive search for the short variability in our source sample. Furthermore, PKS B1222+216 2011ApJ...730L...8A , 3C 279 2008Sci...320.1752M , and PKS 1510-089 2011ApJ...730L...8A,2013A&A...554A.107H,2014A&A...569A..46A are among the seven FSRQs also detected above 100 GeV with imaging air Cerenkov Telescopes. For 3C 454.3, the MAGIC and VERITAS telescopes only obtained upper limits on the flux during flaring states in 2007 2009A&A...498...83A and 2010 2016AJ....151..142A .

The following distances d_ core, 15GHz were determined under the assumption that k_r = 1 : for PKS B1222+216 d_ core, 15GHz = 23.41 pc; for 3C 279 d_ core, 15GHz <7.88 pc; for PKS 1510-089 d_ core, 15GHz = 17.71 pc; for CTA 102 d_ core, 15GHz =46.7 pc; and for 3C 454.3 d_ core, 15GHz = 20.36 pc. Dedicated analyses have also been carried out and found for 3C 454.3 k_r = 0.6 0.8 and d_ core, 15GHz 38 pc, and, since d_ core , ^ 1/k_r , d_ core, 43GHz 9 pc 2014MNRAS.437.3396K . For 3C 273 2013ARep...57...34V found k_r = 1.4 and d_ core , = 134 ^ 1/1.4 using radio observations at frequencies between 4.8 and 362 GHz. Lastly, 2015A&A...576A..43F conducted VLBA observations of CTA 102 ranging from 5 to 86 GHz and found k_r = 1.0 as a best-fit value and d_ core, 86GHz 7 pc.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 279 as a bright flat-spectrum radio quasar, using long 22 and 37 GHz light curves to test multifractal behaviour and ARIMA fits.

quotes.quote: The HPQ quasar 3C 279 2011A&A...532A.146L at z = 0.536 1996MNRAS.279..429N has been observed by Mets a hovi Radio Observatory at 22 and 37 GHz for 24 and 39 years, respectively. The flat spectrum radio quasar 3C 279 has been known as one of the brightest quasars at all wavelengths, and its multiwavelength behavior and jet structure have been studied by different authors (e.g. 2016MNRAS.457.3535Z, 2008ApJ...689...79C, 2001ApJ...558..583H ).

In this work, we apply a wavelet transform-based multifractality analysis approach called wavelet transform modulus maxima (hereinafter WTMM) and study the multiscaling or multifractal behavior of the quasars 3C 273, 3C 279, 3C 345, and 3C 454.3 radio observations at 22 and 37 GHz.

Except for 3C 273 and 3C 279 at 37 GHz, where the fit to ARIMA models does not reduce the autocorrelations left behind more than 2 significant autocorrelations outside of the 95 limit, the ARIMA models fit sufficiently to reduce most of the autocorrelations for the rest of the time series.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 3C 279 shows mismatched gamma-ray and 43 GHz variability, no significant interband correlation, differing PSD slopes, and only a tendency toward harder-when-brighter behavior.

quotes.quote: 3C 279. There is a large outburst superimposing several small flares in the radio light curve, which is not simultaneously observed in the ray band, as displayed in Figure LC_279 (a). At the same time, the several big ray flares (e.g., MJD 56740 and MJD 57190) are also not accompanied by the radio flares. It seems like that the totally different variability behaviors are presented in the two energy bands, which is also consistent with the DCF result; no correlation of flux variation over 1 confidence level is observed between the two energy bands. The derived PSD slopes in the two energy bands are also different; that in the radio band ( 1.34 0.17 ) corresponds to the red-noise process while that in the ray band ( 0.80 0.22 ) is not.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 279 lacks optical-UV disk dominance, has an emission region outside the BLR, and favors dusty-torus external-Compton emission with a higher torus parameter.

quotes.quote: We selected the six FSRQs (CTA 102, 3C 273, 3C 279, 3C 454.3, PKS 1510-089 PKS B1222+216) from the Fermi LAT monitored source list, also studied in 2019ApJ...877...39M , which are known for exhibiting brightest ray flares with average daily fluxes over 10^ 5 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 within 1 statistical uncertainties above 100 MeV 2019ApJ...877...39M .
In the case of 3C 279, the optical-UV data did not indicate a dominance of disk thermal emission over synchrotron radiation.

In PKS B1222+216 and 3C 279, the emission regions are further away from BLR which indicate the dominance of EC_ DT over EC_ BLR as non-thermal high energy emission process. _ BLR values for all sources were kept at 10 except for CTA 102 ( _ BLR = 15 ), which is quite reasonable. A bit higher _ DT value was required for 3C 279 to fit the SED.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 279 as a well-studied VLBI AGN whose one-sided jet bends on kiloparsec scales and shows filamentary structure in RadioAstron observations.

quotes.quote: 1253 053: 3C 279 is one of the most well-studied AGN in VLBI science. It has been monitored by MOJAVE at 15 GHz since 1995 , and by the Boston University Blazar Group at 43 GHz since 2007 . On VLBI scales the jet has a well-collimated one-sided structure -- similar to our S-band images--, however on kiloparsec scales it shows a significant bend towards the east cheung02 . RadioAstron observations at 22 GHz reveal a filamentary structure in the jet interpreted as Kelvin--Helmholtz instability fuentes22 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 279 as a variable TeV blazar with minute-scale variability and 2015 and 2018 GeV/VHE flares seen by Fermi LAT and Cherenkov observatories.

quotes.quote: We also searched intra-day flares and only found 4FGL J1256.1 0547 (3C 279) had minute-scale variability in 2018, and this result has been reported in our previous work wggpasp .

J1256.1 0547 (3C 279) has been extensively studied for its variability properties. For its GeV flare in 2015, the H.E.S.S. observation led to a clear detection during the end of the \ flaring state hess2019aa,pit+18 . For its GeV flare in 2018, intense VHE flares were observed over multiple days after the end of the HE flares eme+19 .

sources.summary: The paper presents high-time-resolution optical polarimetry of the blazar 3C279, finding internight polarization changes and faster minute-to-hour flickering during its observed campaign.

quotes.quote: Galaxies: active: individual: 3C279 -- polarization-- galaxies: photometry The blazar 3C279 ( z=0.538 ) is a strong source across the entire electromagnetic spectrum and one of the best studied extragalactic objects.

We have carried out polarization observations with very high time resolution of the OVV blazar 3C279. A large variation of 10 in the degree of linear polarization was observed on internight timescales. More rapid flickering, with timescales from minutes to hours, was also present within each single night.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 279 as a high-luminosity quasar example expected to be neutron-opaque, with EGRET and gamma-ray break estimates constraining neutrino models.

quotes.quote: We shall show that high luminosity AGN (like 3C 279) can be expected to be opaque to neutrons at energies above about 10^8 10^9 , while low luminosity BL Lacs (like Mrk 501) must be transparent to neutrons at all energies.

We apply this relation to two reference AGN: the BL Lac object Mrk 501, and the quasar 3C 279. The combination of the observed TeV spectrum and the EGRET flux limits for Mrk 501 gives rise to our assumption of a break energy at about 0.3 1 TeV in the context of photohadronic models, while for 3C 279 the EGRET spectrum allows for a break at 3 10 .

This still implies a relation like Eq. ( eq.tau_rel ), but with _ n increased relative to _ by a factor of 10 -- consequently, the possible break in the gamma-ray spectrum of 3C 279 would correspond to E_b 10^8 .

sources.summary: AGILE detected a gamma-ray flare from the flat-spectrum radio quasar 3C 279, measured its high-energy flux, and modeled its spectral energy distribution with synchrotron-self-Compton and external-Compton components.

quotes.quote: We report the detection by the AGILE satellite of an intense gamma-ray flare from the gamma-ray source 3EG J1255-0549, associated to the Flat Spectrum Radio Quasar 3C 279, during the AGILE pointings towards the Virgo Region on 2007 July 9-13.

During 2007 July 9-13 July 2007, AGILE-GRID detected gamma-ray emission from 3C 279, with the source at 2 ^ from the center of the Field of View, with an average flux of (210 38) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for energy above 100 MeV.

The Spectral Energy Distribution is modelled with a homogeneous one-zone Synchrotron Self Compton emission plus the contributions by external Compton scattering of direct disk radiation and, to a lesser extent, by external Compton scattering of photons from the Broad Line Region.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 279 as the first VHE-detected FSRQ and a prototype blazar, with intense broad-line emission, a low-energy-peaked SED, and high Compton dominance.

quotes.quote: 3C 279 is the first FSRQ detected at VHE.

At opposite sides in the blazar sequence , the well-known, bright blazars PKS 2155-304 and 3C 279 are prototypes of the two blazar classes of high-energy peaked BL Lacs (HBL) and Flat spectrum radio quasars (FSRQ).

3C 279 instead is a classic FSRQ, characterized by intense BLR emission ( L_ BLR 2.4 10^ 44 erg/s , though FSRQ can reach 10^ 46 erg/s), by a low-energy-peaked SED (with humps located at IR and MeV-GeV frequencies), by the X-ray band dominated by the inverse Compton (IC) emission of low-energy electrons, and by a very high Compton dominance (up to L_ C /L_ S 100 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 279 as a blazar whose MAGIC spectrum constrains optical EBL limits, but says its five data points do not improve the global fit.

quotes.quote: The MAGIC observation of 3C 279 at z=0.536 set upper limit at the optical band .

The UV-optical band can be constrained by sources at high redshift and lower energy, such as the 3C 279 at z=0.536 . However, since only 5 points of 3C 279 was given by MAGIC it has no help to improve the fit because to include it we need to introduce 3 additional source parameters.

sources.summary: The paper monitored 3C 279 for optical flux and colour variability across multiple timescales and reports intraday flux variations with amplitudes up to 9.2 percent.

quotes.quote: To search for optical variability on a wide range of timescales, we have carried out photometric monitoring of 3C 454.3, 3C 279 and S5 0716+714.

The key motivation of this work was to search for optical flux and colour variability of 3C 454.3, S5 0716+714 and 3C 279 on diverse time scales, including analyses of colour-magnitude variations, inter-band cross correlations, and optical/NIR SEDs.

The percentage variation on a given night is calculated by using the variability amplitude parameter A , introduced by Heidt Wagner (1996), During our 37 nights of monitoring for IDV we found flux variations, on intraday time-scales for 21 nights in B, V, R or I with amplitudes of variability up to 31 for 3C 454.3, 9.2 for 3C 279 and 13.5 for S5 0716+714.

sources.summary: The paper notes 3C 279 was reported as a probable radio-bright LBL neutrino emitter, but it was not supported by the 10-year IceCube point-source list.

quotes.quote: Possible correlations between radio sources and IceCube neutrinos have been reported in Ref. , where it is concluded that four radio bright blazars, namely 3C 279, NRAO 530, PKS 1741 4038, and PKS 2145+067, all LBLs, are highly probable high-energy (E > 200 TeV) neutrino emitters. However 3C 279, the brightest and the only one of these blazars that is included in the list of objects used for the point source search in the 10-year IceCube data set, is not listed among the objects considered as likely neutrino emitters 2020PhRvL.124e1103A .
Recently, possible statistical associations with specific LBL sources (3C 279, NRAO 530, PKS 1741 038, and PKS 2145+067) have been reported in Ref. .

sources.summary: The paper resolves the blazar jet into filaments, argues radio features arise from differential Doppler boosting, and infers a clockwise helical magnetic field in a Lorentz-factor 13 jet.

quotes.quote: Here we show microarcsecond-scale angular resolution images of the blazar obtained at 22 GHz with the space VLBI mission , which allowed us to resolve the jet transversely and reveal several filaments produced by plasma instabilities in a kinetically dominated flow. Our high angular resolution and dynamic range image suggests that emission features traveling down the jet may manifest as a result of differential Doppler-boosting within the filaments, as opposed to the standard shock-in-jet model invoked to explain blazar jet radio variability. Moreover, we infer that the filaments in are possibly threaded by a helical magnetic field rotating clockwise, as seen in the direction of the flow motion, with an intrinsic helix pitch angle of 45 ^ in a jet with a Lorentz factor of 13 at the time of observation.

gmw_00000766.name: UGC 12281
gmw_00000766.overview: UGC 12281 is a low-surface-brightness superthin disk galaxy with no obvious bulge [arXiv:1201.2538]. Despite its low surface brightness, the galaxy exhibits high star formation activity across two-thirds of its disk and shows evidence of extraplanar ionized gas [arXiv:1201.2538].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12281 as an unusual low-surface-brightness superthin disk galaxy with significant star formation, extraplanar ionized gas, no obvious bulge, and a candidate interacting dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: UGC 12281 has been classified as having a pure disk and being a low surface brightness galaxy (LSBG), thus being an obvious member of the so-called superthin galaxies. At the same time it represents an extremely untypical type of LSBG due to its remarkable amount of current star formation and evidence for extraplanar ionized gas. This makes it become a perfect tool to investigate the triggering of star formation in LSB galaxies, located in an alleged isolated area.

We investigate here the superthin UGC 12281. This galaxy has an axis ratio of almost 12. However, revealed it as H bright implying a high star formation activity. Indeed they determined a radial extent of strong star formation to 20 kpc which accounts for 2/3 of the total disk. Hints for extraplanar diffuse ionized gas (eDIG) were detected, too.

gmw_00000767.name: NGC 2320
gmw_00000767.overview: NGC 2320 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0001506, arXiv:astro-ph/0508330] that hosts an inner dust disk [arXiv:0811.1381] and an ionized gas disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0001506]. Its molecular gas and dust exhibit asymmetric structures [arXiv:0811.1381, arXiv:astro-ph/0508330], while its symmetric 24-micron emission may be driven by central star formation or active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:0803.4510].

sources.summary: NGC 2320 is described as a gas-rich elliptical with a compact 24 micron component, a dust disk, asymmetric CO gas, and uncertain star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2320 young05 showed that the V morphology of NGC 2320 is well described by an ^ 1/4 profile out to a semimajor axis of at least 90 , which bolsters its classification as an elliptical even though it has quite a large molecular gas mass of 4 9 . The V-R image of Figure n2320 clearly shows the inner dust disk to be 20 \ in diameter, with somewhat enhanced reddening 10 \ southeast of the nucleus. The unsharp-masked image also shows a bright ring of 35 \ diameter. Molecular gas in NGC 2320 is distributed rather like the dust in the V-R image and it also shows a ``tail'' of gas to the southeast of the nucleus.

Unlike the CO distribution in NGC 2320, the 24 \ emission is notably symmetric. It is well described by a ^ 1/4 model whose half-light ellipse has semimajor and semiminor axes 4 \ 2 \ (1.6 0.8 kpc; Table fittable ).

The difference in scale sizes implies that the 24 \ emission from NGC 2320 is not primarily circumstellar dust around evolved stars, unless there is a strong age gradient in the stellar population such that the inner arcseconds host a larger proportion of intermediate-age stars that are relatively bright at 24 . It is possible that the 24 \ emission in NGC 2320 is attributable to star formation activity that is restricted to the central portions of the molecular disk.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2320 using stellar kinematics and an ionized gas disk, finding similar mass-follows-light and logarithmic-potential fits and radially anisotropic equatorial stellar orbits.

quotes.quote: We present direct observational constraints on the orbital distribution of the stars in the giant elliptical NGC 2320. Long-slit spectra along multiple position angles are used to derive the stellar line--of--sight velocity distribution within one effective radius. In addition, the rotation curve and dispersion profile of an ionized gas disk are measured from the [ OIII ] emission lines. After correcting for the asymmetric drift, we derive the circular velocity of the gas, which provides an independent constraint on the gravitational potential.

Models with radially constant _V and logarithmic models with dark matter provide comparably good fits to the data and possess similar dynamical structure. Across the full range of _V permitted by the observational constraints, the models are radially anisotropic in the equatorial plane over the radial range of our kinematical data ( 1'' 40'' ). Along the true minor axis, they are more nearly isotropic.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2320 as the uncertain case in its CO-rich early-type sample, with 24 micron emission that may reflect AGN activity or modest inner-disk star formation.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 2320 it is not at all clear whether the 24 \ emission is driven by star formation. The galaxy's radio continuum emission is more likely due to AGN activity than to star formation (Young 2005). The 24 \ emission is well fit by a smooth ^ 1/4 model with half-light semimajor and semiminor axes of 4 2 , but this is five times smaller than the effective radius of the stellar distribution. This modestly extended 24 \ emission certainly could be driven by star formation in the inner part of the molecular gas disk, but star formation activity is not required.

sources.summary: NGC 2320 is described as a giant elliptical with mapped CO, asymmetric gas and dust, bright emission, and gas angular momentum consistent with the stars.

quotes.quote: In pursuit of these clues we have used the BIMA millimeter array to map CO emission in the giant elliptical galaxies NGC 83 and NGC 2320 and to search for CO emission from the S0 galaxy NGC 5838.
The molecular gas in NGC 83 is well relaxed, but both CO and dust in NGC 2320 show asymmetric structures which may be linked to a recent acquisition of the gas.
However, the specific angular momentum distribution of molecular gas in NGC 2320 is consistent with that of the stars.

gmw_00000768.name: cE1
gmw_00000768.overview: The compact elliptical galaxy cE1 is located in a small-group environment [arXiv:1009.3185] associated with the host galaxy CGCG 241-068 [arXiv:1103.1257]. It exhibits clear tidal tails [arXiv:1009.3185, arXiv:1103.1257] and hosts a young-to-intermediate-age stellar population with supersolar metallicity [arXiv:1009.3185, arXiv:1511.03264].

sources.summary: The paper identifies cE1 as one of two newly discovered compact ellipticals with tidal tails, young-to-intermediate stellar populations, small effective radii, and a small-group environment suggesting tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: We present two newly-discovered, compact elliptical (cE) galaxies, which exhibit clear evidence of tidal steams, found in a search of SDSS DR7. The structural parameters of the cEs are derived using GALFIT and give effective radii < 400 pc. They also possess young to intermediate-age stellar populations. These two cEs provide direct evidence, a "smoking gun'', for the process of tidal stripping that is believed to be the origin of M32-type galaxies.

One interesting result is the environments in which cE1 and cE2 are found. Conventionally, searches for cEs have focussed on galaxy clusters (e.g. ), on the premise that the cluster environment is more favourable to stripping. However, cE1 and cE2 are in small groups. This can be partly explained by the more rapid dissolution of streams in the complex tidal fields of a cluster ( ), and by the presence of intra-cluster light making detection of tidal features difficult.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J110404.40+451618.9 as a compact elliptical with a visible tidal tail, associated with CGCG 241-068 and lying 15.2 kpc from its host.

quotes.quote: During this search for candidate compact elliptical galaxies (Huxor et al. in prep.), we have found two examples (hereafter cE1 and cE2) of compact galaxies with clearly visible tidal streams, which were identified visually during follow-up of the candidates. Both are described in detail below. SDSS J110404.40+451618.9 (cE1) Although close to its neighbouring galaxy (Figure Fi:cE1image ), a tail can clearly be seen streaming in the direction away from cE1, and a further component of the tail facing the neighbour is visible when this galaxy is subtracted with GALFIT (Figure Fi:cE1image_nohost ) The redshift of cE1 is given by SDSS as 0.0215 0.0002. The neighbouring galaxy (CGCG 241-068) does not have a redshift from SDSS, but NED, using UZC data Falcoetal99 , gives a redshift for the host of 0.0220, which confirms that cE is associated with CGCG 241-068.

At this distance, the cE has an extinction corrected absolute magnitude, using the SDSS catalogue data, in the -band (M _ r0 ) of -18.57 mags, a (g-) _ 0 colour of 0.81 mags, and lies at a projected distance of 15.2 kpc from the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies cE1 among stripped compact ellipticals, noting tidal-stream evidence and deriving a young-to-intermediate age, supersolar metallicity, and slightly enhanced alpha abundance.

quotes.quote: These objects (NGC 3628-UCD1 -- a , NGC 4546-UCD1 -- b , M60-UCD1, VCC 165cE -- , cE1 -- d , cE2 -- e ) are also highlighted in Fig. fig:massrad_stellpop , where stellar age, metallicity, and [ /Fe ] are shown as a parameter in the size stellar mass plane. Two of the six objects have super solar metallicity, and all of them are at least 0.3 dex more metal rich than more diffuse galaxies at similar stellar mass. Two of the stripped high mass CSSs (cE1 and VCC 165cE) show young ages ( < 3 Gyr).

The stellar population parameters with a young age ( 1.5^ +0.4 _ 0.3 Gyr), metallicity around solar ( [ Z/ H ] =-0.13 0.16 ), and [ / Fe ] =0.20 0.12 fit very well to this scenario. cE1 and cE2 Huxor11b discovered two cEs embedded in tidal streams. For cE1 ( d in Fig. fig:massrad_stellpop ) our results and their analysis based on the SDSS spectrum agree qualitatively: young to intermediate age, supersolar metallicity, and slightly enhanced [ /Fe ] . Here we obtain a somewhat younger age and higher metallicity.

gmw_00000769.name: cE2
gmw_00000769.overview: The compact elliptical galaxy cE2 is located in a small-group environment [arXiv:1009.3185] at a projected distance of 18.6 kpc from its host galaxy, III Zw 069 [arXiv:1103.1257]. It exhibits tidal tails [arXiv:1009.3185, arXiv:1103.1257] and contains a stellar population dominated by old stars [arXiv:1511.03264].

sources.summary: The paper identifies cE2 as one of two newly discovered compact ellipticals with tidal tails, young-to-intermediate stellar populations, small effective radii, and a small-group environment suggesting tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: We present two newly-discovered, compact elliptical (cE) galaxies, which exhibit clear evidence of tidal steams, found in a search of SDSS DR7. The structural parameters of the cEs are derived using GALFIT and give effective radii < 400 pc. They also possess young to intermediate-age stellar populations. These two cEs provide direct evidence, a "smoking gun'', for the process of tidal stripping that is believed to be the origin of M32-type galaxies.

One interesting result is the environments in which cE1 and cE2 are found. Conventionally, searches for cEs have focussed on galaxy clusters (e.g. ), on the premise that the cluster environment is more favourable to stripping. However, cE1 and cE2 are in small groups. This can be partly explained by the more rapid dissolution of streams in the complex tidal fields of a cluster ( ), and by the presence of intra-cluster light making detection of tidal features difficult.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J231512.62-011458.3 as a compact elliptical undergoing stripping, with CFHT and WHT imaging showing tidal tails near its host III Zw 069.

quotes.quote: SDSS J231512.62-011458.3 (cE2) The second cE (cE2) is a particularly fine example of stripping in progress. In addition to the discovery SDSS image, this cE is found in archival CFHT imaging, and we have also obtained g-band imaging with WHT/ACAM. SDSS gives an M _ r0 of -18.1 and (g-) _ 0 colour of 0.83, and a redshift for the host of the cE as 0.0252. Using this redshift NED gives a luminosity distance of 109.3 Mpc, and an angular size distance of 104.0 Mpc. With these values, the dwarf lies at a projected distance of 18.6 kpc from its host (III Zw 069) which is 3 mags more luminous than the cE.

Tails can be seen from either side of the dwarf, with the southern tail being very prominent (Figure Fi:cE2image ), and extending beyond the chip edge. A fainter diffuse structure can also be seen extending to the west of the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies cE2 as a tidal-stream compact elliptical, finding discrepant age estimates and using MODS plus SDSS spectra to reassess its older stellar population.

quotes.quote: These objects (NGC 3628-UCD1 -- a , NGC 4546-UCD1 -- b , M60-UCD1, VCC 165cE -- , cE1 -- d , cE2 -- e ) are also highlighted in Fig. fig:massrad_stellpop , where stellar age, metallicity, and [ /Fe ] are shown as a parameter in the size stellar mass plane. Two of the six objects have super solar metallicity, and all of them are at least 0.3 dex more metal rich than more diffuse galaxies at similar stellar mass.

The stellar population parameters with a young age ( 1.5^ +0.4 _ 0.3 Gyr), metallicity around solar ( [ Z/ H ] =-0.13 0.16 ), and [ / Fe ] =0.20 0.12 fit very well to this scenario. cE1 and cE2 Huxor11b discovered two cEs embedded in tidal streams.

For the age of cE2 ( e in Fig. fig:massrad_stellpop ), the agreement is less good. The analysis of the MODS spectrum suggests that the object is dominated by old stars, unlike the intermediate age of 5.4 1.6 Gyr found by Huxor11b . In order to further investigate the difference, we analysed also the SDSS spectrum.

gmw_00000770.name: AGC 191770
gmw_00000770.overview: AGC 191770 is an edge-on low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1702.06026]. Stellar population synthesis of the galaxy indicates a potential dearth of stars younger than 1 Gyr [arXiv:1702.06026].

sources.summary: AGC 191770 is one of three Lick-observed edge-on LSBGs used for stellar-population synthesis; the paper discusses its slit-loss estimate and supports a dearth of stars younger than 1 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Given the uncertainties associated with continuum shapes of the Palomar Hale spectra, we decided to use only the three Lick Shane spectra (of AGC 191770, AGC 228010, and AGC 253926) for our stellar-population study of LSBGs. All these three spectra have S/N > 10 in the continuum wavelength interval 4730--4780  \ and meet our requirements. For stellar population synthesis, STARLIGHT Cid Fernandes05 is a widely used tool.

As shown in Table  tbl-2 in Section 3.2, if a star was being centered at 6000  \ and observed with the same slit positioning schemes and observing configurations as those in our program, it will be off center by 0.61 at 3600  \ and 0.05  in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 191770, by 0.8 at 3600  \ and 0.06 in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 228010, and by 0.8 at 3600  \ and 0.06 in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 253926. The light loss at 3600  \ is (respectively) 4.8 , 15.2 , and 0.6 greater than that at 6500  \ for AGC 191770, AGC 228010, and AGC 253926. Obviously, the relative blue-light loss of AGC 228010 is the largest among the three galaxies. However, such an amount of light loss is calculated under the assumption that our targets were stars. Actually, as seen in Figure   fig:edgeon-slit , they are galaxies with extended areas larger than the slit width. So, the lost blue light is partly gained back from the neighboring pixels, and the effect on relative flux calibration of blue-end spectra is not that significant, although it still exists. The probable light loss at blue-end wavelengths ( 3600   ) would cause an underestimate of late A-type stars. Even with some blue-light loss, we could still obtain the same qualitative conclusion that these three LSBGs have a potential dearth of stars with ages <1  Gyr because late A-type stars typically have ages older than 1  Gyr.

gmw_00000771.name: AGC 228010
gmw_00000771.overview: AGC 228010 is classified as a low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:1702.06026]. Spectroscopic observations of its stellar population indicate a potential dearth of stars younger than 1 Gyr [arXiv:1702.06026].

sources.summary: AGC 228010 is identified as a super-thin low-surface-brightness disk and one of three Lick spectra used for stellar-population synthesis, with the largest estimated blue-light loss.

quotes.quote: Given the uncertainties associated with continuum shapes of the Palomar Hale spectra, we decided to use only the three Lick Shane spectra (of AGC 191770, AGC 228010, and AGC 253926) for our stellar-population study of LSBGs. All these three spectra have S/N > 10 in the continuum wavelength interval 4730--4780  \ and meet our requirements. For stellar population synthesis, STARLIGHT Cid Fernandes05 is a widely used tool.

As shown in Table  tbl-2 in Section 3.2, if a star was being centered at 6000  \ and observed with the same slit positioning schemes and observing configurations as those in our program, it will be off center by 0.61 at 3600  \ and 0.05  in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 191770, by 0.8 at 3600  \ and 0.06 in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 228010, and by 0.8 at 3600  \ and 0.06 in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 253926. The light loss at 3600  \ is (respectively) 4.8 , 15.2 , and 0.6 greater than that at 6500  \ for AGC 191770, AGC 228010, and AGC 253926. Obviously, the relative blue-light loss of AGC 228010 is the largest among the three galaxies. However, such an amount of light loss is calculated under the assumption that our targets were stars. Actually, as seen in Figure   fig:edgeon-slit , they are galaxies with extended areas larger than the slit width. So, the lost blue light is partly gained back from the neighboring pixels, and the effect on relative flux calibration of blue-end spectra is not that significant, although it still exists. The probable light loss at blue-end wavelengths ( 3600   ) would cause an underestimate of late A-type stars. Even with some blue-light loss, we could still obtain the same qualitative conclusion that these three LSBGs have a potential dearth of stars with ages <1  Gyr because late A-type stars typically have ages older than 1  Gyr.

gmw_00000772.name: AGC 253926
gmw_00000772.overview: AGC 253926 is an edge-on low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1702.06026]. Stellar population synthesis of the galaxy indicates a potential dearth of stars younger than 1 Gyr [arXiv:1702.06026].

sources.summary: AGC 253926 is one of three Lick-observed edge-on LSBGs used for stellar-population synthesis; the paper discusses its blue-light loss estimate and the shared young-star dearth conclusion.

quotes.quote: Given the uncertainties associated with continuum shapes of the Palomar Hale spectra, we decided to use only the three Lick Shane spectra (of AGC 191770, AGC 228010, and AGC 253926) for our stellar-population study of LSBGs. All these three spectra have S/N > 10 in the continuum wavelength interval 4730--4780  \ and meet our requirements. For stellar population synthesis, STARLIGHT Cid Fernandes05 is a widely used tool.

As shown in Table  tbl-2 in Section 3.2, if a star was being centered at 6000  \ and observed with the same slit positioning schemes and observing configurations as those in our program, it will be off center by 0.61 at 3600  \ and 0.05  in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 191770, by 0.8 at 3600  \ and 0.06 in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 228010, and by 0.8 at 3600  \ and 0.06 in the opposite direction at 6500  \ for AGC 253926. The light loss at 3600  \ is (respectively) 4.8 , 15.2 , and 0.6 greater than that at 6500  \ for AGC 191770, AGC 228010, and AGC 253926. Obviously, the relative blue-light loss of AGC 228010 is the largest among the three galaxies. However, such an amount of light loss is calculated under the assumption that our targets were stars. Actually, as seen in Figure   fig:edgeon-slit , they are galaxies with extended areas larger than the slit width. So, the lost blue light is partly gained back from the neighboring pixels, and the effect on relative flux calibration of blue-end spectra is not that significant, although it still exists. The probable light loss at blue-end wavelengths ( 3600   ) would cause an underestimate of late A-type stars. Even with some blue-light loss, we could still obtain the same qualitative conclusion that these three LSBGs have a potential dearth of stars with ages <1  Gyr because late A-type stars typically have ages older than 1  Gyr.

gmw_00000773.name: PGC 26218
gmw_00000773.overview: PGC 26218 is a nearby lenticular galaxy that exhibits central star-formation activity and a post-starburst disk [arXiv:2001.05679]. It features multiple nuclear structures and a kinematic misalignment between its molecular gas and ionized hydrogen, suggesting the galaxy experienced a gas-rich minor merger [arXiv:2001.05679].

sources.summary: The paper presents PGC 26218 as a nearby S0 with central star formation, post-starburst disk regions, molecular gas, kinematic misalignment, multiple nuclei, and evidence for a gas-rich minor merger.

quotes.quote: We present 2D-spectroscopic observations from Centro Astron omico Hispano Alem an (CAHA) 3.5 m telescope and the millimetre observation from NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) of the nearby S0 galaxy PGC 26218, which shows central star-formation activity and post-starburst outside in the disk. We estimate the star formation rate (SFR = 0.28 0.01 M_ yr^ 1 ) and molecular gas mass ( log \ M_ H_ 2 =7.60 0.15 \ M_ ) of PGC 26218 based on the extinction-corrected H emission line and the CO- H_ 2 conversion factor ( _ CO ) of the Milky Way, respectively. We find that PGC 26218 follows the star forming main sequence (SFMS) and the Kennicutt-Schmidt law. Comparing the kinematics of CO( J =1-0), stars and H , we find that the rotational axis of CO( J =1-0) is 45 ^ different from that of H .

With the decomposition of the optical image, we confirm that PGC 26218 shows multiple nuclear structures. The projected offset between the most luminous optical center and the center of CO( J =1-0) is 5.2 ( 0.6 kpc) and the latter overlaps with one of the optical cores. These results support that PGC 26218 may have experienced a gas-rich minor merger, extending its star formation and locating it in the SFMS.

gmw_00000774.name: SDSSJ092712.65+294344.0
gmw_00000774.overview: SDSSJ092712.65+294344.0 is a peculiar quasar featuring multiple optical emission-line systems with a velocity offset of 2650 km/s [arXiv:0805.1420, arXiv:1409.1585]. This system has been interpreted as a recoiling supermassive black hole [arXiv:0805.1420, arXiv:2311.14830], a massive binary black hole [arXiv:0809.3446, arXiv:1109.0710], or a superposition of active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1109.0710, arXiv:1409.1585].

sources.summary: The paper discusses SDSSJ0927+2943 as a proposed recoiling quasar whose extreme velocity offset would imply rapid galaxy escape and a short, unusually serendipitous observable quasar phase.

quotes.quote: While this paper was undergoing revision, komoss08 announced that the quasar SDSSJ0927+2943 is a candidate recoiling SMBH with a velocity of 2650 km s ^ 1 . They have observed an offset between the broad emission lines and some of the narrow emission lines in this unusual quasar's spectrum, which they suggest can be explained if the broad line region is gravitationally bound to a recoiling BH and the narrow line region is left behind in the galactic disk. 2650 km s ^ 1 is close to the maximum possible recoil kick velocity, and the actual velocity may be even higher if there is a velocity component perpendicular to the line of sight. Thus, this proposed recoil event is certainly not a typical event.

As mentioned in sec:intro , komoss08 have proposed that SDSSJ0927+2943 is a recoiling quasar with a velocity offset of 2650 km s ^ 1 . We do not plot any recoil trajectories with kick velocities this high, because this is well above galactic escape velocities. Were we to reproduce the distributions in Fig. fig:rvdistns for a run with v_ k = 2650 km s ^ 1 , we would find a delta function at v_ k , because the BH would be ejected from the galaxy with little deceleration. The quasar lifetime, however, would be short.

sources.summary: The review treats SDSS J0927+2943 as a recoiling-black-hole and binary candidate, but emphasizes evidence for superposed or visually close quasars.

quotes.quote: Let us here consider in more details the recoiling black hole candidate SDSS J0927+2943 first noted candidate kz08 , that has been observed and discussed in several papers. The quasar was suspected to be kicked off kz08 , and second idea was that it is hosting a massive black hole binary embedded in a circumbinary disk. found that the system is the SMB with mass ratio q 0.3, where the mass of primary is 2 10^9 M . The semimajor axis was estimated as 0.34 pc, corresponding to an orbital period of 370 years. They found that the binary hypothesis is preferred being 100 times more probable than the ejection hypothesis.

Moreover, the long-slit spectroscopic observations of SDSSJ092712+294344 showed two systems (quasars), one, at z = 0.7128, being at an impact parameter of 1 kpc with respect to the another quasar at z = 0.6972 in the north-west direction viv09 and it seems that the SMB model is most unlikely.
