Although a consensus on the nature of these systems has not been reached (it has been claimed that the galaxies could actually be at a much shorter distance, which would invalidate the conclusions of vD2019_noDM2 ), these results have stirred up discussion on the physical mechanisms that could produce these anomalies. , in particular, using a hydrodynamical simulation, found that the tidal forces associated with extreme close encounters with massive neighbours can be responsible for the effect, predicting that 30 of massive central galaxies harbour at least one DM-deficient satellite. Also recently, took a step forward suggesting that NGC1052-DF2 and NGC1052-DF4 are part of a trail of DM-free galaxies that originated from the same event, a collision of dwarf galaxies resembling the Bullet Cluster collision.

This analysis was motivated by recent observations suggesting this possibility (the NGC1052--DF2 and NGC1052--DF4 galaxies, see vD2018_noDM1,vD2019_noDM2,vD2022 ). We have shown that DM deficient systems appear in the three IllustrisTNG boxes and span a wide range of stellar and subhalo masses, confirming previous results using hydrodynamical simulations within the CDM scenario see e.g. Ploeckinger2018,Haslbauer2019,Haslbauer2019b,Sales2020,Applebaum2021 .

sources.summary: NGC1052-DF4 is discussed as another confirmed dark-matter-deficient satellite of the NGC1052 group, similar to DF2 and difficult to form in standard CDM.

quotes.quote: Subsequent to the discovery of DF2, another dark matter deficient galaxy (DMDG), NGC1052-DF4 (DF4) has been identified . DF4 shares similar age, size, and mass distribution characteristics with DF2. Recent observations and distance analysis have confirmed the status of DF2 and DF4 as DMDGs .

Since DF2 and DF4 are satellite galaxies of the NGC1052 group, the tidal field of the host halo could strip off DM in the outskirts of their halos. Conversely, the stellar components bound in the center experience much lower mass loss, resulting in a reduced ratio of DM to stellar mass. However, simulations show that DMDGs similar to DF2 and DF4 are unlikely to form within the standard cosmological framework .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC1052-DF4 as a satellite dwarf with globular-cluster-like companions whose small velocity dispersion implies little dark matter and argues against strong tidal influence.

quotes.quote: The satellite NGC1052-DF4 of the elliptical NGC 1052 has seven of its own satellites that look like globular star clusters. One is at 7 kpc projected distance from NGC1052-DF4, four at 3 to 5 kpc, and the other two are at closer projected distances. The dispersion of their redshifts relative to NGC1052-DF4 is no more than 6 km s ^ 1 . The mass of NGC1052-DF4, based on the spread of redshifts and distances of these globular clusters, is about what would be expected if NGC1052-DF4 contained no dark matter. This does not look at all likely to be a tidal dwarf situation. The small motions of the globular clusters at considerable distances from the stars in NGC1052-DF4 instead argue for minimal tidal influence on a system that has little dark matter within the distances of the globular clusters from this dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00000786.name: manga-8439-6102
gmw_00000786.overview: The galaxy manga-8439-6102 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1911.00090]. It serves as a key structural example because its photometric and kinematic disc orientation measurements are highly similar, resulting in relatively small pattern-speed errors [arXiv:1911.00090].

sources.summary: The paper uses manga-8439-6102 as an example barred galaxy where photometric and kinematic orientations are similar and PA-related pattern-speed errors are relatively small.

quotes.quote: To facilitate the discussion in the following sections, we will use three galaxies from our sample, as key examples. These galaxies were chosen to illustrate how the measurement of the disc PA can be affected in different scenarios using a photometric or kinematic method (hereafter and ). (1) manga-8439-6102, a galaxy where both and are similar and has one of the smallest errors in our sample. This is the same galaxy that is presented in .
In our sample, we observe a convergence of in 13 galaxies including our 3 example galaxies (see Figure Fig:Omega_Slits ).

gmw_00000787.name: manga-8135-6103
gmw_00000787.overview: The galaxy manga-8135-6103 is a strong barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1911.00090]. It features strong spiral arms that extend to its outermost region, and its bar structure covers the entire field of view of the MaNGA survey [arXiv:1911.00090].

sources.summary: The paper uses manga-8135-6103 as a strong barred galaxy where spiral arms bias the photometric PA, making kinematic orientation more reliable for pattern-speed work.

quotes.quote: (2) manga-8135-6103, a strong barred galaxy where is biased due to the strong spiral arms, however, the kinematic orientation gives a better estimation of . Also illustrates one case where the bar covers entirety the FoV of MaNGA. Our example galaxy, manga-8135-6103 illustrates such systematic, with strong spiral arms that extend to the outermost region, biasing the (see Figure Fig:Iso_Profiles ). This results in a difference with of 33.2 .
An example can be seen in manga-8135-6103 in Figure Fig:PA-error , as the PA of the slits gets closer to the bar minor axis, which lies at 96 .

gmw_00000788.name: manga-8341-12704
gmw_00000788.overview: The galaxy manga-8341-12704 is a strongly barred system with a low inclination angle [arXiv:1911.00090]. It hosts a strong active galactic nucleus, which is associated with perturbed kinematics near the galactic center [arXiv:1911.00090].

sources.summary: The paper highlights manga-8341-12704 as a strongly barred, low-inclination galaxy whose perturbed central kinematics and AGN-related disturbance complicate kinematic orientation and pattern-speed estimates.

quotes.quote: (3) manga-8341-12704, a strongly barred, and low inclined galaxy where the velocity map is highly perturbed, making the more difficult to estimate. Three galaxies present problems with the kinematic orientation obtained from the best Velfit model (manga-8341-12704, manga-7990-12704 and NGC 5406). The example galaxy manga-8341-12704 has two major complications. (1) The low inclination angle of 17.3 , which makes the kinematic information more uncertain; (2) The resolved kinematics traced by H_ show a perturbation near the centre of the galaxy.

The origin of this perturbation may be related to the presence of a strong AGN (Cortés-Suárez et al. in prep), which cannot be modelled by Velfit . manga-8341-12704 is a low inclination galaxy with strong non-axisymmetric motions.

gmw_00000789.name: NGC 5205
gmw_00000789.overview: NGC 5205 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1911.00090] that hosts a candidate ultrafast bar [arXiv:1501.05498, arXiv:1911.00090]. The galaxy contains dust lanes within its bar radius [arXiv:1501.05498], and its bar kinematics have been analyzed using the Tremaine-Weinberg method [arXiv:1911.00090].

sources.summary: The study treats NGC5205 as an ultrafast-bar candidate, finding gas-curve corotation estimates match stellar-kinematic values and that dust lanes cannot explain its low R values.

quotes.quote: We have not found in the literature any HI velocity measurements for the two other ultrafast bars (NGC5205 and NGC6497). However, we did recompute the R_ CR for NGC5205 and NGC6497 using the angular velocity curve obtained from the gas velocity curve along the major axis of these two galaxies see for details garcialorenzo2015 . In these two cases, the new values of R were the same as those obtained from the stellar kinematics. Thus we cannot discard that these two galaxies R might be smaller than one within the errors.

Two of the ultrafast bars, NGC0036 and NGC5205, show PA<0 . This means that for these two galaxies, the presence of dust lanes within the bar radius should lead to lower TW values of _ b than would the real one. Therefore the TW-derived value of R would be greater than the real ones owing to the presence of dust lanes.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 5205 as a CALIFA barred galaxy with an ultrafast bar, dominant centering uncertainty, monotonically decreasing slit-length behavior, and relatively small PSF-convolution changes.

quotes.quote: To compare our results and spot systematic errors in our methodology, we re-analysed a sub-sample of 3 CALIFA galaxies already analyzed with the TW-method by (NGC 5205, NGC 5406 and NGC 6497). According to their light-weighted measurements, these three galaxies host ultra-fast bars. We choose these galaxies due to the controversy surrounding the existence of ultra-fast bars.

Nonetheless, this is the dominant error in two galaxies (manga-8257-12704 and NGC 5205) and is greater than 10 in two more (manga-8243-12704 and manga-8341-12704). Of the 13 galaxies which do converge, 2 of them show a monotonically decreasing function (manga-8453-12701 and NGC 5205), and 3 show an oscillating function that ends up flattening (manga-8312-12704, manga-8313-9101 and NGC 6497). The least affected galaxy was NGC 5205 where and changes by 20 which remains within a 2- error of the original measurement.

gmw_00000790.name: NGC 5406
gmw_00000790.overview: NGC 5406 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1911.00090, arXiv:1807.01092] with spiral arms that host an increased fraction of younger stars [arXiv:1807.01092]. The galaxy's bar and barlens regions show evidence of repeated star formation episodes, while its center exhibits a high velocity dispersion [arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 5406 as a CALIFA barred galaxy with disputed ultra-fast versus slow-bar classification, problematic kinematic orientation, non-convergent slit-length behavior, and strong PSF sensitivity.

quotes.quote: To compare our results and spot systematic errors in our methodology, we re-analysed a sub-sample of 3 CALIFA galaxies already analyzed with the TW-method by (NGC 5205, NGC 5406 and NGC 6497). According to their light-weighted measurements, these three galaxies host ultra-fast bars. In their mass-weighted results, only NGC 5406 becomes a slow bar. We choose these galaxies due to the controversy surrounding the existence of ultra-fast bars.

Three galaxies present problems with the kinematic orientation obtained from the best Velfit model (manga-8341-12704, manga-7990-12704 and NGC 5406). In the case of NGC 5406, the spiral arms that start at the bar region could have a strong non-axisymmetric contribution, that could bias the Velfit model. The non-convergence in the remaining two galaxies (manga-8318-12703 and NGC 5406) is more difficult to explain but could be related to the spiral structure that is covered in the IFU FoV.
NGC 5406 which has the largest projected bar, is the most affected by the convolutions.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5406 has bar and barlens stellar populations like NGC 7563, with young spiral arms, metal enrichment from repeated star formation, and a high-dispersion center.

quotes.quote: NGC 5406 (T = 3.5) : The distributions of the oldest and intermediate age stars in the bar/barlens regions are as in NGC 7563, i.e. the fraction of the oldest stars drops at the galaxy center compared to the barlens region. Outside the barlens the fraction of younger stars increases due to the prominent spiral arms. However, the disk is dominated by very metal-poor stars (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = -0.7), whose fraction starts to drop in the bar region so that in the galaxy center those stars have disappeared.

So, also in this galaxy the bar and the barlens have had repeated episodes of stars formation which have enriched the gas in metals. In the disk outside the bar that has happened in a less extent than in NGC 7563. The galaxy center has a higher velocity dispersion ( = 214 km/sec) than the bar or the barlens ( = 128 and 142 km/sec, respectively), most probably related to higher stellar density.

gmw_00000791.name: NGC 6497
gmw_00000791.overview: NGC 6497 is a barred spiral galaxy that hosts an ultrafast bar [arXiv:1501.05498, arXiv:1911.00090]. The galaxy contains dust lanes within its bar region [arXiv:1501.05498] and exhibits spaxels dominated by both H II regions and diffuse ionized gas [arXiv:1501.02668].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC6497 as an ultrafast-bar case, reports no H I velocity measurement, and says gas-curve and stellar-kinematic estimates still allow R below one.

quotes.quote: One of our galaxies (NGC 6497) shows R < 1 within the errors (1 ) for our four different measurements.

We have not found in the literature any HI velocity measurements for the two other ultrafast bars (NGC5205 and NGC6497). However, we did recompute the R_ CR for NGC5205 and NGC6497 using the angular velocity curve obtained from the gas velocity curve along the major axis of these two galaxies see for details garcialorenzo2015 . In these two cases, the new values of R were the same as those obtained from the stellar kinematics. Thus we cannot discard that these two galaxies R might be smaller than one within the errors.

In contrast, PA_ bar >0 for NGC6497. Thus, according to Gerssen Debattista (2007), the TW method will show higher values of _ b and lower values of R than the real ones owing to dust lanes within the bar region. For this galaxy we obtained the extinction map from the fit of its stellar population see perez2013,gonzalezdelgado2014,cidfernandes2013 .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 6497 as a CALIFA galaxy with an ultrafast bar, oscillating slit-length convergence, and stronger PA sensitivity after spatial-resolution degradation.

quotes.quote: To compare our results and spot systematic errors in our methodology, we re-analysed a sub-sample of 3 CALIFA galaxies already analyzed with the TW-method by (NGC 5205, NGC 5406 and NGC 6497). According to their light-weighted measurements, these three galaxies host ultra-fast bars. We choose these galaxies due to the controversy surrounding the existence of ultra-fast bars.

Of the 13 galaxies which do converge, 2 of them show a monotonically decreasing function (manga-8453-12701 and NGC 5205), and 3 show an oscillating function that ends up flattening (manga-8312-12704, manga-8313-9101 and NGC 6497). In the cases of NGC 5406 and NGC 6497, the slope becomes noticeably steeper as we increase _ eff , making our estimation of more sensitive to the PA error. However, our test suggests that, while the value of remains close to the 2-sigma error, the sensitivity to the PA error does increase.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC6497 as a CALIFA example to show [S II]/H and H-flux diagnostics that separate H II-region and diffuse-ionized-gas dominated spaxels.

quotes.quote: Figure dig_example shows a typical [ S ii ] /H versus H flux plot of the CALIFA galaxy NGC6497. All the data points (spaxels) are significantly detected ( >3 ) in [ S ii ] and H . Spaxels with high H fluxes have low (high) [ S ii ] /H , consistent with the low (high) line ratios of H ii regions (DIG).

gmw_00000792.name: companion to UGC 4541
gmw_00000792.overview: The companion to UGC 4541 is a satellite galaxy that exhibits tidal distortion from its interaction with UGC 4541 [arXiv:0805.2817]. It has a short star-formation timescale and displays a strong nuclear starburst or active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:0805.2817].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the companion to UGC 4541 as a tidally disturbed star-forming satellite with nuclear starburst or AGN-like H-alpha morphology and the shortest satellite star-formation timescale.

quotes.quote: The one bright satellite which is displaced somewhat to a short SF timescale is the companion to UGC 4541, which appears tidally disturbed and may be undergoing an interaction-induced starburst or other nuclear activity. UGC 4541 this system appears to be undergoing significant tidal disturbance; unsurprisingly, the catalogued recession velocity of the companion is close to that of UGC 4541. The \ morphology of the companion reveals a strong nuclear starburst and/or AGN activity; the strength of the \ emission results in the short SF timescale found for this object in section 3.

The satellite galaxy with the shortest SF timescale is the companion to UGC 4541; this not the closest galaxy-satellite pair investigated here, but there does appear to be tidal distortion associated with this interaction. The only evidence of a strong starburst is in the tidally-disturbed companion to UGC 4541.

gmw_00000793.name: companion to UGC 4362
gmw_00000793.overview: The companion to UGC 4362 is a bright satellite galaxy that is very extended in its continuum emission [arXiv:0805.2817]. It exhibits strong star formation with clumpy, resolved H-alpha regions [arXiv:0805.2817].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2551 as having a bright clumpy UV ring whose extended ionized-gas disk counterrotates the stellar disk, while present star formation continues despite counterrotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2551. In this galaxy the UV-ring is very bright and clumpy both in the FUV and in the NUV, and also is rather wide, from R 15^ to R 30^ (2--5 ) galex . The extended ionized-gas disk which is coincident radially with the UV-ring (Fig. fig_fpi ) counterrotates as a whole with respect to the stellar disk we2551 . Earlier we published the long-slit cross-section along the disk major axis we2551 ; now we present a cross-section under 43^ to the line of nodes (Fig. fig_ls ), confirming the gas counterrotation, and also the falling character of the stellar rotation curve.

We have found that current star formation proceeds usually just where the gas lies strictly in the stellar disk planes and rotates there circularly; the sense of the gas rotation does not matter (the counterrotating gas in NGC 2551 forms stars currently).

sources.summary: The paper describes the companion to UGC 4362 as a bright, strongly star-forming object with clumpy resolved H-alpha regions, partly cut off by the CCD edge.

quotes.quote: UGC 4362 the companion is bright in the R band image, and shows strong SF with clumpy, resolved \ regions, but has not been previously catalogued according to NED. The companion is on the extreme edge of the CCD frame so the measured SF rate and R band luminosities in Table 1 should be considered as lower limits, although the majority of the companion does appear to have been imaged, from inspection of sky survey images of this field.

The resulting R band sizes are plotted against the distance to the central galaxy in Mpc, in Fig. fig:Rsatsize . This confirms that all 9 of the likely satellite galaxies are very extended in terms of their continuum emission, with FWHM sizes of 1.5 -- 6.0 kpc.

gmw_00000794.name: companion to UGC 12788
gmw_00000794.overview: The companion to UGC 12788 is a galaxy projected on the outer disk of its central host [arXiv:0805.2817]. Although it may be involved in a tidal interaction, the companion appears completely unaffected in both its optical morphology and star-forming properties [arXiv:0805.2817].

sources.summary: The paper treats the companion to UGC 12788 as a possible satellite projected on the host disk, noting possible tidal interaction but little obvious disturbance in the companion itself.

quotes.quote: UGC 12788 the putative companion is projected on the outer disk of the central galaxy, but does not look like an HII region or part of a spiral arm. This is a possible case of tidal interaction, revealed in the distorted and strongly star-forming arm in UGC 12788, close to the putative companion. However, it should be noted that the object considered here to be a companion galaxy is listed in NED as `West \ region in UGC 12788'.

The closest pairing in projected separation is UGC 12788 and its companion; here the companion appears completely unaffected by tidal effects, in terms of both optical morphology and SF properties, so it is possible that there is a significant line-of-sight separation in this case.

gmw_00000795.name: companion to UGC 5688
gmw_00000795.overview: The companion to UGC 5688 is a galaxy characterized by an elongated light distribution with clumps of emission at both ends [arXiv:0805.2817]. It has a star-formation timescale below 10 Gyr, which may be modestly enhanced by tidal effects [arXiv:0805.2817].

sources.summary: The paper describes the companion to UGC 5688 as clumpy at both ends of an elongated light distribution, with a sub-10 Gyr star-formation timescale and possible modest tidal enhancement.

quotes.quote: UGC 5688 the central galaxy has low R band luminosity and surface brightness. The \ image shows clumps of emission from both ends of the elongated R band light distribution, like the companion of UGC 4574, resembling the pattern of SF seen in many galaxy bars. UGC 6506 this is a similar case to UGC 2603 and UGC 5688, with a low luminosity central galaxy, and this should probably be considered a binary system rather than as a central galaxy with a satellite.
The second closest in apparent separation are UGC 5688 and companion; the latter has a SF timescale below 10 Gyr, which may show a modest enhancement in SF rate as a the result of tidal effects.

gmw_00000796.name: J1110+4817
gmw_00000796.overview: J1110+4817 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 0.74 [arXiv:2006.05144]. It is classified as a compact symmetric object with a bipolar jet [arXiv:2006.05144, arXiv:2307.06363], which exhibits an 87-parsec offset between its radio and optical positions [arXiv:2307.06363].

sources.summary: The paper argues J1110+4817 is most plausibly a compact symmetric object, using archival VLBI morphology, Gaia astrometry, radio spectrum, hot-spot motion, and an unusual transverse extension.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension _ VLBI = 11^ h 10^ m 36 32410 0 00003 and declination _ VLBI = 48 17 52 4498 0 0003

quotes.quote: The quasar J1110+4817 was catalogued as a radio source already in the 1960s named as 5C 2.183, 1968MNRAS.139..529P . Its optical counterpart, SDSS J111036.32+481752.3, is listed with a spectroscopic redshift z^ = 6.168 in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) Data Release 13 http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr13/en/tools/explore/Summary.aspx?id=1237658612517307020 DR13, 2017ApJS..233...25A . However, this value is certainly a result of false automatic identification of emission lines. Here we adopt the considerably lower redshift value z = 0.74 measured by 1996MNRAS.282.1274H and 2016MNRAS.462.1603Y independently.

Based on the analysis of archival multi-frequency VLBI imaging observations, the radio spectrum spanning nearly two orders of magnitude in frequency and peaking at 500  MHz, as well as the location of the optical AGN emission accurately pinpointed by the Gaia space astrometry mission 2016A&A...595A...1G , we find the CSO nature of the object the most plausible scenario, although excluding the possibility of a dual radio AGN system would require further observations. Accepting the CSO interpretation of the radio source, we measure its apparent hot spot separation speed 3c and estimate its kinematic age 530  yr by simply assuming a linear growth rate for the observed symmetric radio structure. According to the lower-frequency VLBI images, there is an extended ( 220  pc) steep-spectrum radio emission in \ seen nearly perpendicular to the jet axis as projected onto the sky.

sources.summary: The paper inspects ILT J111036.37+481752.4 as a known peaked-spectrum source, but finds its fitted spectrum too flat for the study's PS selection.

quotes.quote: The source ILT J111036.37+481752.4 has a counterpart in the CSO sample presented by 2000ApJ...534...90P , and has a peaked spectrum near 250 MHz. However, using our spectral fitting routine described in Section sec:source_selec , we computed the spectral indices of this source to be = 0.48 0.14 and = 0.07 0.05, thus not classifying it as a PS source. This is because the spectrum appears flat between 144 MHz and 1.4 GHz. The source has an abnormally large spectral width of 1.8 GHz - which is substantially larger than the median FWHM of 750 MHz of the PS sample isolated by Callingham_2017 .

sources.summary: The paper reports an 87 pc VLBA-Gaia offset for J1110+4817 and argues archival C-band imaging suggests a bipolar jet likely causes the offset.

quotes.quote: J1110+4817 exhibits an 11.9 1.0 mas (corresponding to 87 pc) VLBA-Gaia offset. The C-band archival image reveals a possible bipolar jet that appears to originate from the Gaia position. Although the cause of the offset is likely due to the small-scale radio jet, confirmation of the jet scenario requires spectral index analysis derived from multi-band images.

gmw_00000797.name: PKS 1510-089
gmw_00000797.overview: PKS 1510-089 is a highly variable flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1301.7677, arXiv:1706.02133] and blazar [arXiv:1112.4614, arXiv:2210.16327] located at a redshift of z=0.361 [arXiv:1305.5449, arXiv:2009.02055]. The active nucleus is powered by a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2007.07672, arXiv:2009.02055] and hosts a curved, superluminal relativistic jet [arXiv:1307.1779, astro-ph/0612149] that exhibits rapid multiwavelength flaring [arXiv:1301.7677, arXiv:2006.03991].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1510-089 as having complex optical/gamma-ray correlation behavior, month-long flares, persistent lag features, and changing gamma-ray to optical flux ratios.

quotes.quote: A more complex correlation behavior was seen in PKS 1510-089 [ 10 ] . Four flares of about one month each were observed in 2008 to 2009 and three of these had good coverage in both optical and gamma-rays. The total DCCF (Figure 11) has a number of correlation peaks.

Two correlation features however, appear to be persistent for all the flares. One is a correlation on short time scales, within 1 - 2 days of lag zero, The second is a strong peak at a lag of about 2 weeks (gamma-rays leading optical). A detailed comparison of the light curves show that the envelope (start and end) of each flare is about the same in the two bands but the shape of the flare is different. The ratio of gamma-ray to optical flux is higher in the beginning of the flare than towards the end. On top of this the flares exhibit strong variability on shorter time scales which is partly correlated and responsible for the correlation peak near zero lag.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes PKS 1510 089 outbursts, measuring extreme gamma-ray luminosity, a 20-minute doubling time, hard short-timescale spectra, and implications for compact jet emission.

quotes.quote: We have reanalyzed the giant outburst of the blazar PKS 1510 089 ( z=0.36 ) that occurred on 2011 October-November. The ray flux in the 0.1-100  GeV energy range exceeded the value of 10^ 5  ph cm ^ 2  s ^ 1 for several days. The peak flux was reached on 2011 October 19, with a value of 4.4 10^ 5  ph cm ^ 2  s ^ 1 , which in turn corresponds to a luminosity of 2 10^ 49  erg s ^ 1 . A very short timescale variability was measured. Particularly on 2011 October 18, the flux-doubling time was as short as 20  minutes.

PKS 1510 089 has redshift z=0.36 (Thompson et al. 1990). In the usual CDM cosmology with the latest measured values of the Hubble-Lema itre constant H_ 0 =70  km s ^ 1  Mpc ^ 1 and _ =0.73 (Komatsu et al. 2011), the luminosity distance is 1934 Mpc, and 1 arcsec corresponds to 5.1 kpc. Fig.  fig:global shows the ray emission as measured by Fermi /LAT of PKS 1510 089 over a period of about four years. In the present work, we focused mostly on outburst B (2011 October/November), but we compared it also with outbursts A and C (2009 March and 2012 February/March, respectively) to draw useful inferences on the source properties.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1510-089 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar whose curved superluminal jet, multiwavelength variability, and high-energy emission require complex blazar-zone physics.

quotes.quote: The radio jet of PKS 1510-089 is curved and shows an apparent superluminal motion as high as 45 times the speed of light 2001ApJ...549..840H,2002ApJ...580..742H,2005AJ....130.1418J . The first large multiwavelength campaign on PKS 1510-089 took place in August 2006 2008ApJ...672..787K and involved the Suzaku and Swift satellites, as well as ground-based optical and radio instruments. The campaign allowed the construction of the broadband spectrum of the object, ranging from 10^ 9 to 10^ 19 Hz. The spectrum was successfully modeled using the one-zone internal shock scenario. 2008ApJ...672..787K focused their work on the explanation of the X-ray part of the SED, where an excess of emissions has been observed.

If interpreted as bulk-Compton radiation, this excess allows an estimate of the pair content of the jet. In the case of PKS 1510-089, the ratio of e ^+ e ^- pairs to the number of protons was estimated to be on the order of 10 . This implies that although the number of e ^+ e ^- pairs is larger than the number of protons, the power of the jet is still dominated by the latter.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1510-089 as an FSRQ whose gamma-ray output is EC-dominated, yielding diminished observed polarization and a compact, symmetric polarization region.

quotes.quote: In this section, we present case studies for two blazars as examples to apply our polarization code: The high-frequency-peaked BL Lac object (HBL) Mkn 421, which is successfully fitted with a pure SSC (Synchrotron-self-Compton) model Chen11 , and the Flat-Spectrum Radio Quasar (FSRQ) PKS 1510-089, which requires an additional EC (external Compton) component to model the ray emission. In the model fit obtained in , the external photons come from a dusty torus. presented model fits to the SEDs and light curves of these two blazars, using their shock-in-jet model as described above.

PKS 1510-089 presents a similar situation, although there are some major differences. First, PKS1510-089 requires a dominating EC component at ray energies, which is independent of the magnetic field strength. Thus in the current scenario, no flare is visible in the Compton bump (Fig. Pks15105 ). Also, due to the contamination of the external thermal radiation from the dust torus, which is unpolarized, the observed polarization percentage will be considerably diminished. Furthermore, EC also introduces strong cooling to the electrons, leading to softer electron spectra (Fig. Electron5 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 1510-089's 2009 flares, focusing on simultaneous data and finding an SSC-constrained emitting region outside the BLR with weak magnetization.

quotes.quote: ht PKS 1510-089 at MJD 54948 PKS 1510-089 ( z = 0.36 , d_ L 1.92 Gpc ), the second most active blazar of the Fermi era 2013MNRAS.430.1324N , has been monitored extensively in the X-ray, optical/NIR, and radio/mm bands. In early 2009, it produced a series of gamma-ray flares, peaking at MJD 54917 (2009 Mar 27), MJD 54948 (2009 Apr 27), and MJD 54962 (2009 May 11) 2010ApJ...721.1425A,2011A&A...529A.145D . The first and the last of them were accompanied by sharp optical/UV flares, but none of them had a clear X-ray counterpart.

Our constraints for the MJD 54948 flare of PKS 1510-089 are presented in Figure fig_pks1510 . The SSC constraint is particularly strong in this case, since L_ /L_X 1000 . The marginal solution is r_ min 0.37 pc at _ min 26 , which is well outside the BLR. The predictions for this solution are _ SSA,obs 1.4 mm , u_ '/u_ B ' 12 , and L_ j,min 2.2 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 0.045 L_ d , which is slightly lower than the total jet power estimate by . Therefore, we suggest that the jet of PKS 1510-089 is only weakly magnetized.

sources.summary: The paper finds that PKS 1510-089 has a northwest jet, monotonic transverse RM gradients in the core, a sign change across the jet, and evidence for a predominantly longitudinal magnetic field.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510-089 : The jet extends towards the north-west at a PA of 30 ^ , in the same direction seen at the smaller scales mapped by the 2 cm MOJAVE observations. The EVPAs corrected for the integrated RM are predominantly transverse to the local jet direction (Fig. Fig1_OJ287_3C279_PKS1510 f), in agreement with the overall trend of polarization structures seen in the MOJAVE maps. The degree of fractional polarization (Fig. Fig1_OJ287_3C279_PKS1510 g) is less than about 5 per cent in the core, and reaches tens of per cent in the jet. Our RM map (Fig. PKS1510_RM ) shows monotonic, transverse gradients in the core region. The example slice shown in Fig. PKS1510_RM displays an RM gradient with a statistical significance of about 4.4 .

A sign change in the RM is observed across the jet. We note that Keck et al. (private communication) have tentatively found similar results for this source, with transverse RM gradients in the core region, using frequencies in the range 22 - 43 GHz. Our RM map also displays a reversal in the direction of the transverse RM gradient further down in the jet of PKS 1510-089.

sources.summary: The paper adopts literature disk and black-hole parameters for J1512.7 0906, models its FSRQ SED, and reports optical-UV accretion-disk emission.

quotes.quote: We generate steady-state, i.e. time-averaged, broadband SEDs of all 8 FSRQs following the details outlined in Section sec3 and reproduce them using both leptonic and lepto-hadronic emission scenarios. The leptonic model SEDs are presented in Figure fig_leptonic_sed and the associated SED parameters are given in Table tab:leptonic_sed_param . The results of the lepto-hadronic SED modeling are shown in Figure fig_hadronic_sed and we provide the physical parameters derived from the modeling in Table tab:hadronic_sed_param .

The leptonic modeling reasonably reproduces most of the observed SEDs of 2FHL FSRQs. The accretion disk emission is observed at optical-UV frequencies in J1224.7+2124, J1427.3 4204, and J1512.7 0906. On the other hand, the optical-UV emission in the remaining 5 sources is dominated by the synchrotron radiation. The high-energy spectra of these objects can be reproduced by a combination of SSC and EC processes.

sources.summary: The paper models the 2015 high state of blazar PKS 1510-089, describing four flares, multiwavelength variability, differing flux-doubling times, and a two-zone emission geometry.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510-089 is one of the most variable blazars in the third Fermi LAT source catalog. During 2015, this source has shown four flares identified as flare A, B, C, and D in between three quiescent states Q1, Q2, and Q3. The multiwavelength data from Fermi LAT, Swift-XRT/UVOT, OVRO, and SMA Observatory are used in our work to model these states. Different flux doubling times have been observed in different energy bands which indicate there could be multiple emission zones.

The flux doubling time from the gamma-ray and X-ray light curve are found to be 10.6 hr, 2.5 days, and the average flux doubling time in the optical/UV band is 1 day. It is possible that the gamma-ray and optical/UV emission are produced in the same region whereas X-ray emission is coming from a different region along the jet axis. We have also estimated the discrete correlations functions (DCFs) among the light curves of different energy bands to infer about their emission regions. However, our DCF analysis does not show significant correlation in different energy bands though it shows peaks in some cases at small time lags. We perform a two-zone multi-wavelength time-dependent modeling with one emission zone located near the outer edge of the broad line region (BLR) and another further away in the dusty/molecular torus (DT/MT) region to study this high state.

sources.summary: The paper reports an unprecedented VHE gamma-ray flare from PKS 1510 089, with intranight variability, rapid cessation, optical counterpart behavior, and VLBI evidence for a distant jet emission region.

quotes.quote: The flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) PKS 1510 089 is known for its complex multiwavelength behavior, and is one of only a few FSRQs detected at very high energy (VHE, E>100 GeV) rays. VHE ray observations with H.E.S.S. and MAGIC during late May and early June 2016 resulted in the detection of an unprecedented flare, which reveals for the first time VHE ray intranight variability in this source. While a common variability timescale of 1.5 hr is found, there is a significant deviation near the end of the flare with a timescale of 20 min marking the cessation of the event.

Optical R-band observations with ATOM reveal a counterpart of the ray flare, even though the detailed flux evolution differs from the VHE ray lightcurve. Interestingly, a steep flux decrease is observed at the same time as the cessation of the VHE ray flare. In the high energy (HE, E>100 MeV) ray band only a moderate flux increase is observed with , while the HE ray spectrum significantly hardens up to a photon index of 1.6 .

Radio VLBI observations reveal a fast moving knot interacting with a standing jet feature around the time of the flare. As the standing feature is located 50 pc from the black hole, the emission region of the flare may have been located at a significant distance from the black hole.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1510-089's orphan gamma-ray flare, measuring hour-scale variability and using multiwavelength SEDs to argue for multiple broadband emission regions.

quotes.quote: We study the temporal and spectral profile of these Orphan ray flares in detail from three ray bright blazars, 3C 273, PKS 1510-089 and 3C 279 and also their simultaneous broadband emissions. We find that the variability timescales of the Orphan ray flares were ( 0.96 0.28 ) days, ( 3.12 2.40 ) hr and ( 2.16 0.72 ) hr, for 3C 273, PKS 1510-089 and 3C 279, respectively. The broadband spectral energy distributions (SEDs) during these flares have been modelled with a leptonic model from two emission regions. This model suggests that Orphan ray flares might have originated from inverse Compton scattering of relativistic electrons by the seed photons from the broad-line region or dusty torus, which is the first region.

PKS 1510-089 The Orphan ray flare from PKS 1510-089 was observed on MJD 54947.5 0.5 with a daily average peak flux of (8.82 0.62) 10 ^ 6 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . The Figure fig:mwlcpks1510 shows the MWLC of this source for the period of MJD 54930-54970. The Orphan ray period was chosen as MJD 54943-54952, which is marked in the same figure. During this period, ray flux increased by a factor of 7.1 without showing much variation at lower energies.

sources.summary: The paper finds PKS 1510-089 variability is dominated by fast flares; candidate 1.2-1.3 year periods appear, but trial-corrected analysis cannot distinguish them from stochastic flare variability.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510-089 A visual inspection of the broadband LCs of this blazar (presented in Figure pks1510lc ) shows that the variability of its emission is mainly dominated by intense and very fast flares. The presence of these features along the LC yields several potential QPOs in the first step of the analysis, performed with the PDM and the REDFIT implementations, the most promising located at a period of 1.2 years in the optical R and V bands and the Fermi LAT LC. This value is consistent with the one claimed by . After performing a rigorous analysis and taking into account the different trial factors, we cannot rule out the possibility of these periods being caused by stochastic flare-like variability (see e.g. the R-band results presented in Figure pks1510_results ). The WWZ time-frequency PSD shows a possible periodicity at 1.3 years, however with a gap present in the time-decomposition between MJD 55500-57000, approximately.

This could indicate that the appearance of this signal could be due to the presence of the aforementioned flares. Moreover, the ZDCF shows a rather sinusoidal shape, with low significance (1 1.5 after trials) and some of the peaks being diluted w..t. the overall evolution of the correlation function. Therefore, we cannot extract a firm conclusion on the existence of this quasi-periodic variability.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1510-089 as historically variable and active in early 2000, with optical microvariability detected in V and R during a March 20 monitoring session.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510-089.- This RLQ has shown intense activity on long and intermediate-term variability Liller75,Tornikoski94,Villata97 , as well as correlated optical/radio variability Tornikoski94 . OM also has been reported Villata97,Xie01,Dai01,Xie04 . In particular, Dai01 reported a variation of 2 mag in 41 minutes in R band at the end of 2000 May. However, Romero99 and Romero02 did not detect any activity when they observed it between 1998 and 1999.

Our observations show that PKS 1510-089 was in a stage of activity during the first semester of 2000. We monitored this object during 4.3 hours in 5 different occasions during the night of 2000 March 20 two months before the observations of Dai01 . Light-curves (Figure fig1c ) indicate the presence of an OM event detected in V and R .

sources.summary: The paper reports AGILE detection of a rapid gamma-ray flare from PKS 1510-089 and discusses its quasar classification, X-ray history, broadband spectrum, and isotropic gamma-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510 089 is a nearby (z=0.361) radio-loud highly polarized quasar (HPQ) already detected also in the MeV--GeV energy band by the EGRET instrument on board CGRO (Hartman et al. 1992). The broadband spectrum of the source is representative of the class of FSRQ with the inverse Compton component dominated by the ray emission and the synchrotron emission peaked around IR frequencies, even if it is clearly visible in this source a pronunced UV bump possibly due to the thermal emission from the accretion disk (Malkan Moore 1986, Pian Treves 1993). PKS 1510 089 has been extensively observed and studied in the X-ray band by the satellites EXOSAT (Singh, Rao Vahia 1990, Sambruna et al. 1994), GINGA (Lawson Turner 1997), ROSAT (Siebert et al. 1998), ASCA (Singh, Shrader George 1997) and Chandra (Gambill et al. 2003). The observed X-ray spectrum was very flat in the 2--10 keV band with photon index of 1.3, but it was steeper ( 1.9) in the ROSAT bandpass (0.1--2.4 keV), suggesting the presence of a possible spectral break around 1--2 keV. The difference of the photon indices could be due to the presence of a soft X-ray excess.

Results During the period 2008 March 1--16, AGILE-GRID detected ray emission from a position consistent with the powerful ray quasar PKS 1510 089 at a significance level of 6.2- with an average flux over the entire period of (84 17) 10^ 8 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for photon energies above 100 MeV.

Using a luminosity distance d _L = 1915 Mpc and the average ray flux observed by the AGILE-GRID during 17--21 March 2008, we obtain for PKS 1510--089 (z = 0.361) an isotropic luminosity L_ ^ iso = 5.3 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1510 089 as a highly variable blazar with AGILE/Fermi gamma-ray activity, optical flares, and hard X-ray spectral behavior.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510--089 PKS 1510 089 is another blazar that in the last three years showed high variability over the whole electromagnetic spectrum. In particular, high ray activity was observed by AGILE and Fermi LAT. AGILE detected intense flaring episodes in August 2007 (Pucella et al. 2008) and March 2008 (D'Ammando et al. 2009) and an extraordinary activity during March 2009 (D'Ammando et al. 2010). During the period 1--16 March 2008, AGILE detected an average ray flux from PKS 1510 089 of (84 17) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for E > 100 MeV.

The Swift /XRT observations showed a harder-when-brighter behaviour of the spectrum in the X-ray band, confirming a behaviour already observed in this source by Kataoka et al. (2008), a trend usually observed in HBLs but quite rare in FSRQs such as PKS 1510 089. This harder-when-brighter behaviour could be due to the contamination at low energies of a second component with respect to the EC emission that usually dominates the X-ray spectrum of the FSRQs. In PKS 1510 089 the X-ray spectral shape of the EC component should remain almost constant.

sources.summary: PKS 1510 089 is observed as a quiet-state blazar whose FIR power-law spectrum and multiwavelength SED favor distinct infrared synchrotron and gamma-ray emitting zones.

quotes.quote: We present the results of observations of blazar PKS 1510 089 with the Herschel Space Observatory PACS and SPIRE instruments, together with multiwavelength data from Fermi /LAT, Swift , SMARTS and SMA. The source was found in a quiet state, and its far-infrared spectrum is consistent with a power-law with a spectral index of 0.7 . Our Herschel observations were preceded by two `orphan' gamma-ray flares. The near-infrared data reveal the high-energy cut-off in the main synchrotron component, which cannot be associated with the main gamma-ray component in a one-zone leptonic model. This is because in such a model the luminosity ratio of the External-Compton and synchrotron components is tightly related to the frequency ratio of these components, and in this particular case an unrealistically high energy density of the external radiation would be implied.

Therefore, we consider a well-constrained two-zone blazar model to interpret the entire dataset. In this framework, the observed infrared emission is associated with the synchrotron component produced in the hot-dust region at the supra-pc scale, while the gamma-ray emission is associated with the External-Compton component produced in the broad-line region at the sub-pc scale.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1510-089 as a highly variable flat-spectrum radio quasar, finding rapid gamma-ray variability and evidence for multiple emission regions along its relativistic jet.

quotes.quote: I present a study of the high-energy ray properties of the flat spectrum radio quasar, PKS 1510-089, based on 3.75 years of observations with the Large Area Telescope (LAT) detector on-board the Fermi ray Space Telescope. Throughout the observing period, the 0.1 GeV < E_ < 300 GeV ray flux was highly variable, undergoing several flaring events where the daily flux exceeded 10 ^ 5 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on 3 separate occasions. The increased photon statistics of these large flares allowed the observations to be re-analysed in 6 and 3 hour intervals, revealing flux doubling timescales as small as 1.3 0.12 hours during the flare rise time, and flux halving timescales of 1.21 0.15 hours during the flare decay.

The >10^ 5 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 flare events were also studied in more detail in an attempt to uncover evidence for the location of PKS 1510-089's ray emission region. In particular, two approaches were used: (i) searching for an energy dependence to the cooling timescales, and (ii) searching for evidence of a spectral cut-off. The combined results of these two approaches, along with the confirmation of 20 GeV photon flux from PKS 1510-089, suggest the presence of multiple ray emission regions being located in both the broad line region and molecular torus region of PKS 1510-089. An analysis of the highest photon events within the 3.75 year data set finds PKS 1510-089 to be a source of 20 GeV rays at the 13.5 confidence level; a observational property which is difficult to explain in the traditional view that ray emission from Active Galactic Nuclei originates from the base of the relativistic jet.

sources.summary: The paper reports H.E.S.S. detection of VHE emission from the quasar PKS 1510 089 and describes its redshift, bright thermal component, broad-line region, and multiwavelength variability.

quotes.quote: The quasar PKS 1510 089 ( z=0.361 ) was observed with the H.E.S.S. array of imaging atmospheric Cherenkov telescopes during high states in the optical and GeV bands, to search for very high energy (VHE, defined as E 0.1 TeV) emission. VHE rays were detected with a statistical significance of 9.2 standard deviations in 15.8 hours of H.E.S.S. data taken during March and April 2009. A VHE integral flux of I(0.15 TeV <E<1.0 TeV )= (1.0 0.2_ stat 0.2_ sys ) 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 is measured. The best-fit power law to the VHE data has a photon index of =5.4 0.7_ stat 0.3_ sys . The GeV and optical light curves show pronounced variability during the period of H.E.S.S. observations.

Unlike typical VHE-detected blazars where the broadband spectrum is dominated by non-thermal radiation at all wavelengths, the quasar PKS 1510 089 has a bright thermal component in the optical to UV frequency band. Among all VHE detected blazars, PKS 1510 089 has the most luminous broad line region (BLR).

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 1510-089 in a four-AGN Fermi LAT variability analysis with near-continuous detections, large gamma-ray flux variations, linear rms-flux behavior, and shot-noise-like PSDs.

quotes.quote: However, for detection and scientific analysis, only data points which satisfy the significance of 3 , corresponding to a TS value of 9 are considered. The 0.1-300 GeV ray light curves of the four AGNs: the FRI radio galaxy NGC 1275, BLL Mrk 421, FSRQ B2 1520+31, and FSRQ PKS 1510-089 between August 05, 2008 (MJD 54683) to October 05, 2015 (MJD 57300) over a uniform integration time of 3-days with detection significance above/equal 3 are shown in Figure fig:latLc . All the sources are detected over 97 e.g.

A different variability of PKS 1510-089 is due the fact that its flux distribution is bi-lognormal with equal variability in both 2016ApJ...822L..13K and consistent with values inferred for others here. Though the histograms are skewed/lognormal, the rms-flux relation is linear for all the sources with large scatters and a flux offset different from zero at the zero rms.

It should be noted that a Doppler boost ( ) of a few to ten can make the NGC 1275 flux ( ^3/distance^2 ) similar to the BL Lacs and FSRQs studied in this work, though the energetics and dynamics details may be different for each source. In this work, we explored the ray variability characteristics of four LAT AGNs: FR I NGC 1275, BLL Mrk 421, FSRQs B2 1520+31 and PKS 1510-089 with all having a near continuous detection in Fermi LAT over an integration time of 3-days.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1510-089 in a low gamma-ray state, detecting persistent VHE emission and modeling its broadband emission with an external-Compton region beyond the broad-line region.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510-089 \ is a bright flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) located at a redshift of z=0.36 ta96 . It was the second FSRQ to be detected in the very-high-energy (VHE, >100 GeV) range ab13 . The source is monitored by various instruments spanning the full range from radio up to VHE gamma rays see e.g. ma10, al14, ah17 .

The MAGIC telescopes collected 75 hrs of data during times when the \ flux measured above 1 GeV was below 3 10^ 8 cm^ 2 s^ 1 , which is the threshold adopted for the definition of a low gamma-ray activity state. The data show a strongly significant ( 9.5 ) VHE gamma-ray emission at the level of (4.27 0.61_ stat ) 10^ 12 cm^ 2 s^ 1 above 150 GeV, a factor 80 smaller than the highest flare observed so far from this object. Despite the lower flux, the spectral shape is consistent with earlier detections in the VHE band. The broad-band emission is compatible with the External Compton scenario assuming a large emission region located beyond the broad line region.

sources.summary: PKS 1510 08 was monitored during an exceptional high-energy flare; the paper reports large-amplitude variability on the second night and links INOV to high gamma-ray activity.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J1512.8 0906: We have monitored PKS 1510 08 during its exceptional flare in 2015 May (May 21 and 22). During this period of the high activity, it was significantly detected at very high energies (VHE, E>100 GeV) by MAGIC telescopes and the elevated activities were seen across the electromagnetic spectrum 2016arXiv161009416M,2016arXiv161102098Z , including the optical monitoring from the Steward observatory. The source is found to exhibit a large amplitude variability ( >15 ) on the second night of the observation (see Table tab:stat ). On the other hand, the length of the observations ( <2 hrs) during the first night was not enough to detect a significant INOV. The coincidence of the detection of the INOV with a high ray activity state (Figure fig_gamma1 ) again indicates a close association of detecting a high amplitude intra-night temporal variability during the high ray state see also for similar results 2014ApJ...789..143P,2016ApJ...819..121P .

Interestingly, on 2015 May 21, i.e., when we could not detect INOV, the noticed optical polarization was 7.4 and next day it increases to 15.3 . This indicates that on 2015 May 22, PKS 1510 08 was in even higher optically flaring state which resulted in the detection of a significant micro-variability in our 2.5 hrs of the night observations.

sources.summary: The paper reports 8.5 years of reverberation monitoring of PKS 1510-089, measuring variable broad lines and continuum, jet contamination, BLR lags near 60 days, and a 5.71 x 10^7 solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: Reverberation results of a flat spectrum radio quasar PKS 1510-089 are presented from 8.5-years long spectroscopic monitoring carried out in 9 observing seasons between December 2008 to June 2017 at Steward Observatory. Optical spectra show strong H , H , and Fe II emission lines overlaying on a blue continuum. All the continuum and emission line light curves show significant variability with a fractional root-mean-square variation of 37.30 0.06 ( f_ 5100 ), 11.88 0.29 (H ) and 9.61 0.71 (H ), however, along with thermal radiation from accretion disk non-thermal emission from jet also contribute to f_ 5100 .

PKS 1510–089 is a well-studied flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) located at a redshift z=0.361 1990PASP..102.1235T . Its optical spectrum shows broad emission lines with a blue continuum 1993MNRAS.263..999T . The broad-band spectral energy distribution (SED) of FSRQs has a double hump structure due to the combined effect of the thermal emission from accretion disk peaking at UV and optical, and non-thermal emission from the jets e.g., 2010ApJ...716...30A .

Using a scale factor of 4.47 and the _ line from rms spectrum which is constructed from the nightly spectrum after subtracting the power-law and Fe II, the black hole mass of PKS 1510-089 is found to be M_ BH =5.71^ +0.62 _ 0.58 10^ 7 M_ .

sources.summary: PKS 1510 089 is identified as a blazar dominating the all-sky neutrino prediction, with Fermi/LAT light curves and zoomed flare intervals used to mark promising flares.

quotes.quote: We further show that the blazars 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089 dominate the all-sky neutrino prediction from bright and short-term blazar flares.

Fermi/LAT γ-ray light curve for PKS 1510 089 in the energy range 100 MeV -- 300 GeV and a time range of 55317 MJD (2010-05-01) - 57662 MJD (2016-10-01) using daily binning. Highlighted flares (blue and gray) correspond to selected time intervals following the method of Sect. Flare_identification , while blue intervals indicate the most promising flares, as listed in Table Tab_neutrino_expectation . The red line corresponds to the identified ground level G.

Fermi/LAT first zoomed γ-ray light curve for PKS 1510 089 in the energy range 100 MeV -- 300 GeV using daily binning. Highlighted flares (blue and gray) correspond to selected time intervals following the method of Sect. Flare_identification , while blue intervals indicate the most promising flares, as listed in Table Tab_neutrino_expectation . The red line corresponds to the identified ground level G.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1510 089 as a bright FSRQ/blazar, analyzing multi-band variability, gamma-ray activity, monitoring data, and wavelength correlations from 2008-2018.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510 089 is a bright FSRQ located at redshift, z = 0.36   thompson.90.pasp, tanner.96.aj . It is one of the nine FSRQs detected in the very-high-energy (VHE 100 Gev) range by the Imaging Atmospheric Cherenkov Telescopes (IACTs) http://tevcat.uchicago.edu and also one of the persistent MeV-GeV emitters e.g. 2017ApJ...849..138K . The High Energy Spectroscopic System (H.E.S.S.) collaboration discovered VHE ray emission > 0.1 TeV from PKS 1510 089 in its 15.8 hours of observation (at 9.2 ) 2013A&A...554A.107H . The source emitted persistent VHE ray emission in MAGIC observations during its low flux state for an extended period of time during 2012 -- 2017 2018A&A...619A.159M .

Fig. fig:lc shows the multi-wavelength light curves of PKS 1510 089 between 2008 and 2018. The aggregated data show frequent changes in the fluxes in different electromagnetic (EM) bands, with the high (active or flaring) states of the ray fluxes shaded in purple (Fig.  fig:lc ); that shading is extended to the other bands for convenient visualization. To examine the apparent correlations between variations at different wavelengths in detail, we divide the complete data into four individual segments, denoted by vertical dashed lines, each of which has at least one high ray state and concludes in a gap in the optical/NIR corresponding to the end of an observing season.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1510-089 as a nearby quasar-hosted blazar detected by EGRET, monitored by Suzaku/Swift, with a broad-band SED used to study inverse-Compton emission.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510 089 is a nearby ( z = 0.361) QHB detected in the MeV GeV band by EGRET. It is a highly superluminal jet source, with apparent velocities of v_ app 10 observed in multi-epoch VLBA observations (e.g., ). Recent observations by BeppoSAX ( ) confirmed the presence of a soft X-ray excess below 1 keV, that may be among the best candidates for detecting the BC bump. The observational campaign of PKS 1510-089 in 2006 August commenced with a deep Suzaku observation lasting three days for a total exposure time of 120 ks, and continued with Swift monitoring over 18 days ( ).

Besides Swift observations, which sampled the optical/UV flux in all 6 UVOT filters as well as the X-ray spectrum in the 0.3 10 keV energy range, the campaign included ground-based optical and radio data, and yielded a quasi-simultaneous broad-band spectral energy distribution from 10 ^9 Hz to 10 ^ 19 Hz. Figure 3 ( left ) shows an overall SED of PKS 1510-089, whereas Figure 3 ( right ) shows in detail the optical--to--X-ray region of the SED.

sources.summary: For PKS 1510-089, the paper finds that a single non-thermal component cannot explain the March 2000 spectral variation, while an added thermal component can reproduce it.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510-089. The reported magnitude for this quasar is m_V=16.3 ( Xie01 ). On 2000 March 20, we measured m_V = 16.86 0.02 at the first data set. A power law with _ t_0 = -0.23 0.03 fits these data (Figures fig83a ).

However, the spectral variation cannot be explained appropriately only with a single non-thermal component (Figure fig83c ). Thus, a thermal component with T_ t_0 26,000 K and b_ V t_0 40 is added (Figure fig83b ). With this configuration only thermal variations can reproduce the observed behavior (Figure fig83d ).

sources.summary: The paper reports strong gamma-ray, optical, and submillimeter variability in PKS 1510 089, with SED modeling that includes disk, SSC, and external-Compton components.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510--089 PKS 1510 089 is another blazar that in the last three years showed high variability over all the electromagnetic spectrum, in particular high ray activity was observed by \ and . \ detected intense flaring episodes in August 2007 ( ) and March 2008 ( ) and an extraordinary actitivity during March 2009 ( ). During the period 1--16 March 2008, \ detected an average flux from PKS 1510 089 of (84 17) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for E > 100 MeV. The flux measured between 17 and 21 March was a factor of 2 higher, with a peak level of (281 68) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on 19 March 2008.

Moreover, between January and April 2008 the source showed an intense and variable optical activity with several flaring episodes of fast variability. A significant increase of the flux was observed also at submillimetric frequencies in mid April, suggesting that the mechanisms producing the flaring events in the optical and ray bands also interested the submillimetric zone, with a delay.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1510-089 as a TeV FSRQ, defining its states at 10 GeV, using literature SEDs, and fitting them with a synchrotron/SSC/external-Compton BLR model.

quotes.quote: Twelve blazars are included in our sample, two FSRQs (3C 279 and PKS 1510-089) and ten BL Lacs (W Com, Mkn 421, Mkn 501, PKS 2155-304, 1ES 1101-232, BL Lacertae, 1ES 2344+514, 1ES 1959+650, PKS 2005-489, S5 0716+714). The two well-sampled SEDs for a source are identified as a low or high state according to the observed or extrapolated flux density at 1 TeV, except for PKS 1510-089. Because the SEDs of PKS 1510-089 are cut off at 1 TeV, the high and low states of this source are defined by the flux density at 10 GeV. The observation data of the broadband SEDs for 3C 279 and PKS 1510-089 are from literature .

The radiation from a BLR is assumed to be a blackbody spectrum and the corresponding energy densities seen in the comoving frame are U^ ' _ BLR =5.37 10^ 3 ^ 2 erg cm ^ 3 and U^ ' _ BLR =4.98 10^ 3 ^ 2 erg cm ^ 3 for 3C 279 and PKS 1510-089, respectively, where we take = . Thus, the syn+SSC+EC/BLR model is used to fit the observed SEDs of the two FSRQs and the model can explain the observed SEDs well as shown in Fig. 1.

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 1510 089 among the main Fermi flare producers, finding generally short flares, mostly negative asymmetry, and a wide range of average photon indices.

quotes.quote: My sample of the brightest gamma-ray flares of blazars reported in Table tab_top40 is produced by only 5 blazars: 3C 454.3 (16 entries), PKS 1510 089 (14 entries), PKS 1222+216 (6 entries), 3C 273 (3 entries), and PKS 0402 362 (1 entry).

There appears to be no general trend between the flare duration and the peak flux. However, there is a significant difference between 3C 454.3 on one hand, and PKS 1510 089, PKS 1222+216 and 3C 273 on the other. Flares produced by 3C 454.3 are on average longer than those produced by other blazars. 3C 454.3 did not produce a flare shorter than 1.6 d , while other blazars generally produced flares shorter than 3 d , with the sole exception of flare 28 (PKS 1510 089 at MJD 55991). For the peak flux greater than 1.2 10^ 5 ph s^ 1 cm^ 2 , there is a complete separation of the two groups. Also, there is no discernible difference between the distributions of flares produced by PKS 1510 089, PKS 1222+216 and 3C 273.

Individual blazars produce flares of significantly different distributions of . 3C 454.3 produces flares characterised by 2.2< <2.45 , i.e. with a hardness typical for the whole sample and with a very small scatter. PKS 1510 089 produces flares with a wide range of average photon index, 2 2.6 . PKS 1222+216 produces relatively hard flares, with 1.9 2.25 , and 3C 273 produces relatively soft flares, with 2.4 .

sources.summary: The paper models an orphan gamma-ray flare in PKS 1510 089 as inverse-Compton emission from a blob crossing a shocked jet-sheath ring, supported by VLBA polarization evidence.

quotes.quote: Synthetic light curves produced by our model are compared to the observed light curves from an orphan flare that was coincident with the passage of a superluminal knot through the inner jet of the blazar PKS 1510 089. In addition, we present Very Long Baseline Array polarimetric observations that point to the existence of a jet sheath in PKS 1510 089, thus providing further observational support for the plausibility of our model. An estimate of the bolometric luminosity of the sheath within PKS 1510 089 is made, yielding L_ sh 3 10^ 45   erg   s^ 1 . This indicates that the sheath within PKS 1510 089 is potentially a very important source of seed photons.

We have stacked radio images of PKS 1510 089 obtained with the VLBA at 43 GHz at multiple epochs. We find the polarimetric signature of a sheath in our stacked map, thus lending observational support to the plausibility of this model of orphan ray flares within blazars.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1510-089 as a VHE-detected FSRQ, deriving BLR parameters from its SED and finding the EC-BLR emission region must lie near the BLR boundary.

quotes.quote: In the case of PKS 1510-089, to our knowledge, no value of the total luminosity of the BLR has been published. We therefore parameterize the luminosity of the BLR as a fraction f = 0.1 f_ 1 of the accretion disk, L_ BLR = f L_d . The accretion disk luminosity was determined by to be L_d = 1.0 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 . Characteristic SEDs of PKS 1510-089 e.g., Abdo10 indicate _ sy 3 10^ 12 Hz, _ EC 10^2 , and f_ EC / f_ sy 20 , for which Equation ublr yields u_ BLR = 4.5 10^ 3 erg cm ^ 3 , yielding a BLR radius of R_ BLR = 7.7 10^ 17 f_ 1 ^ 1/2 cm. The results for a fiducial value of f = 0.1 (i.e., BLR luminosity = 10 of the accretion disk luminosity) are illustrated in Figure PKS1510_01 .

Still, the same conclusion holds: If the GeV rays are produced by the EC-BLR mechanism, the ray emission region must be located near the outer boundary of the BLR, whereas for locations far beyond the outer boundary, the EC-BLR mechanism becomes implausible for the production of the observed GeV ray flux.

sources.summary: PKS 1510-089 is analyzed as a blazar/FSRQ whose infrared-optical and gamma-ray flux distributions split into high and low lognormal states, with energy-dependent dispersion and a harder high state.

quotes.quote: We present a systematic characterization of multi-wavelength emission from blazar PKS 1510-089 using well-sampled data at infrared(IR)-optical, X-ray and ray energies. The resulting flux distributions, except at X-rays, show two distinct lognormal profiles corresponding to a high and a low flux level. The dispersions exhibit energy dependent behavior except for the LAT ray and optical B-band. During the low level flux states, it is higher towards the peak of the spectral energy distribution, with ray being intrinsically more variable followed by IR and then optical, consistent with mainly being a result of varying bulk Lorentz factor. On the other hand, the dispersions during the high state are similar in all bands expect optical B-band, where thermal emission still dominates.

The centers of distributions are a factor of 4 apart, consistent with anticipation from studies of extragalactic ray background with the high state showing a relatively harder mean spectral index compared to the low state.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1510-089 during a 2015 high state, reporting MAGIC VHE variability, multiwavelength coverage, jet activity, and external-Compton SED modelling.

quotes.quote: galaxies: active – galaxies: jets – gamma rays: galaxies – quasars: individual: PKS 1510-089 \ is a bright flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) located at the redshift of z=0.36 ta96 . The source is one of only six objects firmly classified as a FSRQ from which gamma-ray emission has been detected in the very-high-energy (VHE, >100 GeV) range ab13 . Moreover, one of the highest recorded apparent speed of superluminal motion, up to 46 , has been seen in the ultrarelativistic jet of \ jo05 .

\ has been detected by MAGIC during a few day-long observations performed in the middle of a long, high optical and gamma-ray state, showing for the first time a significant VHE gamma-ray variability. Similarly to the optical and gamma-ray high state of the source detected in 2012, it was accompanied by a rotation of the optical polarization angle and the emission of a new jet component observed in radio.
We model the broadband SED in the framework of the external Compton scenario and discuss the possible emission site in view of multiwavelength data and alternative emission models.

sources.summary: The paper follows PKS 1510--089 through 2015 gamma-ray flares, finding delayed radio flaring, paired jet knots, edge-brightened polarization, and a layered, time-variable jet structure.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510--089 is a bright and active ray source that showed strong and complex ray flares in mid-2015 during which the Major Atmospheric Gamma Imaging Cherenkov telescopes detected variable very high energy (VHE; photon energies > 100 GeV) emission. We present long-term multi-frequency radio, optical, and ray light curves of PKS 1510--089 from 2013 to 2018, and results of an analysis of the jet kinematics and linear polarization using 43 GHz Very Long Baseline Array data observed between late 2015 and mid-2017. We find that a strong radio flare trails the ray flares in 2015, showing an optically thick spectrum at the beginning and becoming optically thin over time.

Two laterally separated knots of emission are observed to emerge from the radio core nearly simultaneously during the ray flares. We detect an edge-brightened linear polarization near the core in the active jet state in 2016, similar to the quiescent jet state in 2008--2013. These observations indicate that the ray flares may originate from compression of the knots by a standing shock in the core and the jet might consist of multiple complex layers showing time-dependent behavior, rather than of a simple structure of a fast jet spine and a slow jet sheath.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1510 089 as a variable FSRQ at z=0.361 powered by a massive black hole, with optical and gamma-ray behavior modeled through changing jet parameters.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510 089 It was identified as a quasar firstly by with a visual magnitude of 16.5 mag. It is one of the most variable FSRQs in the 3FGL catalog. Located at a redshift of z = 0.361 1996AJ....112...62T , it is powered by a black hole of mass 5.4 10 ^8 M _ and accretes at the rate of 0.5 M _ /year 2010ApJ...721.1425A .

It has been detected at very high energies by HESS 2013A&A...554A.107H and MAGIC (Major Atmospheric Gamma-Ray Imaging Cherenkov; 2014A&A...569A..46A ). This source has been studied for multi-wavelength flux variability 2017ApJ...844...62P,2013MNRAS.430.1324N as well as subjected to few SED modelling studies 2019ApJ...883..137P,2012ApJ...760...69N . Considering radio observations with the VLBA coupled with optical long term monitoring data argued for the presence of a binary black hole in PKS 1510 089.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first optical circular-polarization attempt for PKS 1510 089 in a high linear-polarization state, deriving upper limits that constrain magnetic-field strength and jet composition.

quotes.quote: We report on optical circular polarization observations of two famous blazars, namely 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089, at high linearly polarized states. This is the first time PKS 1510 089 is observed in optical circular polarization. While only upper limits can be extracted from our observing campaign, the non-detection of optical circular polarization allows us to provide meaningful constraints on their magnetic field strength and jet composition.

In this letter, we present optical CP observations of two well-studied, and highly polarized blazars namely 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089. Assuming an intrinsic CP signal exists, we use the detected upper limits in combination with their linear polarization degree to constrain their magnetic field strength and jet composition. 3C 279 and PKS 1510 089 are famous for their bright outbursts, high variable emission, extreme polarization behavior as well as potentially multimessenger emission Marscher2010,Abdo2010-III,Fermi2016,Kreter2020,Blinov2021 .

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1510-089 as a TeV FSRQ blazar, reporting 3.6-day and 92-day gamma-ray QPOs and considering jet, hotspot, and binary-black-hole explanations.

quotes.quote: We present periodicity search analyses on the ray lightcurve of the TeV blazar PKS 1510-089 observed by the Fermi Large Area Telescope. We report the detection of two transient quasi-periodic oscillations: a 3.6-day QPO during the outburst in 2009 that lasted five cycles (MJD 54906--54923); and a periodicity of 92 days spanning over 650 days from 2018 to 2020 (MJD 58200--58850), which lasted for seven cycles. We employed the Lomb-Scargle periodogram, Weighted Wavelet Z-transform, REDFIT, and the Monte Carlo lightcurve simulation techniques to find any periodicity and the corresponding significance.

PKS 1510-089 (R.A. = 15h 12m 52.2s, Dec. = -09 ^ 06' 21.6") belongs to the FSRQ subclass of blazars, and it is situated at a cosmological redshift of z = 0.361 . It is one of the most well-studied blazars and shows high variability across all electromagnetic wavebands 2018heas.confE..33Z . The H.E.S.S. telescope detected PKS 1510-089 in 2010 at TeV energies 2013A&A...554A.107H .

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 1510-089 as an FSRQ example, modelling its 2015 high optical and gamma-ray SED with synchrotron, SSC, EC, disc, and torus emission.

quotes.quote: We provide, in the online documentation tutorials and in the code associated with this publication, two examples using both gammapy version 0.18.2 gammapy_version and sherpa_1, sherpa_2 version v4.14.0 to wrap 's functions to fit the observed MWL SED of the blazars Mrk 421 (a BL Lac) and PKS 1510-089 (a flat spectrum radio quasar, FSRQ). While the low energy continuum of both source types is interpreted as due to synchrotron radiation of electrons, the high-energy one is commonly explained as due to SSC for BL Lacs and to EC (with a possible additional contribution from SSC process) for FSRQs. These two science cases offer us the possibility to test the fitting procedure with most of the radiative processes implemented in the package.

PKS 1510-089 The MWL SED of PKS 1510-089, characteristic of a high optical and gamma-ray state observed in 2015 ahnen_2017 was modelled with the synchrotron, SSC and EC radiation of a single blob accelerating a broken power-law EED. We included, in the total flux model, the disc and the DT thermal emissions since for FSRQs, while often outshined by synchrotron emission, they are still non-negligible. We considered the DT photon field as target for the EC and neglected any absorption on photon fields external to the blob.

sources.summary: The paper monitors blazar PKS 1510 089, linking its deep optical minima, radio jet structure, and periodicity to evidence for a binary black hole in the nucleus.

quotes.quote: Three deep flux minima were observed with nearly the same time-scales and intervals for the blazar PKS 1510 089 in the past few years. PKS 1510 089 has been the target of many monitoring programs e.g., villata97,xie02 . These minima have nearly the same time-scales ( 42 min) and intervals ( 336 14 d), and based on which, xie02 argued that the minimum was a periodic phenomenon and proposed that there was a binary black hole (BBH) system in the centre of PKS 1510 089.

Evidence for BBH from Radio Observations Evidence for BBH in PKS 1510 089 comes also from radio observations. PKS 1510 089 is a radio-loud source with high polarizations. Radio observations have revealed wiggling jets in PKS 1510 089 and provided another evidence for the BBH system.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1510-089 as the blazar exception to its optical/GeV flux correlation, with z=0.36 and broader scatter than the other sources.

quotes.quote: The only exception being PKS 1510-089, which lacks a clear correlation. Figure 3C454.3 shows the GeV flux versus the R-band flux for 3C 454.3 and PKS 2155-304, clearly showing the dichotomy as well as the correlation between GeV and optical fluxes; whereas figure PKS1510-089 shows PKS 1510-089, which shows no correlation.

The optically-faint and GeV-bright blazars all have redshift greater than 0.36 (that of PKS 1510-089), where as the optically-bright and GeV-faint blazars are all nearby with redshifts less than 0.158 (that of 3C 273).

A roughly linear correlation is found in the log-log representation of the flux comparison (i.e. a power law correlation is seen) for all sources except PKS 1510-089. This blazar shows a broader scatter than others.

sources.summary: The paper presents 0.3-40 keV spectra of PKS 1510 089, finding that a simple Galactic-absorption power law leaves low-energy excess requiring additional spectral modeling.

quotes.quote: PKS 1510 089 Figure fig:QHBspec shows the XISs and HXD/PIN spectra of PKS 1510 089 (including residuals), plotted against the best-fit power-law model with Galactic absorption, using the overall X-ray data between 0.3 and 40 keV. The best fit photon index is = 1.37 0.01 and the unabsorbed X-ray flux is F_ 2-10 keV = (6.31 0.07) 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . However, this model did not represent the spectra well yielding a chi-squared value of 1.06 for 407 dof. The residuals indicate some excess emission at low energies. To represent the observed X-ray spectra, we tried the same analysis as for PKS 0208 512.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1510 089 as a bright FSRQ, comparing March 2009 multiwavelength flares with external-Compton and SSC scenarios for its jet emission.

quotes.quote: We present results of modeling the broadband SED and multiwavelength variability of the bright FSRQ PKS 1510 089 with our time-dependent multizone Monte Carlo/Fokker-Planck code chen_etal:2011:multizone_code_mrk421 . As the primary source of seed photons for inverse Compton scattering, we consider radiation from the broad line region (BLR), from the hot dust of the molecular torus, and the local synchrotron radiation (synchrotron-self-Compton, SSC). We evaluate the viability of different Compton models by comparing simulated multiwavelength light curves and SEDs with one of the best observed flares by PKS 1510 089, in March 2009.

In this paper we extend the model to include external sources of IC seed photons and apply it to study the multiwavelength variability of an archetypical powerful FSRQ, PKS 1510 089. PKS 1510 089 is a FSRQ at a redshift of z = 0.361 thompson_etal:1990 . It is one of the brightest and most variable sources detected by /LAT. A feature that can be interpreted as disk emission (big blue bump, BBB) is clearly visible in its optical/UV spectrum. VLBI observations of its jet show superluminal motion with apparent speed up to 45 =36 , jorstad_etal:2005:VLBA . Since the advent of , the long-term multiwavelength monitoring effort, complemented by more intense campaigns motivated by flaring phases, has lead to the observation of several large correlated flares for PKS 1510 089 abdo_etal:2010:suzaku_obs_of_FSRQs, abdo_etal:2010:pks1510_multiwavelength_flares_2008_2009, dammando_etal:2011:pks1510_with_agile_march_2009, marscher_etal:2010:pks1510, kataoka_etal:2008:pks1510, pucella_etal:2008:pks1510 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1510-089 as a z=0.361 flaring FSRQ with strong variability, VHE gamma-ray detection, EVPA rotation, and a new jet component linked to shocks.

quotes.quote: The extraordinary case of the flaring blazar PKS 1510-089 (contribution) The FSRQ, PKS 1510-089 ( z=0.361 ), shows strong variability and highly superluminal jet components found close in time with gamma-ray flares. Moreover, it was detected at VHE gamma-rays, shows a high level of polarized emission, and a large rotation of the electric vector position angle (EVPA) close in time with a gamma-ray flare.
