quotes.quote: These are (see ): 32A, a QSO/Sy1 at z =1.33 (crosses), 12A a Sy2 at z =0.99 (filled circles) and 14Z, a very red object, probably a Sy2 galaxy, with z_ ph 1.6 .

The two absorbed ROSAT sources (12A and 14Z) have SEDs surprisingly similar to NGC 6240. The galaxy 12A may have a relatively stronger starburst component, indicated by its higher radio luminosity. The main difference lies in the X-ray range, where the luminosity at hard X-ray energies is very similar for all 3 sources, while the intrinsic absorption is clearly different: N_H is about 10^ 24 cm^ 2 for NGC6420, 3 10^ 22 cm^ 2 for 14Z and 10^ 22 cm^ 2 for 12A.

gmw_00000808.name: 14Z
gmw_00000808.overview: 14Z is a very red galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 1.6 [arXiv:astro-ph/0001360]. It is classified as a probable Seyfert 2 galaxy and exhibits a spectral energy distribution similar to NGC 6240 [arXiv:astro-ph/0001360].

sources.summary: The paper describes 14Z as a very red probable Seyfert 2 galaxy with a photometric redshift near 1.6 and an SED similar to NGC 6240 but lower X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: These are (see ): 32A, a QSO/Sy1 at z =1.33 (crosses), 12A a Sy2 at z =0.99 (filled circles) and 14Z, a very red object, probably a Sy2 galaxy, with z_ ph 1.6 .

The two absorbed ROSAT sources (12A and 14Z) have SEDs surprisingly similar to NGC 6240. The galaxy 12A may have a relatively stronger starburst component, indicated by its higher radio luminosity. The main difference lies in the X-ray range, where the luminosity at hard X-ray energies is very similar for all 3 sources, while the intrinsic absorption is clearly different: N_H is about 10^ 24 cm^ 2 for NGC6420, 3 10^ 22 cm^ 2 for 14Z and 10^ 22 cm^ 2 for 12A.

gmw_00000809.name: NGC 6552
gmw_00000809.overview: NGC 6552 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2209.01695, arXiv:2208.11620] that hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2209.01695, arXiv:1103.2181] with a hidden broad-line region [arXiv:1103.2181, arXiv:2208.11620]. Mid-infrared observations reveal a highly ionized nuclear outflow [arXiv:2209.01695] and emission from large polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon molecules [arXiv:2208.11620].

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/MIRI spectra of NGC 6552, identifying it as a Seyfert/Compton-thick AGN with a highly ionized nuclear outflow and warm molecular gas.

quotes.quote: During the commissioning of the James Webb Space Telescope ( JWST ), the Mid-Infrared Instrument (MIRI) observed NGC 6552 with the MIRI Imager and the Medium-Resolution Spectrograph (MRS). NGC 6552 is an active galactic nucleus (AGN) at redshift 0.0266 classified as a Seyfert 2 nucleus in the optical, and Compton-thick AGN in X-rays. This work exemplifies and demonstrates the MRS capabilities to study the mid-infrared (mid-IR) spectra and characterize the physical conditions and kinematics of the ionized and molecular gas in the nuclear regions of nearby galaxies MIRI Imager and MRS data were obtained with the aim to investigate persistence in the MIRI detectors, therefore these data were not optimized for scientific studies.

We obtained the nuclear, circumnuclear, and central mid-IR spectra of NGC 6552. They provide the first clear observational evidence for a nuclear outflow in NGC 6552. The outflow contributes to 68 6 of the total line flux independent of the ionization potential (27 to 187 eV) and critical densities (10 ^4 to 4 10 ^ 6 cm ^ 3 ), showing an average blue-shifted peak velocity of -126 44 km s ^ 1 and an outflow maximal velocity of 689 37 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6552 as a compact bright mid-infrared Seyfert target for MIRI persistence testing, finding its saturated nucleus left weak persistence that dissipated quickly.

quotes.quote: Data from the commissioning activity can be found in PID 1039 where the target chosen for the imager test was the Seyfert galaxy NGC 6552: a compact, bright, mid-infrared source in the Northern continuous viewing zone for JWST A paper detailing the results of the imaging and MRS spectroscopic data for NGC 6552 has been published by . . The Seyfert galaxy was observed for nearly 10 minutes in the same position on the detector. The bright nucleus saturated the pixels at the galaxy centre in less than 3 groups ( < 10 seconds).

These follow up images in Figure fig:Persistence_examples demonstrates that persistence from the galaxy nucleus is seen but at a very low level. The level of persistence seen 15 minutes after the galaxy was observed is < 0.01 of the flux of the target and appears to dissipate entirely by the next observation 30 minutes after the soak image ended.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6552 as an X-ray-luminous AGN, notes its Seyfert 2 classification, and describes ASCA and spectropolarimetric evidence for Compton-thick absorption and an HBLR.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, NGC 4388 and MCG 03-58-007 are Sy2/LINERs from their optical line ratios, NGC 6240 is a pure LINER and NGC 6552 has no line ratios available for classifications, but all of these sources are unambiguously powered by an AGN due to their X-ray luminosity.

NGC 6552 is classified as a Seyfert 2 however, by degrijp92 , though they do not present line ratio data. The Compton thick nature of NGC 6552 was serendipitously discovered by reynolds94 and fukazawa94 with an ASCA observation which shows a reflection dominated spectrum and it also has a HBLR revealed in optical spectropolarimetry tran01 .

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC6552 among Compton-thick AGN used for an infrared colour cut, noting its rising Spitzer mid-infrared continuum and lack of 9 micron coverage for silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:fig11 we have four CT AGN (NGC6240, NGC7479, NGC6552, NGC5643) that is used to define [ 9 m - 22 m ] > 2.0 colour range.

Spitzer low-resolution IRS-LL spectrum of NGC6552 shows a rising MIR continuum between 15-35 m Jarrett2011 . The Spitzer spectrum of NGC6552 do not cover 9 m range, therefore we do not have a measure for its silicate absorption strength.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6552 as a Sy2 galaxy whose JWST nuclear spectrum shows PAH bands; its nuclear PAH ratios indicate larger PAH molecules.

quotes.quote: NGC 6552 is a Sy2 galaxy with confirmed polarised (hidden) broad line region Tran01 .
It is evident from Fig. pah_profiles that the spectra of NGC 6552 and NGC 7469 show the 6.2, 7.7, 8.6, 11.3, and 12.7 m PAH emission features.
The values of the 6.2/7.7 m PAH ratios for the nuclear region of NGC 6552 are located in the region of large PAH molecules (N _ 400; see the top panel of Fig. pah_diagram ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6552 as object 3, a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose leaky-absorber X-ray spectrum requires heavy absorption, a narrow iron line, and modest intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: Objects 3 and 4 are described by the ``leaky-absorber" continua plus a narrow line(s). Object 3 is the well known Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 6552 at z=0.026. Its spectrum requires 1.4 , N_H 6 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , an uncovered fraction of 2 and a narrow Gaussian line at 6.4 keV with an equivalent width of 0.9 keV; its intrinsic luminosity is 6 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 (Fukazawa et al., 1994).

gmw_00000810.name: ESO 14--40
gmw_00000810.overview: ESO 14--40 is a low surface brightness galaxy that exhibits a spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0209157]. Dynamical models of its rotation curve indicate that it is a maximum-disk system with an unexpectedly high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0209157].

sources.summary: The paper includes this low surface brightness galaxy among four ESO systems whose spiral structure and rotation curves support maximum-disk dynamical models with unexpectedly high mass-to-light ratios.

quotes.quote: At the time of writing of their paper there was no surface photometry available for some of the LSBGs in the set of McGaugh et al. (2001), so that no dynamical models could be constructed. I have inspected the images of these galaxies and found four galaxies, ESO 14--40, ESO 206--140, ESO 301--120, and ESO 425--180, which can be used for the present purpose as well (cf. Fig. 1), and contrived to obtain surface photometry of the galaxies.

The rotation curves of the four ESO galaxies could be then interpreted following the prescription described in Fuchs (2002). The principal idea is that in galactic disks spiral arms are preferentially amplified with azimuthal wave lengths of the order of the critical wave length (cf. Fuchs 2002 and references therein). From the observed number of spiral arms one can thus estimate the critical wave lengths and from these the surface densities of the disks. This constrains the decomposition of the rotation curves considerably.

Similar to the other galaxies the inferred mass--to--light ratios of the four ESO galaxies turn out to be also much higher than expected from population synthesis models, although they range at the low end of the spectrum of mass--to--light ratios found for the sample of LSBGs studied here. A possible exception is ESO 302--120, which appears also to be extremely gas rich, M_ gas M_ disk . As can be seen from Table 1 all dynamical models are maximum disk models.

gmw_00000811.name: ESO 206--140
gmw_00000811.overview: ESO 206--140 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0209157] characterized as a nearly pure disk system [arXiv:1407.6044]. Its spiral structure and rotation curve support a maximum-disk dynamical model with a high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0209157].

sources.summary: The paper says ESO 2060140 favors the f(R) prediction over GR and is a nearly pure disk system useful for modified-gravity tests above its self-screening threshold.

quotes.quote: ESO 2060140: f(R) gravity agrees with the measurements more than GR. From table tab:fitcSIS it can be seen that this galaxy becomes self screened for f_ R0 < 7.39 10^ 7 . This galaxy is a pure disk system with B/T = 0.04, which makes the galaxy a good candidate for testing modified gravity.

gmw_00000814.name: NGC 2403
gmw_00000814.overview: NGC 2403 is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610114, arXiv:2405.13499] characterized by a flocculent, bulgeless stellar disk [arXiv:0909.1816, arXiv:2309.07480] and a flat rotation curve [arXiv:2005.11316, arXiv:1802.09967]. The galaxy features an extended, lagging extraplanar gas halo [arXiv:0802.0496, arXiv:1612.00477] that exhibits radial inflow toward the galactic center [arXiv:0802.0496, arXiv:0807.3365].

sources.summary: The paper applies the same accreting fountain model to NGC 2403, reproducing observed extra-planar gas inflow and deriving an accretion rate close to its star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Consider now the ability of the model that includes infall to reproduce the data for the moderately inclined galaxy NGC 2403. showed that the \ measurements of this galaxy reveal an extended layer of extra-planar gas that rotates less rapidly than the disc gas and flows in towards the centre of the galaxy. In FB06 we showed that pure fountain models in which the halo gas rotates less fast than the disc show outflow rather than inflow. Here we show that adding accretion similar to that derived for NGC 891 causes the model to predict inflow similar to that observed in NGC 2403. Fig. f_models2403 shows position-velocity plots along the minor axis of NGC 2403 (middle row) and parallel to this axis at offsets of 2' .

We see that the model with infall reproduces the data quite well. The required accretion rate is M _ inflow 0.8 . For comparison the SFR in NGC 2403 is 1.2 ken03 . fra01 have separated the so-called anomalous (extra-planar) gas in NGC 2403 from the disc gas with a technique described in fra02 . The resulting velocity field of the extra-planar gas (here shown in Fig. f_velfi , upper panel) shows a clear sign of radial inflow in that the kinematical minor axis is rotated counter-clockwise and not orthogonal to the major axis.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2403 is well fit by a flat axisymmetric model, with small perturbed velocities inconsistent with a bar-like halo distortion and a round-halo lower bound.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 Fig. n2403_res shows that a simple, flat, axisymmetric model is a good fit to the \ data for NGC 2403, consistent with the tiny variations in i PA reported by dBWB . The small residuals are somewhat less indicative of spiral streaming than in NGC 3198, and show hints of a more global N-S anti-symmetry at larger projected radii R_ 25 656 in the B-band dV91 . Our fitted values of V_ sys , i PA from a model that includes bisymmetric streaming velocities for R>450 , listed in Table n3198_pars , are in excellent agreement with those found by . Fig. n2403 shows that the perturbed velocities are 10 km/s, but the radial component is generally the larger, which is inconsistent with a non-rotating, bar-like distortion. Thus we again concur with \ that the \ data on this galaxy are consistent with it living in a perfectly round halo.

For NGC 2403, we find A_ tot varies from a maximum of A_ tot = 11.86 km s ^ 1 at 510 \ to A_ tot = 2.12 km s ^ 1 at 809 . Again assuming this spans a full range of possible phase differences and the spiral and halo contributions are separately roughly constant, then A_s 7.0 km s ^ 1 and A_h 4.9 km s ^ 1 . Using the values V_ 2, 4.9 / 1.8 km s ^ 1 and = 120 km s ^ 1 in formula ( potshape ), we again find q_ 0.98 and q_ 0.94 as our estimated lower limits on the axis ratios of the potential and density of the halo in NGC 2403.

sources.summary: The paper presents wide-field Subaru observations of NGC 2403, tracing its RGB-star surface-brightness profile and finding an extended, moderately metal-poor outer component beyond the inner disk.

quotes.quote: As part of our continuing program of ultra-deep imagery of galaxies beyond the Local Group, we present a wide-field analysis of the isolated late-type spiral NGC 2403 using data obtained with Suprime-Cam on the Subaru telescope. The surveyed area reaches a maximum projected radius of 30 kpc or deprojected radius of R_ dp 60 kpc. The colour-magnitude diagram reaches 1.5 mag below the tip of the metal-poor red giant branch (RGB) at a completeness rate > 50 for R_ dp 12 kpc.

Using the combination of diffuse light photometry and resolved star counts, we are able to trace the radial surface brightness (SB) profile over a much larger range of radii and surface brightness than is possible with either technique alone. The exponential disc as traced by RGB stars dominates the SB profile out to 8 disc scale-lengths, or R_ dp 18 kpc, and reaches a V band SB of _V 29 \ . Beyond this radius, we find evidence for an extended structural component with a significantly flatter SB profile than the inner disc and which we trace to R_ dp 40 kpc and _V 32 \ . This component can be fit with a power-law index of 3 , has an axial ratio consistent with that of the inner disc and has a V-band luminosity integrated over all radii of 1--7 that of the whole galaxy. At R_ dp 20 - 30 kpc, we estimate a peak metallicity [ M/H ]   = -1.0 0.3 assuming an age of 10 Gyr and zero element enhancement.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2403 needs either high M/L or distance for a good MOND rotation-curve fit; regular-distance/M/L and razor-thin stellar-disk models are unsatisfactory.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 In Fig fig:com2403 the contour plot of distance against M/L shows the requirement for a good fit to have either an excessively large M/L or distance. This is aggravated by the tight error on the distance of a mere 8 and the relatively low predicted M/L for the given stellar population. For instance, the predicted 3.6 m diet-Salpeter IMF M/L of NGC 2403 is 0.39, whereas for NGC 2903, 3521 and 3621 it is 0.61, 0.73 and 0.59 respectively. In the top left hand panel of Fig fig:com2403 we fit four curves to the observed rotation curve. The solid and dashed curves are the best fit models, which respectively use a >2 discrepant fitted distance and regular M/L , and a more regular distance but more discrepant M/L .

The other two lines are a model with the same parameters as the best fit, but using a razor thin stellar disk (dotted line), and one with a regular distance and M/L (dot-dashed line). Both of these are unsatisfactory fits. As with NGC 2903, this galaxy NGC 2403 can only be consistent with MOND if the cause of its higher than expected M/L is found, however, this is a much less significant problem than NGC 2903 because even with the required M/L for a good fit, it would still have a typical M/L compared to other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper improves NGC 2403's direct metallicity-gradient analysis from seven auroral-line H II regions, finding a direct oxygen gradient, linear N/O behavior, and constant alpha-element ratios.

quotes.quote: We have uniformly determined oxygen abundance metallicities for 14 H 2 regions in the local spiral galaxy NGC 628 and seven H 2 regions in NGC 2403. With high-quality spectroscopic observations, we measured the intrinsically faint [ O 3 ] 4363 and/or [ N 2 ] 5755 auroral lines at a strength of 3 or greater, and explicitly determine electron temperatures in all 14 H 2 regions in NGC 628. The seven H 2 regions in NGC 2403 were chosen to have strong auroral emission lines and, therefore, all have electron temperatures determined at a significance above 4 .

As all previous studies of NGC 2403 have relied primarily on strong-line abundances, with only a small number of direct abundances, the seven direct oxygen abundances presented here allow an improved metallicity gradient analysis. The result from these H II regions, which extend from a galactocentric radius of 0.96 to 10.4 kpc, is an oxygen abundance gradient of 12 + log(O/H) = (8.48 0.04) + (-0.032 0.007) R_g (dex/kpc), with a modest dispersion of = 0.07 dex. Additionally, we measure the absolute and relative N, S, Ne, and Ar Abundances.

For the N/O ratio we find a negative gradient with increasing galactocentric radius in the inner disks of both NGC 628 and NGC 2403. Further out in the outer disk, this relationship flattens out in NGC 628, where we find a plateau past R_g = R_ 25 near an oxygen abundance of 12+log(O/H) = 8.3. Similar trends are seen in other spiral galaxies, where the R_ 25 radius and oxygen abundances of 12+log(O/H) = 8.3 and greater mark points of transition in nitrogen production. NGC 2403, and other previously studied galaxies, are best fit with a single linear fit. However, in theses cases, the galaxies do not have observations in their extended disks beyond R_ 25 and therefore their mechanisms of nitrogen production are not readily comparable to galaxies with observations of greater radial coverage. As expected for process elements, S/O, Ne/O, and Ar/O appear to be constant over a range in oxygen abundance in both NGC 628 and NGC 2403 such that the elements and O are produced in lock-step.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 as a classic high-surface-brightness spiral whose flat rotation curve is well recovered by the maximum-disc algorithm, implying a nearly maximal disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 fig:LSBHSBsidebyside (left) shows NGC 2403 which is a classic example of an HSB spiral galaxy 1986RSPTA.320..447V, 1997MNRAS.290..533D, 2002AJ....123.3124F . NGC 2403 has an archetypal flat rotation curve. It also displays a characteristic feature at R 3 kpc, which is known to be reproduced by maximum disc fits 1986RSPTA.320..447V, 2002AJ....123.3124F . Our automatic algorithm provides the same result, so it can recover well classical visual fits with no manual intervention.

The baryonic and observed rotation curves diverge past the plateau, pointing to a significant dark matter contribution beyond this radius. Since typical dark matter models do not contain central density holes, this maximal scaling is not physical but does provide a hard upper limit to the mass-to-light ratio of the disc. We find = 0.88 0.02 M _ / L _ , which is acceptable in terms of stellar populations albeit slightly higher than the SPARC population synthesis (hereafter pop-synth) value of 0.5 M _ / L _ . NGC 2403 likely has a nearly maximal disc.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2403 as a rotation-curve test case, finding percent-level differences between full and simple superfluid-dark-matter calculations and a minimum allowed superfluid mass.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  fig:fullsimple-NGC2403 , left, we show the rotation curve and /m for NGC2403 for the different types of calculation described above. The calculations differ by a few percent at intermediate radii. The full+nocurl rotation curve lies pretty much on top of the simple rotation curve, while the ``full'' rotation curve differs from the two others at intermediate radii. Thus, the no-curl approximation is the source of the this difference between the full and the simple calculations.

This is illustrated in Fig.  fig:smallfMDM-NGC2403 for NGC2403. The top panel shows solutions /m for various boundary conditions . The bottom panel shows _* for the same boundary conditions. Both panels also show the lower bounds on _* and , respectively, which ensure _ SF > 0 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2403's neutral extraplanar gas as fountain material plus cooling CGM accretion, reproducing the H I distribution and finding outflow, accretion, and disc-growth rates consistent with sustaining star formation.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We use a dynamical model of galactic fountain to study the neutral extraplanar gas (EPG) in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2403. We have modelled the EPG as a combination of material ejected from the disc by stellar feedback (i.e. galactic fountain) and gas accreting from the inner circumgalactic medium (CGM). This accretion is expected to occur because of cooling/condensation of the hot CGM (corona) triggered by the fountain. Our dynamical model reproduces the distribution and kinematics of the EPG H i emission in NGC 2403 remarkably well and suggests a total EPG mass of 4.7^ +1.2 _ 0.9 10^8 M_ , with a typical scale height of around 1 kpc and a vertical gradient of the rotation velocity of 10.0 2.7 km s^ 1 kpc^ 1 .

The best-fitting model requires a characteristic outflow velocity of 50 10 km s^ 1 . The outflowing gas starts out mostly ionised and only becomes neutral later in the trajectory . The accretion rate from the condensation of the inner hot CGM inferred by the model is 0.8 M _ yr ^ 1 , approximately equal to the star formation rate in this galaxy (0.6 M _ yr ^ 1 ). We show that the accretion profile, which peaks at a radius of about 4.5 kpc, predicts a disc growth rate compatible with the observed value.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2403's asymmetric HI line profiles as evidence for a vertically extended, slowly rotating HI layer tied to disk-halo circulation and possible fountain flow.

quotes.quote: The neutral hydrogen line profiles along the major axis of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2403 show a wing towards the systemic velocity. This asymmetry can be explained with the presence of an abnormally thick HI disk (FWHM 5 kpc) or with a two-component structure: a thin disk and a slowly rotating, thicker (1-3 kpc) HI layer. The latter model gives a better representation of the observations. These results throw a new light on the disk-halo connection in spiral galaxies. In particular, the decrease of rotational velocity with height above the plane may be the result of a galactic fountain flow.

The galaxy studied here, NGC 2403, has an intermediate inclination (i= 61^ ). The consequence is that the measured line-of-sight velocities are a combination of rotational, radial and vertical motions and the column densities are integrated along an oblique line-of-sight. Therefore, the interpretation is less straightforward than in face-on or edge-on galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA H I observations of NGC 2403, describing its extended rotating H I layer, anomalous gas component, slower rotation, inflow, and likely galactic-fountain connection to disk star formation.

quotes.quote: High sensitivity H I observations of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2403 obtained with the VLA are presented and discussed. The properties of the extended, differentially rotating H I layer with its H I holes, spiral structure and outer warp are described. In addition, these new data reveal the presence of a faint, extended and kinematically anomalous component. This shows up in the H I line profiles as extended wings of emission towards the systemic velocity. In the central regions these wings are very broad (up to 150 km s ^ 1 ) and indicate large deviations from circular motion.

The rotation velocity of the anomalous gas is 25 50 km s ^ 1 lower than that of the disk. Its velocity field has non-orthogonal major and minor axes that we interpret as due to an overall inflow motion of 10 20 km s ^ 1 towards the centre of the galaxy. The picture emerging from these observations is that of a cold H I disk surrounded by a thick and clumpy H I layer characterized by slower rotation and inflow motion towards the center.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 2403's sub-kpc FIR-radio correlation, finding H II-region q70 peaks, less-active disk behavior, and longer smoothing scales for matching infrared and radio maps.

quotes.quote: In this initial study, we examine the variations of the FIR-radio correlation on sub-kpc scales within four of the nearest face-on galaxies in the SINGS sample: NGC 2403, NGC 3031 (M81), NGC 5194 (M51a), and NGC 6946. Using high resolution Spitzer imaging, we are also able to test the smearing model of with greater accuracy, at higher spatial resolution, and in more galaxies, with the aim of gaining better insight into CR e^ diffusion and confinement within galaxy disks.

For NGC 2403, which does not have a grand-design spiral morphology, we still see q_ 70 peaks associated with H II regions. To quantify radial variations in q_ 70 , along with any dependencies of q_ 70 on the 70 m surface brightness within each galaxy, we perform aperture photometry using apertures having diameters equal to the FWHM of the MIPS 70 m PSF (i.e. 17''). The measured dispersion of q_ 70 within each galaxy is less than 0.25 dex.

Figure res70 shows that scale-lengths < 1 kpc work best to improve the correlation for the more active star-forming galaxies (NGC 5194 and NGC 6946: SFR 4 M _ /yr), while scale-lengths > 1 kpc work best for the less active star-forming galaxies (NGC 2403 and NGC 3031: SFR < 1M _ /yr). We also find that by smearing the 70 m images, we are able to reduce the slope of the observed non-linearity in q_ 70 with 70 m surface brightness by > 25 for each galaxy. This suggests that the non-linearity may be due to the diffusion of CR e^ s and is not expected to be found in the global correlation when integrating the flux over entire galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2403 with NGC 891, describing it as a nearby inclined Sc galaxy whose lagging extra-planar neutral gas shows evidence for inward flow.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 is a nearby Sc galaxy located at a distance of 3.2 Mpc fre01 . NGC 2403 is much less luminous ( L_ B =8.2 10^9 ) and less massive than NGC 891 and is in several respects similar to M33 in the Local Group. It has a fairly high SFR with very bright \ regions dri99 and a global SFR rate of 1.2 ken03 .

NGC 2403 is inclined at 63 rather than being edge-on. This intermediate inclination has the disadvantage that we cannot directly determine the distance from the plane at which emission occurs. It has, however, the advantage that it enables us to distinguish radial outflow from inflow. Recent \ observations of NGC 2403 have revealed a component of neutral gas at lower rotation velocities ( 20-50 ) with respect to the rotation of the thin disc sch00,fra01 . Three-dimensional modelling of the \ data cube has shown that this component is produced by lagging extra-planar gas as observed in edge-on galaxies, and revealed evidence that the extra-planar gas is flowing inward fra01 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 2403's lagging halo gas, a filament outside the bright disk, and a fountain-plus-accretion model that reproduces its radial inflow.

quotes.quote: On the right panel of Fig. \ 891and2403 , the position-velocity (p-v) plot along the major axis of the galaxy NGC 2403 (inclination 60 ) obtained with the VLA ( ). A broad component of gas at rotation velocities lower than the disk gas (also called the ``beard'') is clearly visible at low emission levels (light grey). This beard component is the halo gas in NGC 2403.

NGC 2403 also has a filament with a similar \ mass located in projection outside the bright optical disk and clearly separated in velocity from the disk kinematics (Fig. \ fil2403 ). They are both very similar to Complex C in our Galaxy.

For NGC 891 we found a best-fit accretion rate of about 3 (see the blue curves in Fig. \ indirect ) and for NGC 2403: 0.8 . In NGC 2403, this model is also able to reproduce the observed radial inflow of the halo gas (see ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2403 differs from the other resolved sample galaxies: CO poorly traces cold dust, FIR-linked attenuation is offset from molecular clouds, and optical-depth trends are steeper.

quotes.quote: The particular case of NGC 2403 is interesting in that it shows an opposite behaviour compared to the other elements of the sample. found that in NGC 2403 the CO emission does not correlate well with tracers of cold dust when examined as small spatial scales, which may partly explain the absence of a good correlation between the molecular gas and the attenuation. Along this line, a recent study of has shown that NGC 2403 has a thermal component that is not heated by the star forming regions.

In NGC 2403 however the dust attenuation associated with FIR emission is not in the same place as the molecular clouds, which causes the relation to break down. Though, since the FIR emission in NGC 2403 is at least partly from dust absorbing light in the diffuse interstellar medium (ISM), since the atomic gas is also in the diffuse ISM and not in star forming regions, the dust attenuation appears correlated with atomic gas.

The slope is relatively similar for NGC 4254, NGC 4321, and NGC5194, but it is about twice as steep for NGC 2403. Conversely, NGC 2403 levels at a much lower face--on optical depth than average, whereas NGC 4254 and NGC 4321 level at a higher face--on optical depth.

sources.summary: The paper deconvolves the NGC2403 VLA HI tutorial dataset, giving its visibility count, channel setup, angular size, image grid, flagging, and processing time.

quotes.quote: NGC2403 As a larger test, we deconvolve the NGC2403 dataset that is also distributed with a tutorial http://casa.nrao.edu/Doc/Scripts/ngc2403 tutorial.py for the CASA software. This dataset has 432,783 visibility records for 127 channels starting at 1418.25MHz with a channel bandwidth of 24.414kHz taken by the VLA. The synthesised beam size is around 12 arc seconds and the object is around 35 arc minutes across. For this test, the image is deconvolve onto a 1024 1024 pixel grid with a pixel size of 2 arc seconds.

This dataset includes some records affected by interference, and the noisy records were flagged and removed before deconvolution. As per the CASA tutorial, calibration and continuum subtraction were performed, with channels 21-30 and 92-111 used for continuum estimation. The execution time for a single channel for this dataset is 1.5 hours, and the reduction time for the 61 line channels was around 90 hours.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 2403 as above the usual TS threshold but only 2.55 significant after background modeling, far off the FIR-gamma correlation and possibly a fluctuation or non-SFR source.

quotes.quote: In addition to the SFGs identified in the 3FGL, our analysis identifies the systems NGC 2403 and NGC 3424 to have ray emission with a statistical significance that exceeds the TS = 25 barrier typically used for source identification, with TS values of 37.5 and 30.9 respectively. However, by using P _ bg to determine the possibility that these systems are the result of background fluctuations, we find that the statistical significance of these systems is only 2.55 and 2.36 , respectively. Given that we analyze 584 SFGs, it is reasonable that two false detections would be found at these significances.

The systems NGC 3424 and NGC 2403 lie far off of the FIR to ray correlation, providing some evidence that these systems either stem from background fluctuations, or are ray bright for reasons unrelated to their star-formation rate. We note that Equation eq:theequation is resilient to the significant mismodeling of these systems. Since they lie far off of the FIR to ray correlation, no values of , , and are likely to provide a good fit. Thus, they have only a marginal influence on our best-fit parameters.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2403 as a nearby pure-disc benchmark whose disc assembled over half its stellar mass since z<1 and has a metallicity gradient similar to NGC 300.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403, NGC 300 and M33 are three nearby pure-disc galaxies with similar stellar mass in different environments, they are benchmarks for understanding late-type spiral galaxies in different environments. The chemical evolution and growth of their discs are investigated by using the simple chemical evolution model, in which their discs are assumed to originate and grow through the accretion of the primordial gas, and the gas outflow process is also taken into account. Through comparative study of the best-fitting model predicted star formation histories for them, we hope to derive a picture of the local environment on the evolution and star formation histories of galaxies and whether or not the isolated galaxies follow similar evolution history.

Our results show that these three galaxies accumulated more than 50 percent of their stellar mass at z < 1 . It can be also found that the metallicity gradients in isolated spiral galaxies NGC 2403 and NGC 300 are similar and obviously steeper than that in M33, when the metallicity gradients are expressed in dex R_ 25 ^ 1 . The similar metallicity gradients in NGC 2403 and NGC 300 indicate that they may experience similar chemical evolutionary histories. The principal epoch of star formation on the discs of NGC 2403 and NGC 300 is earlier than that on the disc of M33, and the mean age of stellar populations along the discs of both NGC 2403 and NGC 300 is older than that of M33.

sources.summary: NGC 2403 has reported, possibly variable GeV emission coincident with SN 2004dj; the model spectrum is shown, but star-formation activity is unlikely to explain the observed flux.

quotes.quote: Among those added eight galaxies, M33 and NGC 2403 have been already detected by Fermi LAT Xi2020,Ajello2020 . Especially, NGC 2403 is interesting because its emission is decaying with time and this emission is coincident with SN 2004dj.

Recently detection of GeV gamma rays from NGC 2403 by Fermi LAT was reported Xi2020b , and the gamma-ray flux seems variable. Then it was proposed that this gamma-ray emission is coming from a coincident supernova 2004dj. However, flux variability is statistically marginal, and a possibility of gamma-ray emission from other origins remains. Therefore we show here the gamma-ray spectrum of NGC 2403 predicted by our model in Figure fig:spectrum_examples . The observed flux is about an order of magnitude higher than the expected flux, and it is unlikely that star-formation activity and cosmic-ray interaction explain the observed flux.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 2403 globular clusters, portraying the galaxy as isolated and bulgeless with an undisturbed H I disk, inner-halo/disk clusters, and extended cluster formation.

quotes.quote: The globular cluster (GC) systems of low-mass late-type galaxies, such as NGC 2403, have been poorly studied to date. As a low mass galaxy (M _ = 7 10 ^ 9 M _ ), cosmological simulations predict NGC 2403 to contain few, if any, accreted GCs. It is also isolated, with a remarkably undisturbed HI disk. Based on candidates from the literature, Sloan Digital Sky Survey and Hyper Suprime-Cam imaging, we selected several GCs for follow-up spectroscopy using the Keck Cosmic Web Imager.

The general rotation of the stellar and HI disk from 2002AJ....123.3124F shows that the SE side of NGC 2403 is receding at 250 km ^ 1 while the NW side is `approaching' us at 10 km s ^ 1 (the maximum disk rotation velocity is around 130 km s ^ 1 ). The mean RV of NGC 2403 is 133 km s ^ 1 . On the basis of measured velocities relative to the galaxy's velocity field we suggest that D6, F1 and JD1 (and F46) are GCs likely associated with the halo rather than the disk of NGC 2403.

sources.summary: The paper presents new H I observations of NGC 2403, finding an extended filamentary complex likely produced by a recent interaction with DDO 44 rather than a fountain.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: 3-hours centred on the galaxy ( =7 h 36 m 44 s , =65^ 35'35'' )

quotes.quote: We present new neutral hydrogen (H i ) observations of the nearby galaxy NGC 2403 to determine the nature of a low-column density cloud that was detected earlier by the Green Bank Telescope. We find that this cloud is the tip of a complex of filaments of extraplanar gas that is coincident with the thin disk. The total H i mass of the complex is 2 10^ 7 M _ or 0.6 of the total H i mass of the galaxy. The main structure, previously referred to as the 8-kpc filament, is now seen to be even more extended, along a 20 kpc stream.

NGC 2403 is a member of the M81 group, located at a distance of 3.18 Mpc madore91 ( 1'=0.93 kpc, 1''=0.015 kpc) with a systemic velocity of 133.2 km s ^ 1 fraternali02 . The main H i disk is inclined by 63° fraternali02 and has a mass of 3.24 10^ 9 M _ fraternali02 . We obtained new H i radio observations of NGC 2403 and the GBT cloud in order to determine the nature of this cloud and explore its connection with the previously reported 8-kpc anomalous velocity filament fraternali02 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2403 as a nearby late-type spiral whose compact stellar nucleus hosts a highly variable X-ray source; its nuclear cluster is bright, extended, massive, and intermediate age.

quotes.quote: Discovery of a Transient X-ray Source in the Compact Stellar Nucleus of NGC 2403 Mihoko Yukita 1 , Douglas A. Swartz 2 , Roberto Soria 3 , and Allyn F. Tennant 4 1 University of Alabama in Huntsville, Dept. of Physics, Huntsville, AL, USA 2 Universities Space Research Association, NASA Marshall Space Flight Center, VP62, Huntsville, AL, USA 3 Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics, Cambridge, MA, USA 4 Space Science Department, NASA Marshall Space Flight Center, VP62, Huntsville, AL, USA We report the discovery of an X-ray source coincident with the nuclear star cluster at the dynamical center of the nearby late-type spiral galaxy . The X-ray luminosity of this source varies from below detection levels, 10 ^ 35 \ in the 0.5-8.0 keV band, to 7 10 ^ 38 \ on timescales between observations of < 2 months. The X-ray spectrum is well-fit by an accretion disk model consisting of multiple blackbody components and corresponding physically to a compact object mass of 5 . No pulsations nor aperiodic behavior is evident in its X-ray light curve on the short timescales of the individual observations.

The X-ray properties of the source are more similar to those of the nuclear source X-8 in M33, believed to be a low-mass X-ray binary, then to those of the low-luminosity active galactic nucleus in NGC 4395. The brightness of the nuclear star cluster, M_I 11.8 mag, is typical of clusters in late-type spirals but its effective radius, r_e 12 pc, is several times larger than average indicating a relatively relaxed cluster and a low probability of a central massive object. The cluster has a mass 10 ^ 6.5 \ and an age of 1.4 Gyr estimating from its observed colors and brightness.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 distance and H I velocity-field measurements to show that DDO 44 and its H-alpha clump do not fit NGC 2403 peripheral disk kinematics.

quotes.quote: A dwarf spheroidal galaxy DDO 44 is located at 79 arcmin in the direction of NNW from the Sc galaxy NGC 2403. The distance to it, based on the tip of the red giant branch was measured by k99,alo06,dal09 . The mean distance value amounts to 3.20 0.10 Mpc, which almost coincides with the distance estimate of 3.13 0.10 Mpc to NGC2403 from the Cepheids fre01 .

The field of radial velocities in NGC 2403 was constructed by beg87 based on observations in the neutral hydrogen 21 cm line. The resulting map of the velocity field extends to the distance of 18 arcmin from the center of NGC 2403, i.e. about a quarter of its distance to DDO 44. From these data, the radial velocities in the gas disk of NGC 2403 in the direction towards DDO 44 show a systematic decrease from the value of +133 to +60 km ^ 1 . Therefore, the radial velocity of the clump in DDO 44 that we measured in no way fits into the kinematics of the peripheral gas disk of NGC 2403, and can obviously be attributed to the dSph system itself.

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 2403 lacks an identified bar; its small flocculent bulge, low B/T, and non-flattened isophotes support treating the central component as a bulge.

quotes.quote: In NGC 2403 we do not identify a bar. The galaxy is flocculent, and morphologically similar to M 33. The bulge in NGC 2403 is small. It has B/T 0.05 and the radial size of the bulge of is roughly 4 of the disk scale length. Also, the isophotes of NGC 2403 do not become flatter (that is more bar like) in the bulge region.
We feel this small size and isophote shape likely indicate that this is a bulge and not a bar.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2403 as an intermediate spiral galaxy, comparing MDM and CDM rotation-curve fits and predicting a smaller escape-speed discrepancy than for dwarf galaxies.

quotes.quote: We then construct in Section 3 CDM models for the galaxies NGC 2403, DDO 170 and DDO 154 (whose rotational curves have previously been modelled using MDM), and calculate and compare escape speeds from the stellar disc as a function of galactic radius for both types of model. We show that the MDM models predict specific values for the escape speeds that are generally different from those in the CDM models.

As in the BPR model, we use data from , , and for the intermediate spiral galaxy NGC 2403 and the dwarf galaxies DDO 170 and DDO 154. Again assuming a constant mass-to-light ratio for each galaxy and taking M_ L and r_ 1,d as fixed parameters, we fit the rotational curves of these three disc galaxies by using , v_ max and r_ s as fit parameters.

The offset between the two models appears to be nearly constant out to the truncation distance. It is both absolutely and relatively larger for the dwarf galaxies ( v 30 kms^ 1 and v/v_ CDM(IT) 0.2 ) than for NGC 2403 ( v 10 kms^ 1 and v/v_ CDM(IT) 0.03 ).

sources.summary: The paper recalibrates NGC 2403 ancillary imaging, verifies many diagnostic regions with HST images, and notes larger luminosity uncertainties from its distance estimate.

quotes.quote: This was not the case with NGC 2403 ancillary data from SINGS, where astrometry of the published R-band continuum image was incorrect. We used GAIA software to rederive astrometry for both images by matching the stars to the Guide Star Catalog II GSC_2 . We then subtracted the R band continuum image, scaled by a uniform factor k_R , off the narrow band image to correct for the presence of continuum.

NGC 2403 As explored in Section section_sample , this paper presents a new continuum-subtracted image of this galaxy, following an astrometry recalibration. A large subsample of regions within the diagnostic regime were verified against HST images. This galaxy has larger than average uncertainties on individual regions' luminosities, arising primarily due to a significant uncertainty in the distance estimate, following the study of Freedman2001 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2403 among seven nearby galaxies and publishes its Spitzer point-source catalog for the first time using full SINGS mosaics.

quotes.quote: We present Spitzer IRAC 3.6-8 and MIPS 24 point-source catalogs for seven galaxies: NGC 6822 , M 33 , NGC 300 , NGC 2403 , M 81 , NGC 0247 , and NGC 7793 . The catalogs contain a total of 300,000 sources and were created by dual-band selection of sources with >3 detections at both 3.6 and 4.5 . The source lists become significantly incomplete near m_ 3.6 =m_ 4.5 18 . We complement the 3.6 and 4.5 fluxes with 5.8 , 8.0 and 24 fluxes or 3 upper limits using a combination of PSF and aperture photometry.

Spitzer point-source catalogs of NGC 6822 D 0.46 Mpc, ref:Gieren_2006 , NGC 2403 D 3.1 Mpc, ref:Saha_2006 , NGC 0247 D 3.6 Mpc, ref:Madore_2009 and NGC 7793 D 4.1 Mpc, ref:Tully_2009 are being published here for the first time.
For NGC 6822, NGC 2403, M 81, and NGC 7793, we used the mosaics from the SINGS survey ref:Kennicutt_2003 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2403 in a very-low-resolution H I test, using GBT and THINGS data and only the receding disk half because the other half is contaminated by Milky Way emission.

quotes.quote: We selected 4 galaxies (NGC 2403, NGC 2903, NGC 3198 and NGC 5055) for which we have both very high resolution \ data and THINGS Fraternali+02 and single-dish observations. Effelsberg data for NGC 3198 and NGC 5055 were kindly provided by B. Winkel Winkel+12,Winkel+12_2 . NGC 2403 deBlok2+14 and NGC 2903 (Pisano et al., in prep.) where observed with the Green Bank Telescope (GBT) and kindly supplied by D. \ J. \ Pisano. We compared high-resolution rotation velocities and velocity dispersions obtained with \ with the low-resolution ones derived both with 2D and 3D approaches.

For NGC 2403, we used only the receding half of the disc, since the approaching half is contaminated by \ emission from the Milky Way. Unlike the 2D approach, \ recovers correct rotation velocities for all these galaxies in every location of the disc.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 2403 with NFW and ISO halos, tests one- and two-component stellar decompositions, and finds molecular gas contributes little to the dynamics or fitted rotation curves.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 In Figures fig:N2403-NFW and fig:N2403-ISO we plot the results of the mass modelling for NGC 2403. NFW and ISO halo models were fitted to the THINGS H i observed rotation curve. The stellar mass distribution can be described by either a 1- or 2-component decomposition.

We fit halo rotation curves using both stellar distributions, treating the 1- and 2-component models separately. 2013APJ...777....5S did not solve for a 2g value for NGC 2403. We therefore only consider a predicted molecular gas rotation curve using .

sources.summary: The paper studies 11 H II regions in nearby spiral NGC 2403, using spatially resolved spectra to test oxygen-abundance diagnostics across varying ionization states.

quotes.quote: This paper presents a spectroscopic investigation of 11  regions in the nearby galaxy NGC 2403. The  regions are observed with a long-slit spectrograph mounted on the 2.16 m telescope at XingLong station of National Astronomical Observatories of China. For each of the  regions, spectra are extracted at different nebular radii along the slit-coverage.

As an ideal target for our work, NGC 2403 is a late-type spiral (Sc III) galaxy and is rich in  regions 1983AJ.....88..296H, 1990A&A...237...23S, 1997ApJ...489...63G , which guarantees sufficient candidates of proper objects for sample selection. The location of NGC 2403 is of particular advantage for the long-slit spectrometry.

In a short summary, through spatially resolved spectrophotometry of 11  regions in NGC 2403 and thereby naturally sampling various ionization states, our study corroborates the theoretical expectation and offers the observational evidence of the similarities and differences between the empirical diagnostics of the oxygen abundance.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2403 as the prototypical slow-rotating, inflowing EPG case, notes foreground contamination and a bright anomalous filament, and finds similar conclusions to prior VLA work.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403. The anomalous gas of this galaxy has been studied in detail first by Schaap+00 and then by Fraternali+02 , and represents the prototypical case for a slow rotating and inflowing EPG. We compare our findings with those of Fraternali+02 in Section ssec:previous . The overall \ emission is contaminated by the Galactic \ foreground in a few channels at negative velocities, which we have blanked (white horizontal stripes). Also here a prominent filament is visible in minor axis pv-slices at offsets 0 and along the major axis see also deBlok+14 . As expected, the filament is not reproduced by our model.
We used the same data as in Fraternali+02 and reached very similar conclusions.

sources.summary: The paper computes NGC 2403 gamma-ray and radio fluxes, treating its extension explicitly and excluding unrelated patchy emission before using it in luminosity-SFR correlations.

quotes.quote: For two objects, M33 and NGC 2403 , data in the 0.1 - 100 GeV band are not reported in 4FGLR2; we thus compute their fluxes from the 0.1 - 800 GeV energy fluxes and the best-fitting spectral energy distributions (SEDs) provided by ajello2020 .

Due to the extension of NGC 2403 , we calculated the radio flux within the optical isophote of 25 \ mag \ arcsec ^ 2 . We excluded patchy emission clearly disconnected from the main source and not associated with the optical emission of the galaxy, assuming that it does not come from the stellar populations.

Eleven galaxies in our sample ( M31 , NGC 253 , SMC , M33 , LMC , NGC 2146 , NGC 2403 , M82 , NGC 3424 , Arp 299 , Arp 220 ) have fluxes at 150 MHz available in the literature. For NGC 1068 we take the flux at 145 MHz and the spectral index given by Kuehr1981 , and extrapolate the flux to 150 MHz. In all cases, we derive the corresponding luminosities at this frequency ( L_ 150 MHz ) using the K20 distances. We report these values in Table tabledata , together with the respective references.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC2403 consistent with three corotation radii and a repeated resonance-coupling pattern, supported by angular-offset and metallicity-break evidence.

quotes.quote: For NGC2403, NGC3893, and NGC5676 we found data to be consistent with three different CRs, and for three other galaxies we find two. First, the case of special interest is the galaxy NGC2403 and the first resonance coupling in NGC3893. Here, we see the case where OLR_ in =OUHR_ out and OUHR_ in =CR_ out , neither of which has been previously reported as far as we aware. Their synchronous appearance is not accidental and also shows agreement with Sect. sec:crposition , which gives exactly the predicted for NGC2403, where it is repeated twice.

Indeed, we see the expected zero angular offsets in radii close to CRs obtained in NGC628 and for the first CR in NGC2403, where the 2011ApJ...735..101F analysis is not sufficiently extended. For example, we can see that the angular offsets measured by 2008AJ....136.2872T for NGC2403 could easily be fitted by two curves, or that the metallicity values in 2013ApJ...775..128B for the same galaxy show a break at the location of the first CR.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2403 to show a second mechanism for false R23 abundance-gradient bends, tied to changes in the excitation-parameter gradient across the disk.

quotes.quote: A manifistation of this effect will be demonstrated with a galaxy NGC2403. We compiled 47 published measurements of the strong oxygen line intensities in H ii regions of the galaxy NGC2403 (Fierro et al. 1986, Garnett et al. 1997, Garnett et al. 1999, McCall et al. 1985, Smith 1975, van Zee et al. 1998). Fig. figure:izl2403 a shows the radial (O/H) _ P abundance distribution (the filled circles) for H ii regions in NGC2403 derived with Eq.( equation:ohp ).

Inspection of Fig. figure:izl2403 a shows that the variation in the logarithm of (O/H) _ P abundance with radius can be fitted by a straight line. At the same time the variation in the logarithm of (O/H) _ R_ 23 abundance with radius shows evidence of a bend in the slope of the abundance gradient, Fig. figure:izl2403 b. Thus, a false bend in the slope of the (O/H) _ R_ 23 abundance gradient can appear due to a bend in the excitation parameter gradient within the disk of a galaxy.

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 2403 as a distant survey outlier, attributing its small carbon-star sample and unusually high luminosities to incomplete survey coverage.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2403 the small number of carbon stars lies in the fact that the survey has been incomplete. All 4 known C-stars have luminosities that are much higher than the average in galaxies for which we know the LF in more detail. The same is true for NGC 300.

The data show that in well populated LFs, the mean M_ bol is between --4 and --5. It also shows that the mean in NGC 300 and NGC 2403 is much higher. Unless one would invoke a large uncertainty in the distance or a burst of recent star formation, the most natural explanation lies in the incompleteness of the surveys in these distant galaxies.

sources.summary: NGC 2403 is paired with UGC 128 in a hybrid MOND test, showing that MOND cannot fit arbitrary rotation curves unrelated to luminous mass.

quotes.quote: The HSB/LSB pair NGC2403/UGC 128 are two galaxies that occupy identical positions on the Tully Fisher relation, with indistinguishable luminosities and flat rotation amplitudes, but with very different central surface brightnesses. We will now construct a hybrid model galaxy, by taking the rotation curve of NGC 2403, but where we will replace the optical photometry of NGC 2403 by that of UGC 128. This results essentially in a low surface brightness version of NGC 2403. The similarity of the two galaxies maximizes the opportunity for both MOND and dark matter theories to achieve a fit to the hypothetical hybrid.

In the dark matter case one can reach a very tolerable fit, which is comparable in quality with the fit of the real NGC 2403 (see Begeman, Broeils and Sanders 1991, their Fig. 1). The conclusion we can reach from this is that MOND can not fit any arbitrary rotation curve, dispelling the myth which asserts it was designed to do just this.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 2403 as an intermediate-inclination galaxy with anomalous H I, a lagging extraplanar component, inflow, and a long filament.

quotes.quote: For other galaxies seen at intermediate inclinations (NGC 2403 and NGC 4559 ) or more ``face-on'' (M 101 and NGC 6946) the presence of gaseous halos has been inferred from the anomalous velocities. In NGC 2403 and NGC 4559 the signature is the asymmetric velocity shape of the \ line profiles (Fig. \ f_n2403 , bottom left). The deep \ observations of NGC 2403 with the VLA and a careful 3-D modelling have led to conclusions very similar to those obtained for the edge-on NGC 891. Also in NGC 2403 there is a vertically extended (a few kpc) gas component with lagging rotation. It has a total mass of about 3 10^8 ( 10 of the total \ mass) and contains long filamentary structures.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 2403, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 NGC 2403 (Fig. fig:ngc-2403 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 2403 belongs to the M81 group, and is a late-type Sc spiral. Its 3.6 m IRAC image shows multiple spiral arms and a bright central component. However, neither the IRAC image, nor the radio continuum map show a nuclear source. Therefore, we used 3.6 m isophote fits to determine center coordinates and used the resulting center position ( = 4 7, = 13 6) as an initial estimate for an unconstrained \ run.

sources.summary: The paper maps neutral hydrogen around NGC 2403, finding no convincing group clouds and implying any dark halos around the galaxy contain under 1 percent neutral gas.

quotes.quote: If NGC 2403 is surrounded by a population of dark matter halos similar to those proposed for the Milky Way in recent models, our observations imply that their content is less than 1 of their total mass.

The NGC 2403 group contains the bright spiral galaxy NGC 2403, 3 additional large galaxies, and at least 4 dwarf or low surface brightness satellite galaxies. Table 1 lists the properties of the group.

The largest galaxy, NGC 2403, shows a thick, lagging layer and a few filaments with anomalous velocities, which are similar to the M81/M82 clouds in mass frat02 . These features ma be analogues to Milky Way HVCs, and their origins are similarly unclear. These features have been attributed to a combination of outflow from a galactic fountain and accretion from the IGM frat06 ; both of these phenomena may be induced by interactions between group galaxies lar78, san08 .

sources.summary: The paper reports faint diffuse polarized emission in NGC 2403, concentrated mostly in the western half and optical-disk edges, with the weakest emission on the receding major axis.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 There is a faint polarized component which is predominantly diffuse, but is too faint to characterize well with the current observations. Smoothing to a beam size of 45^ 45^ enhances the signal to noise ratio of some of the polarized emission, which is concentrated in the western half of the galaxy, with some localized enhancements at the eastern and western edges of the optical disk. But even after application of smoothing, it is still extremely faint (typical surface brightness 30-50 Jy/beam, reaching as high as 100 Jy/beam). The lowest brightness of polarized emission occurs on the receding major axis (PA = 125 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). Deeper observations would be required to further constrain the magnetic field properties in this target.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2403 as a flocculent, high-surface-brightness M81 group member, finding a 6 kpc bar, anomalous extra-planar H I, and a measured bar pattern speed.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 is a high surface brightness member of the M 81 group with flocculent arms and bright \ regions and a total star formation rate 0.1 \ Kennicutt1989 . In the inner kpc, this galaxy contains about half as much molecular gas as its atomic hydrogen content e.g., ThornleyWilson1995 , suggesting that the formation of molecular hydrogen is constrained by the low surface density of the gas Helferetal2003 . The central gas surface densities are below the threshold star formation density of Kennicutt1989 . Fraternalietal2001 have detected anomalous neutral hydrogen extending beyond the disk plane of the galaxy interpreted as galactic fountains powered by stellar winds from massive stars and supernova explosions, which in turn could be triggered by the bar. Confirmed by a large sample of \ regions, there is a gradient in O/H across NGC 2403 with a slope of 0.1 dex kpc ^ 1 Garnettetal1997 , supporting the study by Beckmanetal1987 who found that non-circular motions are not very efficient in mixing the ISM in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 in dust-heating tests, noting evidence for a 160-250 m transition, SED fits, and flocculent structure that may shape star-formation geometry.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2403, however, the draine07b models predict that dust heated by the diffuse ISRF is seen at > 70 m, whereas the bendo12a analysis found that dust heated locally by star forming regions dominates the SED at < 160 m.

To examine how the dust temperatures and masses change if we divide the dust SED into different components based on the dust heating sources, we performed SED fits to the 160-500 m data for NGC 628, 2403, and 5457, three galaxies where, based on our results, star forming regions were responsible for 50 of the heating for the dust seen at 160 m but the evolved stellar populations were the dominant source of heating for the dust seen at 250 m. Among the galaxies in the sample, the dust temperatures and masses from fits to the measured 160-500 m data for these three galaxies should diverge the most from the quantities that would be found if we separated the SED into separate components based on the results from Section s_decompositionanalysis . ^a All flux density measurements except the 60 m measurements and the 100 m measurement for NGC 2403 include color corrections. ^b See the text in Section s_discussion_sed for how these numbers are set. The global 70-500 m flux densities that we use are given in Table t_sedfit . Because the PACS and SPIRE flux calibration have been recently updated, we needed to re-measure the flux densities; the measurements were made within apertures 1.5 the size of the optical disc given by devaucouleurs91 (although this crosses the edges of the PACS images for NGC 5457). For NGC 2403, which was not observed at 100 m with PACS, we used the 100 m flux density from the Infrared Astronomical Satellite (IRAS) given by rice88 .

Meanwhile, NGC 2403 and 7793 are both flocculent galaxies where, because these two galaxies lack the large shock fronts created by the spiral density waves, star formation is expected to occur in clouds that collapse because of local graviational instabilities.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 2403 in the M81 Group outskirts, notes a variable central X-ray source, and models a low-surface-brightness nuclear cluster after masking nearby dust lanes.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 resides in the outskirts of the M81 Group at a distance of 3.1 Mpc saha2006 . In addition to the NC, NGC 2403 hosts a highly variable central X-ray source, likely to be an X-ray binary system with a stellar mass BH accretor yukita2007 . We fit a flat sky level to the host galaxy and a single \ component to the NC in every band. Dust lanes southwest of the NC were masked out during the fits. With an effective radius of 6.13 pc and an absolute magnitude of 11.62^ +0.11 _ 0.10 in F814W, the cluster has a low surface brightness, leading to relatively large uncertainties in the fit parameters.

sources.summary: The chapter describes NGC 2403 as a star-forming spiral with extraplanar gas whose lagging rotation and inflow are reproduced by fountain-accretion models.

quotes.quote: The second galaxy that was considered in these early works is NGC 2403, a smaller galaxy with relatively high SFR= 1.2 Kennicutt+2003 and large amounts of extraplanar gas Fraternali+2002 . The kinematics of the extraplanar is characterized by a lagging rotation and a global inflow towards the center. Remarkably, the fountain accretion models is able to reproduce this pattern very well \ Fraternali&Binney2008 . The inflow is essentially due to the acquisition of low angular momentum from the ambient gas (Fig. \ fig:orbitsAccretion ). In order to reproduce the observed kinematics of the extraplanar gas, accretion at a rate of about M_ acc 0.8 is required.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 2403 as a low-surface-brightness spiral with vertical velocity gradients and a flat rotation curve fit by the authors model.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 The spiral galaxy NGC 2403 is shown in Fig. fig:2403jpg . This LSB galaxy is also the subject of many publications e.g. Jaretal,LovKie,McG14 . It is discussed by Jaetal11 in the context of ``a global thin-disc model of spiral galaxies'' as a system with vertical gradients of the azimuthal velocity. Based on data from Fraetal02 , they find a near linear decrease at heights above the plane between (0.6 and 3.0) kpc of (- 10 4) ^ 1 ^ 1 . In Fig. fig:2403 the RCs taken from figure 1 of FriMSal display a perfectly flat V_ obs that could best be fitted with 1 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2403 is very well fit by a flat axisymmetric disk model, with a rotation curve matching earlier work but excluding anomalous outer gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 Our flat, axisymmetric disc model fits the data from this galaxy very well, as shown in Figure  fig.N2403 . The rotation curve is in excellent agreement with that from dB08, and also SZS10 , although our uncertainties tend to be smaller than those quoted by the THINGS team. SZS10 were unable to find evidence for a significant non-axisymmetric component in the potential. Our fit excluded gas emission beyond 900 \ because at least some of it caused the rotation curve to drop at those radii. We suspect that this behaviour was due to gas at anomalous velocities that was not eliminated by our mask.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 as a THINGS H I test galaxy, deriving an intensity profile and rotation curve that the neural network matches closely.

quotes.quote: In order to do this we present black two example test galaxies, NGC 2403 and NGC 3198, observed using the V ery L arge A rray (VLA) as part of T he H i N earby G alaxy S urvey (THINGS) walter2008 , and showing a diversity of rotation curve shapes. These galaxies are two of 17 THINGS galaxies used for mixed training and testing using the network and chosen heuristically for the appearance of their well defined rotating H i disks. The names and publications for the galaxies used in this sample are shown in Table table:THINGS_info . black Figure fig:NGC2403 shows the derived intensity profile and rotation curve for NGC 2403. We include the rotation curve modelled using BBarolo 3DBAROLO on the datacube (Di Teodoro Lelli, private communication). In comparison, we see that the neural network's predicted rotation curve matches closely and so we are convinced that the network is able to recover physical information well.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 2403 as a nearby bulgeless low-mass spiral, comparing its growth history with similar discs and finding 66 percent of its stellar mass assembled in the past 8 Gyr.

quotes.quote: NGC 7793, NGC 300, M33 and NGC 2403 are four nearby undisturbed and bulgeless low-mass spiral galaxies with similar morphology and stellar mass. They are ideal laboratories to study disc formation scenarios and stellar mass growth histories.

By comparing the best-fitting model results of the three other galaxies we obtain similar results, 72 (NGC 300), 66 (NGC 2403) and 79 (M33) stellar mass were assembled within the past 8 Gyr (i.e. z = 1 ). These four disc galaxies simultaneously increase their sizes and stellar masses as time goes by and they grow in size at 0.30 times the rate at which they grow in mass. The scale-lengths of these four discs are now 20 - 25 larger than at z = 1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 2403 ionised gas kinematics, finding a slowly rotating halo component, neutral-gas-like halo kinematics, broad outflow line profiles, and fountain-model implications.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the kinematics of the ionised gas in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2403 using deep long-slit spectra obtained with the 4.2-m William Herschel Telescope. The data show the presence of a halo component of ionised gas that is rotating more slowly than the gas in the disk. The kinematics of this ionised halo gas is similar to that of the neutral halo gas. On small scales, broad line profiles (up to 300 \ wide) indicate regions of fast outflows of ionised gas. We discuss these new results in the context of galactic fountain models.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2403 as an SABc galaxy with amorphous spiral features, near-rigid central velocity structure, bright emission regions, uncertain barred status, and weak non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403: This SABc galaxy shows amorphous spiral features. The velocity maps and the PV diagram show an almost rigid structure near the centre of the galaxy. Bright \ regions can be seen in the monochromatic image. It is not clear whether this galaxy in barred or not. According to 1997MNRAS.292..349S ( 1997MNRAS.292..349S ), their Fourier harmonic analysis of the \ velocity field shows that non-circular motions are not important in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 2403 a nearby, well-resolved Sc spiral like M33, notes earlier cluster candidates, and finds 14 central clusters with more expected outside the field.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 NGC 2403 is a nearby spiral, morphologically very similar to M33 apart from the fact that NGC 2403 lacks a distinct nucleus. It is a textbook example of an Sc-type spiral, and it is very well resolved on our NOT images. A photographic survey of star clusters in NGC 2403 was already carried out by Battistini et. al. 1984, who succeeded in finding a few YMC candidates. NGC 2403 spans more than 20 20 arcminutes in the sky, so we have been able to cover only the central parts using the ALFOSC. Within the central 6.5 6.5 (about 6 6 Kpc) we have located 14 clusters altogether, but the real number of clusters in NGC 2403 should be significantly higher, taking into account the large fraction of the galaxy that we haven't covered, and considering the fact that in the other galaxies we have studied, many clusters are located at considerable distances from the centre.

sources.summary: The paper places this galaxy in Group C, where sparse inner rotation-curve data leave the core uncertain and the BDM fit effectively reduces to an NFW-like profile.

quotes.quote: We only present here the conclusions of the last group C, composed of three galaxies (NGC2403, NGC50455 and NGC7331) BDM2011 . These galaxies have fitted values such that r_c/r_s < 10^ 6 for most stellar mass models. The reduced number of data close to galactic center prevents us from finding whether these galaxies actually have or not a core. We conclude in that ISO and Burkert are poorly fitting profiles, meanwhile BDM has a central value for r_c 0 , so BDM reduces to NFW profile and both have equivalent fits.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2403 as a lower-mass NGC 891 analog with a lagging H I halo, shell measurements, and possible past nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: M101 has a similar mass and SFR to NGC 891 5 M_ yr ^ 1 in kuntz10 , whereas M31 has a similar mass but much lower SFR, and NGC 2403 is about ten times less massive but has a relatively high SFR for its mass 0.6 M_ yr ^ 1 in see erratum heald11 and a lagging H 1 halo, suggesting it is a lower mass analog to NGC 891.
In NGC 2403, thilker98 identify both kinematically young (expanding) shells and older stalled shells with a missing mass range of 10^6-10^7 M_ per hole and ages between 1--200 Myr.

The case of NGC 2403 is especially interesting in this vein because a Chandra observation shows X-ray emission that is more centrally concentrated than the star-forming regions yukita10 . Since NGC 2403 has a relatively high SFR ( 1 M_ yr ^ 1 ) for its mass ( M_T 10^ 10 M_ ) and the X-ray emission (if produced by star formation) represents a significant amount of mass, NGC 2403 may have had a nuclear starburst that has propagated outwards or shut off while the disk star formation continued yukita10 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a low-column-density H I cloud northwest of NGC 2403, associated with an anomalous-velocity filament and possibly caused by IGM accretion or a minor dwarf-galaxy interaction.

quotes.quote: These observations, with a 5 detection limit of 2.4 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 over a 20 km s ^ 1 linewidth, reveal the presence of a low-column density, extended cloud outside the main \ disk, about 17' ( 16 kpc or 2 R_ 25 ) to the NW of the center of the galaxy. The total \ mass of the cloud is 6.3 10^6 \ , or 0.15 percent of the total \ mass of NGC 2403. The cloud is associated with an 8-kpc anomalous-velocity \ filament in the inner disk, previously observed in deep VLA observations by Fraternali et al. \ (2001, 2002). We discuss several scenarios for the origin of the cloud, and conclude that it is either accreting from the intergalactic medium, or is the result of a minor interaction with a neigbouring dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The model gives NGC 2403 a high inner gas Toomre parameter, long viscous times beyond two disk scale lengths, and an anomalous thick H i layer with slow infall.

quotes.quote: A high Toomre parameter Q_ gas 8 is found in the inner disks of NGC 2403, NGC 2967, NGC 4214, and NGC 7793 decreasing to Q_ gas 1.5 2 in the outer disks.
The viscous timescales exceed the gas depletion time in NGC 2403, NGC 2967, NGC 4214, and NGC 7793 beyond R 2 R _ d , the radius within which half of the galaxy's luminosity is contained.

In NGC 2403, 2002AJ....123.3124F found a faint extended and kinematically anomalous gas component. The cold H i disk is surrounded by a thick and clumpy H i layer characterised by slow rotation and infall motion (10-20 km s ^ 1 ) towards the center.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 2403 as a late-type Sc spiral with nearly constant orientation angles, low radial velocities, and a quiet rotation-dominated velocity field.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 NGC 2403 is a late-type Sc spiral galaxy whose rotation curve has been determined by many groups (see and references therein). The TRM finds that both the P.A. and the inclination angle have small variations with radius, i.e. < 10^ both in the inner and outer disk as R_ 25 500'' (see Fig. NGC2403 -- note that in the bottom part of panel (a) it is reported the behavior of v_r(R) as measured by : this same profile will be reported below for all galaxies it is available.). Because both angles are nearly constant, we find v_t(R) v_c(R) .

sources.summary: The Euclid paper describes NGC 2403 as a late-type spiral with a very extended disk, outer stellar component, possible stream/satellite interaction, extraplanar gas, and many extragalactic globular clusters.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 is a late-type spiral, without a measurable bulge, morphologically very similar to M33 and NGC 300 williams13 . Like Holmberg II, it lies on the outskirts of the M81 group of galaxies karach02 . The exponential disk of NGC 2403 is extremely extended, out to 18 kpc, with an additional stellar structural component reaching even larger distances 40 kpc, barker12 . Recent deep Hyper Suprime-Cam imaging with Subaru reveals stellar streams in the direction of NGC 2403 emanating from a candidate dwarf satellite DDO 40 carlin19 .

sources.summary: MOND rotation-curve fits for NGC 2403 agree with observations at the Cepheid distance, with only modest inner-region excess and reasonable stellar mass-to-light ratios.

quotes.quote: Here we see that for two of the galaxies, NGC 2403 and NGC 7331, the MOND rotation curves agree well with the observed curve. In both of these cases, the predicted rotation curve lies somewhat above the observed curve in the inner regions but this could be due to beam-smearing. The implied mass-to-light ratios generally fall within the range that would be considered reasonable for stellar populations (Table 1).
For the galaxies NGC 2403 and NGC 7331, MOND rotation curves agree acceptably with the observed curves when these galaxies are taken at the Cepheid-based distance.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2403 with UGC 128, noting similar size, rotation-curve amplitude, two-armed spiral structure, and maximum-disk mass but different mass-to-light ratios.

quotes.quote: De Blok McGaugh (1996) have pointed out that the bright galaxy NGC 2403 is very similar to the LSBG UGC 128. Both have about the same physical size, their rotation curves have the same amplitudes, and both show well developed two--armed spiral structure. Thus the maximum disk of NGC 2403 has the same mass as the maximum disk of UGC 128. Only their mass--to--light ratios are different.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2403 as the closest SONG analogue to M33, with similar optical appearance, CO emission consistent with individual GMCs, and comparable molecular mass.

quotes.quote: The survey contains a close analogue to M33 both in optical appearance and CO distribution: NGC 2403. This galaxy is an Sc galaxy at a distance of 4.2 Mpc, and is shown in the Hubble Atlas Sandage61 on adjoining panels to emphasize their similarity. The observed CO distribution of NGC 2403 is in fact consistent with originating in individual GMCs like that in M33. The total mass of M33, or 3 10 ^7 M _ Engargiola02 , is close to that of NGC 2403, which we infer to be 7 10 ^7 M _ from Young95 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 as the nearby Scd host of a transient X-ray pulsar and summarizes prior X-ray detections and Chandra source-population work in its disk.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery and analysis of the transient X-ray pulsar CXOU J073709.1+653544 detected in the 2004 August--October Chandra and XMM-Newton observations of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2403.

The spiral Scd galaxy NGC 2403 at 3.2 Mpc (Freedman Madore 1988), provides a good opportunity to study X-ray source populations in a normal galaxy. NGC 2403 was a target of previous X-ray observations with the Einstein (Fabbiano Trinchieri 1987; Fabbiano et al. 1992), ROSAT (Roberts Warwick 2000; Liu Bregman 2005) and ASCA observatories (Kotoku et al. 2000), which detected five discrete sources with one bright source classified as an ultraluminous X-ray source (ULX) with luminosity above 10^ 39 ergs s ^ 1 . Recent Chandra observations covering the inner 25 of the D_ 25 area of NGC 2403 have revealed 41 discrete sources with apparent luminosities down to 10^ 36 ergs s ^ 1 and allowed to study unresolved soft X-ray emission from the galactic disk .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2403 as a high-surface-brightness comparison to UGC 128, emphasizing their similar Tully-Fisher position but much different density and mass-to-light properties.

quotes.quote: A comparison of a HSB and a LSB galaxy at identical positions on the Tully-Fisher relation (i.e. at fixed V_ max ) (de Blok McGaugh 1996) showed that LSB galaxy UGC 128 is almost a factor 10 less dense than the HSB galaxy NGC 2403 (analogous with their low stellar and surface densities). UGC 128 also has a factor of 7 higher total M/L (measured within a fixed number of scale lengths) and must therefore be more massive than HSB galaxy NGC 2403 (which has the same luminosity).

This question is extensively discussed in de Blok McGaugh (1996) where the LSB galaxy UGC 128 and HSB galaxy NGC 2403 (which lie at identical positions on the TF relation) are compared. The conclusion reached there is that the total mass volume density enclosed within a fixed number of scale lengths is a factor 10 lower in UGC 128 than in NGC 2403.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2403 as a pseudobulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 .SXS6../Sc(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge is comprised of several clumps of stars, reminiscent of a very late-type disk galaxy. The decomposition yields n_b=0.7 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =1.2 mag.

sources.summary: For NGC 2403, the paper adopts a Cepheid distance, K87 photometry, and Begeman rotation curve, noting that repeated HI re-observations have not changed the rotation curve used.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 This galaxy has a distance based on Cepheid variables (Freedman Madore 1988; Freedman et al. 2001). Photometry by K87 and rotation curve by Begeman (1987).
