Like for NGC 3198 this galaxy has also been re-observed in the on numerous occasions, which has never led to any alteration of the rotation curve used here.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 to motivate self-absorption corrections, noting that an edge-on view would hide substantial H I mass and bias edge-on galaxy modeling.

quotes.quote: In Paper II, we demonstrated that the face-on galaxy NGC 2403 has at least 10 more mass than an optically thin model would have measured. Rotating NGC 2403 to an edge-on geometry and assuming a spin temperature of 100 K, we found that 22 of the total mass would have been hidden by self-absorption. A lower median spin temperature of 80 K increases this fraction to 30 (see Section 7 of Paper II).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2403 as an isolated comparison disk, noting its undisturbed H I field, unbroken young stellar component, and little temporal evolution in scale length.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 is the most isolated with no large nearby companion and an undisturbed HI field 2013ApJ...765..120W ; and references therein .
In plain contrast, the HI disk of NGC 2403 is pristine and unbroken out to at least 15 kpc 2002AJ....123.3124F, 2008AJ....136.2648D .
Characteristically, NGC 2403 and NGC 300 have seen little temporal evolution in the stellar disk scale length 2013ApJ...765..120W ; 2015arXiv151004768K , suggesting a quiet past in SF.

sources.summary: The paper assigns NGC 2403 a CO conversion assumption, shows HI scale heights under different gravitational potentials, and compares its H2 radial profile with literature results.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2403, 2016Frank used the MW _ CO as this galaxy was not included in study.

In Fig. fig:h_sepcomp , we show three different HI scale heights out to R=20 kpc for NGC 2403: each of these scale heights is obtained with a different gravitational potential but the same velocity dispersion radial profile _ HI (R) .
For completeness, we also compared our _ H _2 radial profile for NGC 2403, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055 with those reported by .

sources.summary: The paper uses HALOGAS data for NGC 2403, notes its star formation is less radially extended than H I, and fits its energy profile with a two-phase model.

quotes.quote: For NGC 0925, NGC 2403, NGC 3198, and NGC 5055, we used the publicly available data cubes from the Hydrogen Accretion in LOcal GAlaxieS (HALOGAS) Survey 2011Heald , which have a better signal-to-noise ratio with respect to the robust-weighted THINGS data cubes adopted in 2019Bacchini .
For DDO 154, NGC 2403, NGC 3198, and NGC 6946, _ SFR (R) is less radially extended than _ HI (R) , as the FUV emission from the outermost radii goes below the sensitivity limit of the observations.
fig:2phase_panels show the result of this analysis for NGC 2403 and NGC 4736: the profiles of the observed energy are remarkably well reproduced by the two-phase best model, for both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2403 as a real low-redshift spiral validation case, modeling its kinematics from low-resolution H I observations to compute PV parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC2403 is a nearby spiral galaxy that is part of the M81 group. The computation of the PV parameters described in Sect. sec:new_par is obtained after modeling its kinematics with . We used single dish HI observations from the Green Bank Telescope DeBlok_2014, DiTeodoro_2015 with a synthesized beam size of 29.7" 29.3", allowing a sampling of the semi-major axis with 3 independent resolution elements.

sources.summary: The atlas calls NGC 2403 a late-type spiral like M33, with faint disk radio continuum, bright H II-region coincidences, and anomalous-velocity halo gas possibly tracing accretion.

quotes.quote: This late-type spiral is a twin of M 33. The faint radio continuum from the disk has a number of brighter spots superimposed, which are coincident with prominent H II regions (Drissen et al. ). The galaxy has become known for the well-documented ``anomalous'' velocity gas component associated with the halo (Faternali et al. , ) which may be the signature of accreting gas.

gmw_00000815.name: UGC 128
gmw_00000815.overview: UGC 128 is a low-surface-brightness disk galaxy [arXiv:1802.09967, arXiv:astro-ph/9903164] that features a well-developed, two-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0209157]. Its rotation curve rises slowly [arXiv:1611.02716, arXiv:astro-ph/9903164], reflecting a stellar disk with a low surface density [arXiv:1802.09967] and a negligible effect on its dark matter halo [arXiv:1611.02716].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 128 as a low-surface-brightness counterpart to NGC 2403, with much lower disc surface density, slower-rising rotation, and a high maximum-disc mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: UGC 128 fig:LSBHSBsidebyside (right) shows UGC 128, which is a LSB counterpart of NGC 2403 Verheijen:1999fw . The two galaxies have similar V_ flat and total baryonic mass, but the effective radius of UGC 128 is 4.5 times larger than that of NGC 2403. Hence, the stellar and gas discs of UGC 128 have significantly lower surface densities. This difference in surface densities is known to correspond to a difference in rotation curve shapes 1997MNRAS.290..533D, 2013MNRAS.433L..30L .

The rotation curve of NGC 2403 reaches 130 km s ^ 1 within the first 5 kpc, while the one of UGC 128 has barely reached the same value of within 40 kpc. When is maximized, can reproduce the rotation curve until 16 kpc, which is comparable NGC 2403 when scaled by the disc scale length ( 16/4.5 3.5 kpc). There is, however, a major difference. UGC 128 has =4.07 0.17 , significantly larger than NGC 2403.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts UGC 128 with NGC 6503, describing its slowly rising rotation curve and negligible stellar-disk effect while still obtaining a strong SIDM halo fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 6503  and UGC 128  clearly illustrate the diverse features in the rotation curves caused by the baryon distribution in Fig.  fig:examplesv130 . Both galaxies have V_f 130  km/s , but their inner rotation curves are very different. For NGC 6503, the circular velocity increases sharply in the inner regions and reaches its asymptotic value around 5  kpc ; in UGC 128, it increases very mildly and reaches V_f at 20  kpc . Despite the dramatic differences, the SIDM halo with median concentration provides a remarkable fit to both galaxies.
In contrast, the stellar disk has a negligible effect on the SIDM halo of UGC 128.

sources.summary: UGC 128 is paired with NGC2403 to test MOND using matched Tully-Fisher properties but different surface brightness, producing a failed hybrid fit.

quotes.quote: The HSB/LSB pair NGC2403/UGC 128 are two galaxies that occupy identical positions on the Tully Fisher relation, with indistinguishable luminosities and flat rotation amplitudes, but with very different central surface brightnesses. This is extensively described in de Blok McGaugh (1996). We will now construct a hybrid model galaxy, by taking the rotation curve of NGC 2403, but where we will replace the optical photometry of NGC 2403 by that of UGC 128. This results essentially in a low surface brightness version of NGC 2403. The similarity of the two galaxies maximizes the opportunity for both MOND and dark matter theories to achieve a fit to the hypothetical hybrid.

sources.summary: UGC 128 is used as a typical LSB galaxy whose baryon-poor, slowly rising rotation curve probes a large halo fraction and favors a constant-density core.

quotes.quote: The total rotational velocity and the baryonic contribution from the stars and gas from a ``typical'' LSB galaxy (UGC 128) are shown by the open squares. The mass to baryon ratio for this galaxy is nearly 20:1 and the rotation curve data probes a remarkable 25 of the expected virialised halo.

Galaxies, such as UGC 128, sample the halo mass distribution to a distance 25 of the virial radius. Their rotation curves rise slowly within the inner 10 -20 kpc, and their rotation curves are best fit with a large inner region that has close to a constant density. These galaxies are not rare and they have properties that connect smoothly into samples of higher surface brightness galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper compares UGC 128 with NGC 2403, noting similar size, rotation-curve amplitude, two-armed spiral structure, and maximum-disk mass but different mass-to-light ratios.

quotes.quote: De Blok McGaugh (1996) have pointed out that the bright galaxy NGC 2403 is very similar to the LSBG UGC 128. Both have about the same physical size, their rotation curves have the same amplitudes, and both show well developed two--armed spiral structure. Thus the maximum disk of NGC 2403 has the same mass as the maximum disk of UGC 128. Only their mass--to--light ratios are different.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 128 as an LSB comparison case, stating that it is much less dense and has much higher total mass-to-light ratio than NGC 2403.

quotes.quote: A comparison of a HSB and a LSB galaxy at identical positions on the Tully-Fisher relation (i.e. at fixed V_ max ) (de Blok McGaugh 1996) showed that LSB galaxy UGC 128 is almost a factor 10 less dense than the HSB galaxy NGC 2403 (analogous with their low stellar and surface densities). UGC 128 also has a factor of 7 higher total M/L (measured within a fixed number of scale lengths) and must therefore be more massive than HSB galaxy NGC 2403 (which has the same luminosity).

This question is extensively discussed in de Blok McGaugh (1996) where the LSB galaxy UGC 128 and HSB galaxy NGC 2403 (which lie at identical positions on the TF relation) are compared. The conclusion reached there is that the total mass volume density enclosed within a fixed number of scale lengths is a factor 10 lower in UGC 128 than in NGC 2403.

gmw_00000816.name: SMMJ04135+1027
gmw_00000816.overview: SMMJ04135+1027 is a bright submillimeter-selected quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.83 [arXiv:astro-ph/0009024, arXiv:astro-ph/0309184]. The galaxy has a high infrared luminosity of 3e13 solar luminosities [arXiv:astro-ph/0009024, arXiv:astro-ph/0309184] and exhibits carbon monoxide gas emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0309184].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMMJ04135+1027 as a bright submillimeter-selected z=2.83 quasar, describes its optical spectrum, and compares its far-infrared SED and luminosity to starburst/QSO templates.

quotes.quote: Our optical identification of the bright submm source, which we henceforth refer to as SMMJ04135+1027, is an I = 20.5 0.1 \ mag point source.

To determine the redshift and the nature of SMMJ04135+1027 we have obtained an optical spectrum also with FORS1. The spectrum was observed in MOS mode with resolution R=150 . The spectrum exhibits the characteristica of high redshift quasars, such as broad emission lines (e.g. \ Ly , C IV , C III and Si IV ), Lyman Forest absorption bluewards of the Ly emission line, and a power law continuum. The redshift has been measured using the C III line to be 2.83 0.01 , which is consistent with the other emission lines.

Shorter wavelength data would be needed to see if the hot dust component that is found in low--z QSOs such as PG0050+124 is also present in SMMJ04135+1027. For an Arp220 SED, the total luminosity of SMMJ04135+1027 is 3 10^ 13 L _ .

sources.summary: The paper reports SMM J04135+10277 as a bright submillimetre quasar in A478, with high 850 and 450 micron fluxes, redshift 2.837, CO detection, and large infrared luminosity.

quotes.quote: The submillimetre source, SMM J04135+10277, has f_ 850 = 25 m and f_ 450 = 55 m - these fluxes should be corrected with a magnification factor of 1.3. This is the brightest source in the entire survey and also bright for other similar surveys. The quasar has a redshift of 2.837.

CO has been detected at z=2.84 (Hainline et al, in prep). An analysis of the SED suggests that the quasar has a larger submm/far-IR emission than other quasars. The IR luminosity is L_ IR = 3 10^ 13 L _ .

gmw_00000817.name: PMN J2134--0419
gmw_00000817.overview: PMN J2134--0419 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=4.33 [arXiv:1803.10452]. Very long baseline interferometry reveals a central radio jet exhibiting apparent superluminal motion [arXiv:1803.10452], while larger-scale observations indicate a bent jet structure extending to 30 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1803.10452].

sources.summary: The paper measures radio-jet proper motion in the high-redshift quasar PMN J2134--0419, finding apparent superluminal motion, relativistic beaming, and evidence for a bent large-scale jet.

quotes.quote: To date, PMN J2134--0419 (at a redshift z=4.33 ) is the second most distant quasar known with a milliarcsecond-scale morphology permitting direct estimates of the jet proper motion. Based on two-epoch observations, we constrained its radio jet proper motion using the very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) technique. The observations were conducted with the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 5 GHz on 1999 November 26 and 2015 October 6. We imaged the central 10-pc scale radio jet emission and modeled its brightness distribution.

By identifying a jet component at both epochs separated by 15.86 yr, a proper motion of =0.035 0.023 mas yr ^ 1 is found. It corresponds to an apparent superluminal speed of _ a =4.1 2.7 . Relativistic beaming at both epochs suggests that the jet viewing angle with respect to the line of sight is smaller than 20 , with a minimum bulk Lorentz factor =4.3 . The small value of the proper motion is in good agreement with the expectations from the cosmological interpretation of the redshift and the current cosmological model. Additionally we analyzed archival Very Large Array observations of J2143 0419 and found indication of a bent jet extending to 30 kpc.

gmw_00000818.name: NGC 1291
gmw_00000818.overview: NGC 1291 is a nearly face-on, double-barred early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1202.2358, arXiv:1901.06394] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1202.2358]. Its structure features an outer ring rich in neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1810.02075, arXiv:2304.00515] and a long-lived inner bar assembled over 6.5 billion years ago [arXiv:1901.06394, arXiv:1909.01704].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1291 as a face-on ring galaxy, cataloging its bulge and ring X-ray sources, finding many LMXB candidates, and identifying its nuclear source as a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: Box 2208, GR-710 03, Heraklion, Crete, Greece We present \ studies of the X-ray binary (XRB) populations in the bulge and ring regions of the ring galaxy NGC 1291. We detect 169 X-ray point sources in the galaxy, 75 in the bulge and 71 in the ring, utilizing the four available \ observations totaling an effective exposure of 179 ks. The X-ray colors suggest that a significant fraction of the bulge ( 75 ) and ring ( 65 ) sources are likely low-mass X-ray binaries (LMXBs). The spectra of the nuclear source indicate that it is a low-luminosity AGN with moderate obscuration; spectral variability is observed between individual observations.

NGC 1291 is a remarkable ring galaxy, first noted by Perrine1922 . It was classified as (R)SB(s)0/a (RC3; deVaucouleurs1991 ), and is virtually face-on Mebold1979 . The galaxy includes a fainter outer ring and a prominent bulge, which was considered as a prototypical pseudobulge e.g., Kormendy2004 . At a distance of 8.9 Mpc, NGC 1291 has M_ B,0 = 20.6 deVaucouleurs1975 .

sources.summary: The paper reports the first detected box/peanut structure in an inner bar, using MUSE kinematics to show NGC 1291 has double h_4 minima tied to its inner bar.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of the first Box-Peanut (B/P) structure in an inner bar detected in the face-on galaxy NGC 1291. We use the integral field data obtained from the MUSE spectrograph within the TIMER project. The B/P structure is detected as bi-symmetric minima of the h_4 moment of the line-of-sight velocity distribution along the major axis of the inner bar, as expected from numerical simulations. Our observations demonstrate that inner bars can follow a similar evolutionary path as outer bars, undergoing buckling instabilities.

The rightmost panels of Fig. fig:kin show the h_4 spatial distribution and radial profiles of NGC 1291. Clear, symmetric, double minima in the h_4 are seen along the major axis of the inner bar. These minima are neither observed along the galaxy nor the main bar major axis indicating that they are related to the presence of the inner bar. The double minima occur at a projected distance of 4 , which corresponds to 26 of the inner bar effective radius.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1291 as an unambiguous nearby double-barred galaxy with a directly visible inner bar, an inner disc, hollows, and an inner bar assembled over 6.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: NGC 1291 and NGC 5850 are undoubtedly double-barred hosts. NGC 1291 is a closeby (distance of 8.6 Mpc) and almost face-on ( i 11 ^ ) galaxy, whose inner bar is perfectly observed in direct images without the need for any further analysis (e.g. ellipse fitting or unsharp masking). NGC 5850 (distance 23.1 Mpc; i 39 ^ ) belongs to the sample of double-barred galaxies analysed by deLorenzoCaceresetal2008, deLorenzoCaceresetal2013 , who confirmed the presence of the inner bar not only photometrically but also spectroscopically through the kinematic diagnostics of the hollows (see Sect. sec:kin ). Butaetal2015 classified these two galaxias as (R)SAB(l,bl,nb)0 ^ + and (R ' )SB(,bl,nr,nb) a b, respectively. NGC 1291 and NGC 5850 are used throughout this paper as benchmarks to study the properties of double-barred galaxies with the superb spatial resolution provided by MUSE.

Our analysis confirms the presence of hollows appearing in the stellar velocity dispersion distribution at the ends of the inner bars. Both galaxies host inner discs matching in size with the inner bars, suggestive of a dynamical formation for the inner bars from small-scale discs. The analysis of the star formation histories for the structural components shaping the galaxies provides constraints on the epoch of dynamical assembly of the inner bars, which took place > 6.5 Gyr ago for NGC 1291 and > 4.5 Gyr ago for NGC 5850.

sources.summary: The paper catalogs old globular clusters in NGC 1291, finding bimodal colors, different spatial distributions, and a high specific frequency for its cluster system.

quotes.quote: We present a new catalog of 81 ancient globular clusters (GCs) in the early-type spiral (SB0/a) galaxy NGC 1291. Candidates have been selected from B , V , and I band images taken with the Hubble Space Telescope, which also reveal 17 younger ( few 100 Myr) clusters. The luminosity function shows a peaked shape similar to that found for GC systems in other spiral and elliptical galaxies. The ancient clusters have a bimodal color distribution, with approximately 65 (35 ) of the population having blue (red) colors. The red, presumably metal-rich GCs are more centrally concentrated, as expected for a bulge population; while the blue, presumably metal-poor GCs, are more broadly distributed, consistent with expectations of a halo population.

The specific frequency of GCs in NGC 1291 is higher than found previously in most spiral galaxies. However, if we consider just the blue subpopulation, we find S_ N, blue =0.50 0.06 , quite similar to that found for other spirals. This result supports the hypothesis of a universal population of halo GCs in spirals.

sources.summary: The paper uses KMTNet imaging of NGC 1291 to measure deep surface-brightness profiles, characterize its Type I disk and outer ring, and find no significant halo excess.

quotes.quote: We present our observing strategy and optimal data reduction process to recover the faint extended features in the imaging data of NGC 1291 taken with KMTNet.

As a pilot study, we chose NGC 1291 which has a largest angular size among our sample, and also has a negligible contamination of Galactic cirrus. Therefore, this target is appropriate to test whether we can reach the precision required to study faint outskirts of nearby galaxies with KMTNet. Some basic properties of NGC 1291 are listed in Table 1.

NGC 1291 is known as a barred galaxy with an outer ring with a radius of 9 kpc. The outer ring is more prominent in both UV and FIR images than in optical images, suggesting that a significant number of young stars and amount dust reside in the ring 2012ApJ...756...75H .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1291 as a double-barred galaxy with the first detected box/peanut structure in a face-on inner bar, implying a long-lived inner bar assembled over 6.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: We present a thorough study of the central structures of the double-barred galaxy NGC 1291. We used a two-dimensional multi-component photometric decomposition performed on the 3.6μm images from S ^4 G, combined with both stellar kinematics and stellar population analysis carried out using integral field data from the MUSE TIMER project. We report on the discovery of the first Box-Peanut (B/P) structure in an inner bar detected in the face-on galaxy NGC 1291. The B/P structure is detected as bi-symmetric minima of the h_4 moment of the line-of-sight velocity distribution along the major axis of the inner bar, as expected from numerical simulations. Our observations demonstrate that inner bars (similarly as outer bars) can suffer buckling instabilities, thus suggesting they can survive a long time after bar formation.

The analysis of the star formation history for the structural components shaping the central regions of NGC 1291 also constrains the epoch of dynamical assembly of the inner bar, which took place > 6.5 Gyr ago for NGC 1291. Our results imply that the inner bar of NGC 1291 is a long-lived structure.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1291 as a milder LMXB outlier: its X-ray growth curve deviates from near-infrared light, but the luminosity cutoff is only a two-sigma effect.

quotes.quote: Individual galaxies The L_X growth curves of two galaxies, NGC1553 and NGC1291, show significant deviations from the distribution of the NIR light (Fig. fig:gcurves_lx ). The luminosity distributions for these two galaxies also exhibit largest deviations from the average XLF, although consistent with the latter within statistical uncertainties, according to the Kolmogorov-Smirnov test (Table tab:ks_test ). These two galaxies might indeed present an example of the population age dependent variations in the shape of the luminosity distribution. In both cases the deviations from the average LMXB XLF occur at the high luminosity end as might be expected for such variations.
NGC1291 The deviations of the L_X growth curve from the NIR profile are significantly less pronounced and are within about 2 .
For a Poisson distribution with expectation value of 2.7, the probability to draw zero is 6.7 , i.e. the case of NGC1291 presents 2 deviation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1291 as a face-on Sa galaxy with misaligned primary and secondary bars, substantial H I gas, little central-bulge star formation, and BLAST-detected core and ring structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1291 NGC 1291, presented in Fig. fig:ngc1291 , is the brightest Sa galaxy in the Shapley-Ames Catalogue bregman1995 . NGC 1291 has an orientation that is almost `face on' and has a primary bar and secondary bar that have a 30 \ misalignment with each other perez2006 . The total H 1 mass is 2.4 10^9 M _ , and there is little evidence for star formation in the central bulge hogg2001 . BLAST made four observations of a 0.4 \ area centered on NGC 1291, with a total integration time of 92 minutes. Although diffuse, BLAST detects both the central core of the galaxy, as well as the ring-like spiral structure 4 \ from the core.

sources.summary: The ELVES paper compares its NGC 1291 satellite search with byun2020, recovering most reported candidates, rejecting two likely backgrounds, and adding six candidates with mixed distance outcomes.

quotes.quote: NGC 1291 byun2020 : We recover all the satellite candidates reported by byun2020 that fall within 300 kpc projected of NGC 1291 except for three: Dw11, Dw15, and Dw14. The first falls just outside the footprints of the DECaLS tracts used in our search. The latter two had morphologies strongly indicating they are background. One is a distant edge-on disk galaxy with a clear bulge, and the other has a bar and spiral arms yet is quite small ( r_e<5 ). We note that our DECaLS/DES search recovers 6 candidates not included by byun2020 , including two we have rejected as background contaminants, one we have confirmed via SBF, and three that remain without distance confirmation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1291 as a face-on barred system with an inner disc, faint outer stellar disc, and HI distributed as an outer ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 1291: This galaxy is observed to posses an `ansae' type diffuse bar, an inner disc and a faint outer stellar disc. The 1 distribution is observed be distributed in the form an outer ring that is coincident with the outer stellar ring. This galaxy is nearly face-on which is also evident from the 1 velocity map. See the centre left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic2 for the optical and 1 maps.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC1291 as underpredicted by 4-7 orders of magnitude, describes it as an early-type spiral, and finds the adjusted model within about one order.

quotes.quote: Sources that were under-predicted 4-7 orders of magnitude are: NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC1553, NGC4261, NGC4438, NGC4594, NGC4697 and IC4296.

NGC1291 is an early type spiral, NGC1316 is disturbed early type elliptical, NGC 1553 has a spiral feature passing threw a center, NGC 4261 core is surrounded by a disk of gas and dust, NGC4438 has an outflow bubbles which also may indicate angular momentum to dominate the accretion flow, NGC4594 is a spiral galaxy, the last two sourced NGC 4697 and IC4296 are elliptical radio galaxies.

Other sources (NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC4438, NGC4472, M87, NGC4594, NGC4697, IC1459, IC4296) differ from their observational counterparts of about 1 order of magnitude, lots of them even less than order of magnitude (see Table 1.)

gmw_00000819.name: PKS J0352-0711
gmw_00000819.overview: PKS J0352-0711, also known as 3C 94, is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.966 [arXiv:2003.08692, arXiv:2308.06080]. Observations reveal two distinct outflows within the quasar that feature high-ionization phases and super-solar metallicity [arXiv:2003.08692].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HST/COS spectra of PKS J0352-0711, deriving two quasar outflows with high-ionization phases, distances, kinetic luminosities, and limited AGN feedback impact.

quotes.quote: We analyze absorption troughs from two outflows within PKS J0352-0711 (S1 at 1950 km s ^ 1 and S2 at 3150 km s ^ 1 ) from spectra taken with Hubble Space Telescope /Cosmic Origin Spectrograph, which cover the diagnostic-rich 585-900 \ rest frame wavelength range. In S2, for the first time we clearly detect absorption troughs from Ca iv , Ca v , Ca v* , Ca vii* , and Ca viii* . The column density measurement of Ca v suggests S2 has a super-solar metallicity. Both outflows require at least two ionization phases where the column density of the very high-ionization phase is roughly 15 times larger than the corresponding high-ionization phase.

Through Monte Carlo measurements of the O v* absorption troughs, we determine the electron number density of S2 (fully corroborated by independent measurements from the Ca vii* and Ca viii* troughs), yielding a distance of 9 pc and a kinetic luminosity of 2 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . S1 is located farther away at 500 pc from the central source with a kinetic luminosity of 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .
PKS J0352-0711 ( z = 0.9662, J2000: R.A. = 03:52:30.55, decl. = 07:11:02.3) was observed by HST /COS in August of 2017 (PID 14777).

sources.summary: 3C 94 is described as one of the most redshifted sample quasars; telluric and edge effects raise line-profile uncertainties, while its narrow component appears slightly blueshifted.

quotes.quote: In the two most redshifted quasars in the sample at z > 0.9 (3C 94 and PKS 0420), the reddest part of the spectrum has a slightly lower S/N due to the correction of the telluric band that extends between 9250 and 9700 in the observed wavelength. This results in a kind of flat-topped and even multipeaked profile in (see Fig. fig:f3 ), and slightly larger uncertainties in the determination of the higher fractional intensity centroids of , and to less extent in .

In 3C 94 and PKS 2344+09 the NC appears slightly blueshifted. Two factors may be contributing to the shift: the first one and more probable is the possible presence of an unresolved SBC included in the NC of , in correspondence with the observed SBC of (see below), which would also explain the relatively high value obtained for the FWHM 1600 for 3C 94. A second factor to take into account in the case of 3C 94, as we mentioned previously, is that both the \ profile and the \ blue blend are affected by a telluric band extending between 9250 \ and 9700 \ in observed wavelengths (that correspond to 4700 4900 \ in the rest-frame), whose correction can also introduce greater uncertainty in the determination of the position of the peak and the centroids at the highest fractional intensities.

gmw_00000820.name: CSL-1
gmw_00000820.overview: Initially identified as a potential cosmic-string gravitational lens candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0501590, arXiv:astro-ph/0511085], CSL-1 is actually a pair of interacting giant elliptical galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0601494, arXiv:astro-ph/0611744]. Located at a redshift of 0.46 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501590, arXiv:astro-ph/0601494], the two components are separated by approximately 1.9 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0601494, arXiv:astro-ph/0603838] and may be in the early stages of merging [arXiv:astro-ph/0603838].

sources.summary: The paper treats CSL-1 as a two-object lens candidate with identical spectra and redshifts, testing and rejecting a tidally disrupted dark-matter halo as the likely lensing explanation.

quotes.quote: The lens candidate CSL-1 has been interpreted as evidence for a cosmic string. Here we test the hypothesis that the lensing comes from a tidally disrupted dark matter halo. We calculate the mass-density relationship that one would expect from structure formation theory and come to the conclusion that in order to explain the lensing using dark matter, the halo would have to have a mass greater than the Milky Way. There is apparently no such object seen in the data.

The Capodimonte-Sternberg Lens candidate 1 (CSL-1) consists of two objects with identical spectra and redshifts ( z=0.46 ) separated by an angular distance of about 1.8''= 8.6 10^ 6 radians . The spectra are consistent with giant elliptical galaxies, and if they were to lie at precisely the same redshift, then their maximum separation would be around 11 kpc (assuming _m =0.3, _ =0.7, h=0.65 for the cosmological parameters). It is possible that these two images are two separate galaxies located along the same line of sight with similar observed redshifts. However, the similarity between their spectra has lead Sazhin et al to suggest that the two objects may both be images of the same object viewed through a gravitational lens .

sources.summary: The review describes CSL-1 as an extended lensing candidate at redshift 0.46 whose identical spectra and morphology were argued to favor cosmic-string lensing despite tension with CMB bounds.

quotes.quote: However a new candidate event named CSL-1 (Capodimonte-Sternberg Lens candidate no.1) was reported a couple of years ago by a Russian-Italian collaboration . The most intriguing property of CSL-1 (Fig. 5) is that the object is clearly extended and the isophotes of the two sources show no distortion at all. Also both images have a redshift of 0.46 and the two spectra are identical at a 99.96 confidence level. Extensive modeling of the photometric properties of CSL-1 showed that no traditional lensing by compact lens can satisfactorily fit the observed morphology.

The most likely explanation of the observed properties of CSL-1 is that it is the result of lensing by an interposed cosmic string. Such a string should have G 4 10^ 7 to produce the observed image separation ( 2'' ) at the observed redshift z=0.46 0.008 . This range is marginally in conflict with the statistical CMB bounds coming from the lack of linear discontinuities in the WMAP maps (see eq ( smb )) and with the corresponding bound coming from the stochastic gravitational wave background of eq ( gwb ).

sources.summary: The paper concludes that CSL-1 is not a cosmic-string lens but a pair of interacting giant elliptical galaxies with similar spectra, redshift, and photometry.

quotes.quote: On January 12 of 2006, the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observed the peculiar double extragalactic object CSL-1, suspected to be the result of gravitational lensing by a cosmic string. The high resolution image shows that the object is actually a pair of interacting giant elliptical galaxies. In spite of the weird similarities of the energy and light distributions and of the radial velocities of the two components, CSL-1 is not the lensing of an elliptical galaxy by a cosmic string.

The Capodimonte-Sternberg-Lens candidate n. 1 (CSL-1; _ 2000 =12^h 23^m 30 5 , _ 2000 =-12 38 57 0 ), a peculiar extragalactic object observed in the OACDF by csl1 , hereafter Paper I, appears as a double source projected onto a low density field. The two components of the source are 1.9 arcsec apart, well resolved (i.e. extended) and, when observed from the ground, show roundish and identical shapes. Low resolution spectra look identical with a high confidential level (.l.) and both give the same redshift of 0.46 0.008 . Photometry matches the properties of giant elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: HST imaging shows CSL-1 is not a cosmic-string lens but likely a close pair of elliptical galaxies, possibly starting to merge, with mismatched orientations and asymmetries.

quotes.quote: The galaxy image pair CSL-1 has been a leading candidate for a cosmic string lens. High quality imaging data from the Hubble Space Telescope presented here show that it is not a lens but a pair of galaxies. The galaxies show different orientations of their principal axes, not consistent with any lens model.

We argue that CSL-1 is definitely not a string lens, but likely a pair of galaxies, possibly in the early stages of merging. This conclusion agrees with independent results derived from Director's Discretionary time on HST awarded in May 2005 and taken in January 2006 to address the same problem as our GO proposal . CSL-1 was discovered in the Capodimonte Deep Field survey, where it appears as a pair of giant elliptical galaxy images separated by 1.9 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper revisits CSL-1 as a peculiar extragalactic object first considered a cosmic-string lens candidate, but HST imaging showed it is a pair of interacting galaxies.

quotes.quote: In , and some of us discussed the unusual properties of a peculiar extragalactic object (hereafter CSL-1) which, by a careful analysis of its photometric and spectroscopic investigation seemed to be a good candidate. In fact, CSL-1 looks as a double source projected against a low density field.

These observational evidences led to two possible explanations: either CSL-1 was a rare close pair of two very similar and isolated giant elliptical galaxies, or it was a gravitational lens phenomenon. In the latter case, detailed modeling showed that the properties of CSL-1 could be explained only by the lensing of an E-type galaxy by a cosmic string. In fact, the most relevant feature of the two CSL-1 images is that their isophotes appeared to be undistorted down to the faintest light levels, while the usual gravitational lenses (i.e. lenses created by a bound clump of matter) produce inhomogeneous gravitational fields which always distort the multiple images of extended background sources .

Such image, collected by the authors on January 11 2006 using the ACS/WFC on HST, showed beyond any doubt that CSL-1 is a pair of two interacting galaxies (see the detailed discussion presented below and in saz06 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats CSL-1 as a z=0.46 pair of galaxies/lensing event attributed to a foreground cosmic string and uses it with nearby candidates to constrain cosmic-string lens parameters.

quotes.quote: They claimed that a pair of galaxies CSL-1 is a galaxy at z=0.46 gravitationally lensed by a foreground cosmic string. The observed image separation 1.9'' implies that somewhat large tension is needed, but this does not pose any serious problem if e.g., one considers cosmic strings with high winding numbers . The string lens hypothesis is still to be explored, but if it is the case the system serves as an ideal laboratory to study very early universe.

They identified about 9 lens candidates in the 16' 16' field with CSL-1 at the center. While it is unclear how likely these are galaxies pairs lensed by a cosmic string because of the lack of any detailed information on these lens candidates, we adopt 5 lens candidates with flux ratio larger than 0.5 , as well as CSL-1 itself, as ``true'' gravitational lens events to see how the model parameters are constrained by this kind of additional lens events.

gmw_00000821.name: NGC 524
gmw_00000821.overview: NGC 524 is a massive, nearly face-on lenticular galaxy that dominates a small group [arXiv:1212.2288, arXiv:astro-ph/0102374]. It hosts a relaxed, central molecular gas and dust disk with a low star formation rate [arXiv:1212.2288, arXiv:1609.03559], as well as a chemically decoupled nucleus [arXiv:1105.1755] and an active supermassive black hole [arXiv:1903.03124].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 524 as a group galaxy with X-ray emission, a chemically decoupled metal-rich nucleus, dusty disk and ionized gas, and an extended globular cluster system.

quotes.quote: NGC 524 This galaxy is part of a group that includes at least eight smaller galaxies known as CfA 13 ( GH83 ). This group was detected in the X-ray study by MDMB2003 . The parameter fitted to this galaxy is 0.41 0.02, which translates into a power-law slope of --1.46 0.12. The galaxy nucleus appears to be chemically decoupled from its bulge, being about 0.5--0.6 dex more metal-rich according to SAV92 and S2000 , who also find evidence for a dusty disk and ionized gas within 3 kpc of the galaxy centre. HH85 found a rich and extended GCS with some 2830 880 clusters.

LBHFG2001 used a KMM analysis on HST/WFPC2 data to find colour peaks at (V-I) _0 = 0.98 and 1.19 mag. More recently, BFBK2004 found a broad (V-I) colour distribution and the possible existence of peaks at (V-I) _0 = 0.9 and 1.1. However, none of these results are conclusive.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 524 as a lenticular-galaxy test case, modeling its molecular gas, stellar mass distribution, dust disk, and low star-formation rate to validate morphological quenching.

quotes.quote: We run a simulation designed to mimic the stellar and gaseous properties of NGC524, a local lenticular galaxy, and find a gas disc structure and global star formation rate in good agreement with the observations. Morphological quenching thus seems to be a robust mechanism, and is also consistent with other observations of a reduced star formation efficiency in early-type galaxies in the COLD GASS survey.

NGC 524 is a red lenticular galaxy (a fast rotator) seen close to face-on, it has a central disc containing 6.7 10^7 of molecular gas within a radius of 1.1 kpc Crocker2011 , and an estimated SFR of 0.035 -- 0.051 yr ^ 1 . NGC 524 was chosen for this comparison because of the regular, symmetric structure of its gaseous and stellar components (with also regular kinematics), suggesting the absence of recent accretion events. It also hosts a well-resolved dust disk, the structure of which we can compare to simulations.

We find a total stellar mass of 4.3 10^ 11 . The gas and dark matter components are finally added using an MGE representation of an exponential disc and a Burkert profile, respectively. The dark matter halo has a core radius of 15 kpc (corresponding to 3 R_ e ), and its mass is 1.6 10^ 13 so that dark matter represents 1/3 of the total mass within 3 R_ e .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 524 as a circumnuclear star-forming fast rotator, physically distinct from widespread star-forming systems, with more tenuous ongoing star formation and CO detection suggesting a late star-formation epoch.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear Star-Forming Fast Rotators The \ galaxies (NGC 524, NGC 4459, NGC 4526, NGC 5838, NGC 5845) appear to represent a completely different physical state than the \ systems described above. In Figure sfrmstar , these galaxies are seen to be those star-forming systems with higher masses and therefore lower molecular gas fractions and specific star formation rates. Consequently, the current star formation events do not dominate the SSP ages, all of which are measured to be intermediate to old in the regions of star formation. Unlike in the \ galaxies, these regions of star formation are spatially very distinct, with all of the star formation, younger SSP ages, ionized gas, molecular gas, dust, and stellar kinematic structures being spatially coincident in well-defined central disks (see the definition of this class in SFdyn ).

In contrast, the presence of on-going star formation in other \ galaxies (NGC 524, NGC 5838, NGC 5845) is more tenuous. These galaxies have more tentative PAH detections ( maybeSF ) and only marginally detected younger SSP ages and metallicity gradients. Nevertheless, the PAH emission remains spatially coincident with thin, regular dust disks \ and with embedded stellar kinematic structures (Figure starsgas ). These systems may have reached the end of their star formation epochs, since only one (NGC 524; Crocker et al. in prep ) is detected in CO and the other tracers of star formation (PAH, / ) can be affected by a B star population that has not yet died.

sources.summary: NGC 524 has a small outer H I cloud, a weak ionized-gas disk, CO-detected molecular gas, and mostly molecular cold ISM in its central regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 524 We detect a small \ cloud in the outer regions of this galaxy. Possibly this corresponds to a small gas-rich companion, although no direct optical counterpart is visible on fairly deep optical images Jeong09 . A small galaxy, of which the redshift is not known, is seen about 1 arcmin from the \ cloud. Possibly a small companion is stripped from its \ by NGC 524, in a similar way as is occurring in, e.g., NGC 4472 McNamara94 .

NGC 524 has a weak disc of ionised gas which rotates with the same sense as the stars Sarzi06 . This disc is also detected in CO with an implied molecular gas mass of 1.6 10^8 M_ (Crocker et al. \ unpublished). No counterpart to this gas disc is detected in , implying that most of the cold ISM in the central regions of NGC 524 is in the form of molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies ALMA CO emission in NGC 524, modeling its relaxed central molecular disk, SMBH mass, central hole, and possible warp or radial gas flow.

quotes.quote: We present high angular resolution ( 0 3 or 37 pc ) Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array (ALMA) observations of the CO(2-1) line emission from a central disc in the early-type galaxy NGC 524 . This disc is shown to be dynamically relaxed, exhibiting ordered rotation about a compact 1.3 mm continuum source, which we identify as emission from an active supermassive black hole (SMBH). There is a hole at the centre of the disc slightly larger than the SMBH sphere of influence. An azimuthal distortion of the observed velocity field is found to be due to either a position angle warp or radial gas flow over the inner 2 5 .

NGC 524 is a nearly face-on early-type galaxy with a core stellar light profile Faber+1997AJ114.1771 . It has an I band effective radius ( R_ e ) of 51 and a stellar velocity dispersion within 1R_ e of _ e =220 Cappellari+2006MNRAS366.1126,Cappellari+2013MNRAS432.1709 . It is classified as a fast rotator, with a specific angular momentum within 1R_ e of _ R_e =0.28 Emsellem+2007MNRAS379.401 .

sources.summary: The paper reports CO detections in UGC 0968, including both CO(1-0) and CO(2-1), a double-horn CO(1-0) profile, and an uncertain compact-distribution assumption.

quotes.quote: We report the new detection of CO(1-0) emission in four galaxies (UGC 0968, UGC 5617, UGC 6946, and UGC 8355) and CO(2-1) emission in two of them (UGC 0968 and UGC 5617).

Among the 4 sources showing CO(1-0) detection, UGC 0968 and UGC 6946 exhibit double-horn profiles, while UGC 5617 and UGC 8355 have single-peak profiles. Galaxies detected in the CO(2-1) line (UGC 0968 and UGC 5617) have profiles similar to those found in the CO(1-0) line.

It is possible to evaluate this ratio only for two objects, namely UGC 0968 and UGC 5617 (Tab. tab1 ). For UGC 0968 the assumption of a compact CO distribution is less obvious, since one peak of the CO(2-1) horn profile is less pronounced than in CO(1-0).

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 524's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 524: The M/L is scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 . We classify this galaxy with T=2 (a classical bulge). Although the galaxy is circular, significant stellar rotation is nonetheless seen Simien2000,Emsellem2004 ; therefore the S0 classification is dubious and the galaxy could be a core elliptical McConnellMa2013 . We deproject the multi-gaussian expansion of the photometry profile as given in Cappellari2006 and get R_e=47.1'' . We ignore the possible small disk detected by Sani2011 ( B/T 0.83 ); KormendyHo2013 give B/T=0.92 .

sources.summary: NGC0524 is a high-mass fast-rotating early-type galaxy with a relaxed molecular disc; CO dynamics imply a uniformly light, Kroupa-like IMF.

quotes.quote: NGC0524 is a high-mass isolated fast-rotating ETG, orientated nearly face-on to our line of sight. It has a disc of molecular gas and dust that is clearly visible in optical imaging, but very little star formation. The molecular disc is regular and relaxed at our resolution, and its surface brightness profile is well fitted with an exponential disc model. However, our CO observations require a much lower M/L, which is also modestly centrally peaked. Thus despite being a massive ETG, the IMF in this object seems to be uniformly light, approximately Kroupa like.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 0524 as a core galaxy with embedded discs, a nuclear dust disc, molecular gas, and a well-defined core affected by fitting choices.

quotes.quote: NGC 0524 and NGC 4473 have very clear evidence for embedded discs, both morphologically and kinematically. NGC 0524, has a nuclear dust disc which makes the analysis of the nuclear light profile difficult, but is the only core galaxy with molecular gas 2011MNRAS.414..940Y , while dynamical models for NGC 4473 require two counter-rotating discs 2007MNRAS.379..418C . The cores in these two galaxies seem to be well defined, but the cores might also depend on the actual choice of the fitting range.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 524 as a massive S0 group-dominant galaxy with a relatively rich globular-cluster system, detecting 617 globular clusters in the HST data.

quotes.quote: NGC 524 This massive S0 galaxy dominates a small group. It has a relatively rich GC system with a specific frequency S _N hv81 3.3 ash98 . Here we detect a total of 617 GCs, second only to the cD galaxy NGC 4486 (M87).

gmw_00000822.name: NGC 1023
gmw_00000822.overview: NGC 1023 is a barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1310.2607] and the brightest member of a small galaxy group [arXiv:2104.06396]. It features a stellar disk [arXiv:1204.3472], an unsettled, irregular neutral hydrogen structure [arXiv:1007.2059], and is interacting with the nearby dwarf satellite galaxy NGC 1023A [arXiv:2104.06396].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1023 as an edge-on lenticular, brightest group member whose UV photometry and SED are matched by a 1:5 minor-merger simulation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 is an almost edge-on lenticular galaxy, classified as SB0 by most catalogues (e.g. RC3; De Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), at an estimated distance of 11.4 Mpc (Tonry et al. 2001). It belongs to a sparse group, rich in spirals, and is the brightest member of the LGG 70 group (Garcia 1993). In the optical images (The Carnegie Atlas 1994) the galaxy appears to be composed of a faint stellar disk, a inner and brighter lens and a bar at 45 ^ from the apparent disk major axis. A central nuclear disk is also present, with relatively young stars (cf. Debattista et al. 2002).

The whole SED of NGC 1023 and its global properties are well matched by a simulation which provides a minor merger. The total mass of the primary system is 1 10 ^ 13 M _ with gas fraction 0.01, that-one of the companion is 5 times less with the same gas fraction.
The fit corresponds to a galaxy age of 11.9 Gyr. The strong interaction phase between the systems started 7.7 Gyr ago and the final merger 1.8 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1023 among the weakest-field outliers in its galaxy fits, with high predicted velocity dispersion and low GC kurtosis suggesting possible plane-of-sky rotation.

quotes.quote: We can see that the models can describe all the galaxies successfully (the ^2 confidence is always higher than 5 ). The worst cases are NGC 1023 and NGC 4494 with a confidence of approximately 38 . These galaxies require a weaker gravitational field than expected by the priors, as follows from panels (b)-(e) of g1 and g14 .
The objects for which we obtained the reconstructed dynamical acceleration, a , that is too low , NGC 1023 and particularly NGC 4494, might be quickly rotating in the plane of the sky.

The predictions on velocity dispersion are usually too high ( pred ). This is also the case of the galaxies NGC 1023 and NGC 4494 for which we obtained the worst models ( confidence of around 38 for both). Statistical error can easily account for this confidence value. Nevertheless, the shape of the best-fit models for these galaxies seems to be too high for the outermost GCs (Figures fig:g1 and fig:g12 ). The reason for this discrepancy might be the rotation of the GC system in the plane of the sky so that the attractive force is overestimated. This suggestion is supported by the fact that NGC 1023 and NGC 4494 have very low values of the s_4 parameter among the real galaxies in our sample ( tabgcss ) and as we showed in gcss , this suggests a systemic rotation of the GC system.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1023 as a J-PLUS case study, detecting 523 globular-cluster candidates and linking their metallicity structure to past accretion events.

quotes.quote: We also study the stellar population content of GC candidates in NGC 1023. To detect GCs, our methodology is based on Source Extractor and does not require a previous filtering or modelling of the host galaxy. As a case study, we use data from the S0 galaxy NGC 1023.

In NGC 1023, GCFinder identifies 523 GC candidates. We observe evidence of color bimodality in a few broad-band colors but not on narrow-band colors. The SED analysis reveals a clear metallicity bimodality and we observe that narrow-band filters are very useful to constrain metallicities. We also identified a broad age-metallicity relation as well as a wide metallicity distribution that are evidence that NGC 1023 experienced accretion events in the past.

sources.summary: The paper presents FAST H I observations of NGC 1023, arguing its warped gas disk and dwarf interactions support S0 formation from a spiral via tidal interactions.

quotes.quote: Based on the atomic-hydrogen ( ) observations using the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope (FAST), we present a detailed study of the gas-rich massive S0 galaxy NGC 1023 in a nearby galaxy group. The presence of an extended warped disk in NGC 1023 indicates that this S0 galaxy originated from a spiral galaxy. The data also suggest that NGC 1023 is interacting with four dwarf galaxies.

The massive S0 galaxy NGC 1023 is the brightest member of this group at a distance of 10.4 Mpc Cappellari+11,Tonry+01,Dolfi+21 . The gas content of early-type galaxies is often thought to be insignificant. NGC 1023 is one of the nearest members of the rare class of rich S0 galaxies, making it an ideal laboratory to test a massive S0 galaxy formation within a galaxy group. NGC 1023 has a complex set of substructures, including a young (3.4 Gyr) and metal-rich ( [ Fe/H ] = 0.50 dex) nuclear disc and a faster bar Corsini+16,Debattista+02 . Deep observations from the Westerbork Sybthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) showed that NGC 1023 has an extended and complex gas distribution Sancisi+84,Morganti+06 .

sources.summary: The paper uses planetary nebulae to trace NGC 1023 halo kinematics, finding it is an S0 fast rotator whose outer angular momentum rises then declines toward the slow-rotator regime.

quotes.quote: Small rotation is observed in the E1 galaxy NGC 3379, while higher rotation velocities are observed in the S0 galaxy NGC 1023. The kinematics of the PNe system is in good agreement with the stellar absorption line kinematics in the radial region in which both data sets overlap, as shown in N+08 and D+07.

Both NGC 1023 and NGC 3379 are classified as fast rotators, they have a monotonically rising _R within 0.5R_e reaching 0.4 and 0.15 respectively. Their halos, however, behave differently. In NGC 3379, _R remains almost constant, ranging between 0.2 and 0.15; in NGC 1023, _R keeps rising up to 0.5 at R=2-3R_e and then declines monotonically, almost approaching the slow rotator regime. This is a consequence of the combined effects of the declining rotation curve and the rising velocity dispersion profile observed in NGC 1023 (see N+08).

sources.summary: The paper finds poor halo fits for NGC 1023, favoring a high-F_d compact halo but attributing difficulties to its centrally peaked dispersion, rotation, and barred SB0 structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 Our analysis favours a dark halo with F_ d 9.9 (near maximum) and small radius R_ h <10 R_ e . The poor halo fits for this galaxy are perhaps unsurprising given the poor fits to the ``stars only'' basic model. The stellar _ los profile is more centrally peaked than the (photometric) S ersic model. It was observed noordermeer2008,coccato2009 that the inner regions <100 ) are affected by strong rotation. NGC 1023 is a SB0 galaxy with a fast rotating bar which would imply a maximal (i.e. baryon dominated) disc vpd02 .
We omit NGC 1023 in the analysis.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1023 as a dominant galaxy of a small group in a six-galaxy lenticular sample, using planetary nebulae to separate spheroid and disc kinematics out to large radii.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we therefore apply the kinematic bulge--disc decomposition technique developed in Ari to the sample of six S0s with suitable PNe data presented in Ari2 . This sample was selected to span a range of environments in order to see if the evolutionary properties depend systematically on surroundings: NGC 7457 and NGC 3115 are isolated; NGC 1023 and NGC 2768 are the dominant galaxies of two small groups (5-6 members); while NGC 3384 and NGC 3489 are satellite galaxies of the Leo Group (30 members). The Hubble types of these galaxies and the adopted distance moduli are listed in Table tab:galfitinput .

In this paper, we have analysed the kinematics of a sample of six lenticular galaxies using the planetary nebulae data presented in Ari2 to determine their dynamical properties out to large radii. This analysis has emphasised the importance of treating the spheroidal and disc components of these systems as distinct entities both photometrically and kinematically, and doing a careful job of separating them. Through this process, the rather heterogeneous properties of S0s over-all start to be resolved into more consistent sub-components, and a reasonably coherent picture begins to emerge.

sources.summary: The paper uses planetary nebulae and H I kinematics to show faint fuzzy clusters in NGC 1023 follow the stellar disk rather than a spheroid or merger stream.

quotes.quote: To identify their origins, we compare planetary nebulae and neutral hydrogen with faint fuzzy positions and line-of-sight velocities in NGC 1023. In this way, we rule out scenarios in which these objects are associated with an on-going merger or with a spheroid population in NGC 1023. Their kinematics are indistinguishable from the stellar disk population in this galaxy, and we conclude that faint fuzzies are most likely just remnant open clusters.

The lower panel of Fig. fig:vlos compares the FF kinematics to those of the PNe that cortesi11 found did fit with a simple disk-plus-spheroid model of NGC 1023, with different symbols identifying with which component the PNe are most likely associated. Again, the picture is fairly clear: the FF kinematics follow those of the disk PNe rather closely.

sources.summary: The paper investigates NGC 1023's nuclear stellar disk, finding it younger and more metal-rich than the bulge and linking it to star formation from enriched gas.

quotes.quote: Using archival Hubble Space Telescope imaging and ground-based integral-field spectroscopy, we investigated the structure and stellar populations of the nuclear stellar disc hosted in the interacting SB0 galaxy NGC 1023. The stars of the nuclear disc are remarkably younger and more metal rich with respect to the host bulge. These findings support a scenario in which the nuclear disc is the end result of star formation in metal enriched gas piled up in the galaxy centre. The gas can be of either internal or external origin, i.e. from either the main disc of NGC 1023 or the nearby satellite galaxy NGC 1023A.

The global properties and spectral energy distribution of NGC 1023 are suggestive of a minor merger occurred 2 Gyr ago Bettoni2012 . The broad-band images of the centre of NGC 1023 obtained by Hubble Space Telescope (HST) show an almost edge-on NSD Faber1997, Silchenko1999, Sarzi2006, Ledo2010 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1023 as a lenticular galaxy whose globular clusters trace disk and spheroid components, with red clusters linked to the disk and blue clusters mainly to the spheroid.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a new catalogue of GC velocities and colours for the lenticular galaxy NGC 1023, we study their kinematics and spatial distribution, in comparison with the underlying stellar kinematics and surface brightness profile, and we test a new method for studying GC properties. Specifically, we decompose the galaxy light into its spheroid (assumed to represent the bulge + halo components) and disk components and use it to assign to each GC a probability of belonging to one of the two components. Then we model the galaxy kinematics, assuming a disk and spheroidal component, using planetary nebulae (PNe) and integrated stellar light.

We find that the reddest GCs are likely to be associated with the disk, as found for faint fuzzies in this same galaxy, suggesting that the disk of this S0 galaxy originated at z 2 . The majority of blue GCs are found likely to be associated with the spheroidal (hot) component. The method also allows us to identify objects that are unlikely to be in equilibrium with the system.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023 as an aligned S0 system with a barred bulge, companion interaction evidence, declining outer tracer rotation, and a proposed early gas-rich minor-merger history.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 is the brightest member of a small galaxy group in Perseus (namely the NGC 1023 group), which is composed of 13 bound galaxies Tully1980 . Specifically, NGC 1023 is classified as an SB0 galaxy from early photometric studies that confirmed the presence of a barred bulge (e.g. Barbon1975 ), which is found to rotate rapidly Kormendy1982 . NGC 1023 has also a companion dwarf satellite galaxy, NGC 1023A, located South-East of its nucleus (see figure 2 of Barbon1975 ). The possible interaction between these two systems has been discussed in the literature as a potential explanation for the origin and peculiar distribution of the neutral hydrogen HI mass cloud observed around NGC 1023 Sancisi1984,Capaccioli1986 .

NGC 1023 is characterized by an increasing stellar rotation velocity profile from the innermost regions within 0.5 R_ e (ATLAS ^ 3D ) out to 1 R_ e , beyond which the rotation remains roughly constant out to 2 R_ e . There is, possibly, evidence that the stellar rotation velocity starts decreasing beyond 2 R_ e (see Fig.3).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1023 has faint extended clusters rotating with the galaxy and associated with its disk; it also describes NGC 1023 as a dominant SB0 group member.

quotes.quote: Moreover, in Fig. fig:brodie.fig3 , the radial velocity plot for the faint extended clusters in NGC 1023, we see clear evidence for rotation of the cluster system and this kinematic signature corresponds quite closely to the rotation of the galaxy itself, as measured along the major axis by sp97 . The cluster radial velocities were measured from the blue spectra and have been corrected by 133 km/s as determined in Section sec:data . The bulge effective radius for NGC 1023 is < 2 kpc mh01 so the clusters are 2 bulge effective radii from the galaxy center (1 =2.9 kpc at the distance of NGC 1023). This strongly suggests that these objects are associated with the galaxy's disk rather than its bulge.
NGC 1023 is the dominant member of a well-defined group of 15 galaxies and NGC 3384 is a member of the Leo I group gar93 .
Both NGC 1023 and NGC 3384 are classified as SB0s while NGC 3115 is a highly bulge-dominated galaxy, transitional in type between E7 and S01.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1023 as an S0 galaxy with star-formation indications and faint extended red clusters that bias its globular-cluster turnover magnitude if included.

quotes.quote: Turning to the S0's, NGC 1023 and NGC 3384 are perhaps candidates for hosting intermediate-age populations. Both galaxies show indications of star formation activity in their inner regions (Kuntschner et al. , Sil'chenko ). However, the faint TOM of NGC 1023 does not seem to be related to intermediate-age clusters. Larsen Brodie identified beside the ''normal'' compact GCs (both red and blue) a population of faint extended red GCs. The inclusion of these latter objects in the luminosity function is mainly responsible for the deviating position of NGC 1023.
Leaving them aside results in a distance modulus well agreeing with the SBF distance.

sources.summary: NGC 1023 is placed among gas-rich accretion systems; its large massive H I structure rotates overall but remains irregular and unsettled.

quotes.quote: The second category (denoted A, for accretion) contains galaxies where the \ is found in unsettled structures that are clearly connected to a recent or an ongoing gas-rich accretion. The galaxies that fall in this category are NGC 1023, NGC 2768 and NGC 5198, while to some extent NGC 3489 could also fall in this category. The \ in NGC 1023 shows overall rotation, but the kinematics shows many irregularities and the \ is clearly not settled.

Another example is NGC 1023 where a large and fairly massive \ structure is observed that shows an overall rotation pattern, but that clearly has not settled into a disc. This system may evolve into a galaxy with a large, regular gas disc, similar to the one seen in NGC 4278.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 1023's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023: The M/L of Bower2001 is already corrected for G.E.; we scale it to the distance of KormendyHo2013 and calibrate it from the V band to 3.6mu using the V band aperture photometry from Hyperleda. The Spitzer images come from Program 69, PI Giovanni Fazio, Observer ID 2. We perform a Sersic bulge plus exponential disk decomposition along the major axis of the galaxy following Fisher2008 and masking the region where the bar is present (see Fig. fig_maxfits and Table tab_maxfits ). This gives R_e=7.5'' , which agrees with Fisher2010 . The 2-dimensional fit (including a bar) of Sani2011 gives R_e=18.8'' , but the peculiar fit residuals suggest that the bar has not been modeled correctly.

sources.summary: The paper reports strong SBF signals for several NGC 1023 satellite candidates, using redshifts and visual SBF similarity to confirm group membership.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 Table tab:ngc1023_sbf gives the SBF results for candidate satellites in the field of NGC 1023. Several of the dwarfs had very strong SBF signals that put them at the distance of NGC 1023 ( D=10.4 Mpc). For four of the candidates, we did not attempt an SBF measurement, either because the candidate was too irregular or because there was too much scattered light from a nearby star. For three of these IC 239, UGC 2157, and dw0240+3903, the candidates have redshifts from trentham2009 that are within 300 km/s of NGC 1023. trentham2009 consider these three to be high confidence members for the NGC 1023 group, and we consider these to be confirmed members as well.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023 as the nearest S0 galaxy in the sample, with HST-studied globular clusters and a third population of red, extended clusters of unknown origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 At 9.8 Mpc, NGC 1023 is the nearest S0 galaxy. It is the brightest galaxy in a group of 13 galaxies tul80 . Its GC system has been studied recently using HST by lar00 . As well as the expected blue and red GC subpopulations, they identified a third subpopulation of red, spatially extended clusters. The origin of these extended clusters is currently unknown.

sources.summary: This paper describes NGC 1023 as an SB0 galaxy with central kinematic twists, h3/h4 asymmetries, prior long-slit data, and western-side features needing confirmation.

quotes.quote: This is an example of an SB0 galaxy showing h_3 anti-correlated with V inside 10 , and correlated outside Bow01 . There is a strong twist of the zero velocity curve near the center. Stellar kinematics has been previously published by SP97b , Nei99 , Sil99 , including multiple long-slit data parallel to the major-axis Deb02 . The h_3 and h_4 maps show significantly negative values on the western side of the galaxy which are not observed by Deb02 . The cause for this discrepancy is not known, so these features should be confirmed.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1023 as a barred disk galaxy whose deprojected bar orientation may bias black-hole scaling-relation residuals.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 1023, a quick visual inspection reveals a disk/bar position angle of 80 ^ /72 ^ , suggesting that the inclination of this galaxy does not result in us looking down the length of the bar. However, as Debattista (2002) revealed, after deprojecting this galaxy, it was found to have a strong bar whose position angle is 102 ^ offset from the galaxy's (projected) major-axis. That is, we are in fact looking down the barrel of the bar in this galaxy. A full treatment of each of the barred galaxies is beyond the scope of this paper, it is however noted that the (projected) bar position angles can appear more aligned with the (projected) major-axis than they are in reality, as is the case with NGC 1023.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC1023, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC1023 (Fig. fig:NGC1023 ) is an S0 that clearly contains a bar, which in this case is not aligned with the disk. Attempts to fit the isophotal twists with coordinate rotations in disk or bulge proved ineffective. A faint nuclear point source can be fitted, but we omit it since it has little effect on the bulge parameters.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1023's cluster system, confirming red faint fuzzies in its disk and linking blue clusters and surrounding HI to interaction with NGC 1023A.

quotes.quote: We confirm the association of the red FFs with the disk of NGC 1023, consistent with them being long-lived open clusters. Half of the blue FFs appear to be associated with the dwarf satellite galaxy NGC 1023A (which was largely absent from the original HST/WFPC2 coverage), while the remainder are spatially coincident with the densest HI gas that surrounds NGC 1023. The blue FFs have colours that are consistent with young (few 100 Myr old) star clusters that formed during the most recent interaction between NGC 1023 and its satellite NGC 1023A.
This supports the impression by eye in Figure 2, and confirms the findings of Larsen Brodie (2000), that red FFs are indeed associated with the disk of NGC 1023.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1023 as an early-type galaxy at 11.1 Mpc whose hundreds of candidate star clusters are used for a VLA search for IMBH accretion signatures.

quotes.quote: Forbes et al. \ recently used the Hubble Space Telescope to localize hundreds of candidate star clusters in NGC 1023, an early-type galaxy at a distance of 11.1 Mpc. Old stars dominate the light of 92 of the clusters and intermediate-age stars dominate the light of the remaining 8 . Theory predicts that clusters with such ages can host intermediate-mass black holes (IMBHs) with masses M_ BH 10^5 M_ . To investigate this prediction, we used 264 s of 5.5 GHz data from the Karl G. \ Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) to search for the radiative signatures of IMBH accretion from 337 candidate clusters in an image spanning 492 (26 kpc) with a resolution of 0 40 (22 pc).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1023 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is a barred lenticular galaxy (SB0), brightest member of a group of 13 galaxies 2001ApJ...546..681T . It has a small companion NGC 1023A, which is probably interacting with the main galaxy 1984MNRAS.210..497S , but it is likely too small to cause significant disruptions in the dynamical state of the main galaxy 2008MNRAS.384..943N . The ATLAS^ 3D velocity map 2004MNRAS.352..721E covers the innermost regions of the galaxy, up to half effective radii, and displays rotation with maximum amplitude of 120 and strong twist of the zero-velocity line. The stellar kinematics along the major axis of 2002MNRAS.332...65D and 2017MNRAS.471.4005B show that the rotation continues to increase mildly in the outer regions exceeding 200 beyond 2 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1023 with HST imaging, identifying 221 cluster candidates and at least three cluster populations, including bright bimodal-color clusters and fainter extended red objects.

quotes.quote: Using HST images we have carried out a study of cluster populations in the nearby S0 galaxy NGC 1023. In two WFPC2 pointings we have identified 221 cluster candidates. The small distance ( 9 Mpc) combined with deep F555W and F814W images allows us to reach about two magnitudes below the expected turn-over of the globular cluster luminosity function. NGC 1023 appears to contain at least three identifiable cluster populations: the brighter clusters show a clearly bimodal color distribution with peaks at = 0.92 and at = 1.15 and in addition there are a number of fainter, more extended objects with predominantly red colors.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1023 as a nearby S0 galaxy with a peculiar companion, possible recent encounter, and globular-cluster contamination issues from its low Galactic latitude.

quotes.quote: At a distance of around 10 Mpc cia91 , NGC 1023 is one of the nearest S0--type galaxies. It is included in the Atlas of peculiar galaxies arp66 because of a small companion galaxy, NGC 1023A, which may have suffered a recent close encounter with NGC 1023 itself san84 . The companion galaxy is quite noticably bluer than NGC 1023. Its integrated broad-band colors, U-B = 0.0 and B-V=0.7 cap86 would imply a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = 1.7 or less for an entirely old stellar population bc2001 , or the galaxy might have experienced recent star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1023 as a classical-bulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 1023 .LBT-../SB0 Classical Bulge. The bulge looks very much like and E type, there is no sign of spiral structure of any disk-like morphology. The decomposition yields n_b=2.5 , and the bulges is inactive with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.1 mag.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 1023 and concludes a more complete model gives a normal rotationally supported disk, explaining earlier strange kinematics as a likely gas-stripped spiral.

quotes.quote: In Section sec: NGC1023 application we make a first real application of the method to the NGC 1023 catalogue, in which we show that this likelihood fitting can reproduce the strange results in EDO if we also neglect the spheroidal component, but that the more complete model presented here results in a picture in which NGC 1023 has a normal rotationally-supported disk.

The error bars on all parameters are duly reduced, and we now find that the kinematics of NGC 1023 are exactly what one would expect for a very normal disk galaxy, with V / _ 4.1 throughout the disk, similar to what one finds in the stellar component of a spiral galaxy.

Thus, we seem to have found the explanation for the strange kinematics originally inferred from these data for NGC 1023, and it is now revealed to be most likely a spiral galaxy that has simply been stripped of its gas.

sources.summary: NGC 1023 is presented as a barred lenticular galaxy with a strongly declining dispersion profile and rotation that rises, plateaus, and then drops beyond 3 effective radii.

quotes.quote: This is a barred lenticular galaxy (SB0). Our measured velocity dispersion profile declines strongly with radius, with values eventually falling below the resolution limit. Over the same radial range, the rotational amplitude gradually increases with radius before plateauing near 1 \ (to 200 km s ^ 1 ) and eventually dropping beyond 3 \ (see also, e.g., Noordermeer:2008kx,Coccato:2009je,Cortesi:2011df ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1023 for a detailed binding-energy calculation, deriving the minimum energy needed to remove stellar gas and applying it to galactic-wind energetics.

quotes.quote: We computed this minimum energy in detail for NGC1023, which has a stellar velocity dispersion close to the mean in our sample, assuming a Navarro, Frenk White (1996) distribution with a concentration parameter of 10 for the ,bb=10 142 570 700,clip f7.eps dark matter and a King model for the stellar distribution with a core radius of 51.9^ (Jarrett et al. 2003). This calculation gives a minimum energy of 2.01 keV per particle, which is equal to 7.2 _*^2 in NGC1023. Based on our detailed calculation for NGC1023 presented above, the rate of increase in the total gravitational binding energy of the gas due to stellar mass loss can be estimated as W = 7.2 M_* _*^2 .
